gmw_00000389.name: Mrk 231
gmw_00000389.overview: Mrk 231 is a nearby ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1005.2877, arXiv:2003.06359] and Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:0709.1713, arXiv:1010.3846] in a late-stage merger phase [arXiv:1005.2177, arXiv:1504.07393]. The system hosts a luminous active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1005.2877, arXiv:1402.4811] and a multiphase molecular outflow [arXiv:1405.4833, arXiv:1504.07393] alongside intense starburst activity [arXiv:1005.2877, arXiv:1402.4811].

sources.summary: The paper presents Mrk 231 as an X-ray exposed ULIRG with strong far-infrared water lines, arguing grain-surface chemistry in its shocked active nucleus can enhance warm water.

quotes.quote: Objects such as Mrk 231 have very violent environments in the central regions around the super-massive black hole. This particular object has a derived 8 - 1000 m infrared luminosity L_ IR =4.0 10^ 12 L _ Boksenberg1977 . A highly absorbed power-law X-ray spectrum was observed by Braito2004 . It is in these extreme environments, that we expect cloud-cloud collisions to cause C and J shocks, and strip the grains of their ice layers. The internal UV field created by X-rays from the accreting black hole does not allow to reform the ice.

This increased column helps to explain unusually strong water lines observed for Mrk 231, that has an accreting supermassive black hole with X-ray luminosity L_X 10^ 44 erg/s. This ULIRG shows for example a very high H _2 O 2_ 02 1_ 11 / CO J=8-7 ratio. These ratios are not observed in typical starforming environments that are mainly exposed to UV only (PDR) environments, such as the one observed in the Orion Bar Habart2010 or M82 Panuzzo2010 , where this ratio is typically an order of magnitude or more smaller than in Mrk 231, and as such these very bright H _2 O lines might be typical for regions that are exposed to high X-ray fluxes, close to the accreting black hole in active galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper presents high-resolution dense-gas observations of Mrk 231, finding HCN/HCO+/HNC line wings from a dense molecular outflow and HC3N emission from a warm shielded nuclear region.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The phase center was set to =12:56:14.232 and = 56:52:25.207 (J2000).

quotes.quote: Our goal is to study gas properties in large-scale molecular outflows and winds from active galactic nuclei (AGNs) and starburst galaxies. We obtained high-resolution (1. 55 1. 28) observations of HCN, HCO ^+ , HNC 1--0 and HC _3 N 10--9 of the ultraluminous galaxy (ULIRG) Mrk 231 with the IRAM Plateau de Bure Interferometer. We detect luminous emission from HCN, HCO ^+ and HNC 1--0 in the QSO ULIRG Mrk 231. All three lines show broad line wings - which are particularly prominent for HCN.

Our interpretation of the HCN-luminous wings of Mrk 231 is that the molecular gas in the outflow is clumpy and dense, with an enhanced HCN abundance. The similar spectral shapes (and spatial extent within the errors) of the CO and HCN 1--0 line wings suggest that the emission is emerging from the same outflowing gas. This would then imply that there is no significant evolution (e.g. star-formation or evaporation) of the cloud properties along the molecular part of the outflow.
The detection of HC _3 N emission toward the inner region of Mrk 231 raises interesting questions about regions of shielded gas near the nucleus of this ULIRG QSO.

sources.summary: The paper presents Mrk 231 as a nearby quasar with galactic and FeLoBAL outflows, a peculiar FUV spectrum lacking broad absorption, merger-triggered activity, and a powerful kpc-scale wind.

quotes.quote: Mrk 231, the nearest ( z = 0.0422) quasar, hosts both a galactic-scale wind and a nuclear-scale iron low-ionization broad absorption line (FeLoBAL) outflow. We recently obtained a far-ultraviolet (FUV) spectrum of this object covering 1150 -- 1470 \ with the Cosmic Origins Spectrograph on board the Hubble Space Telescope . This spectrum is highly peculiar, highlighted by the presence of faint ( 2 of predictions based on H ), broad ( 10,000 km s ^ 1 at the base), and highly blueshifted (centroid at -3500 km s ^ 1 ) Ly emission. The FUV continuum emission is slightly declining at shorter wavelengths (consistent with F_ ^ 1.7 ) and does not show the presence of any obvious photospheric or wind stellar features. Surprisingly, the FUV spectrum also does not show any unambiguous broad absorption features.

The disturbed morphology of the host suggests that both the quasar and starburst are triggered by a recent merger event (e.g., Hamilton Keel 1987; Hutchings Neff 1987; Surace et al. 1998; Veilleux et al. 2002, 2006). In recent years, Mrk 231 has become the archetype of galactic-scale quasar-driven winds. These outflow events are purported to self-regulate the growth of the black hole (BH) and spheroidal component of the galaxy, and explain the relatively tight BH-spheroid mass relation (e.g., Di Matteo et al. 2005; Murray et al. 2005; Veilleux, Cecil, Bland-Hawthorn 2005 and references therein). Evidence in Mrk 231 for a powerful kpc-scale wind with outflow velocity of up to 1000 km s ^ 1 and mass outflow rate of order 400 -- 1000 M _ yr ^ 1 has now been seen in the neutral gas (Rupke, Veilleux, Sanders 2005; Rupke Veilleux 2011, 2012), the molecular gas (Feruglio et al. 2010; Fischer et al. 2010; Sturm et al. 2011; Aalto et al. 2012; Cicone et al. 2012), and the warm ionized gas (Lipari et al. 2009; Rupke Veilleux 2011, 2012).

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 231 as a nearby ultraluminous infrared Seyfert/BALQSO with AGN plus starburst activity, broad absorption lines, reddening, and feedback from a BAL wind.

quotes.quote: Mrk 231 is a nearby ( z=0.0421 ) ultraluminous infrared galaxy that has a Seyfert 1 optical spectrum sanders88 . The infrared emission is thought to be a combination of AGN and starburst activity e.g., and references therein farrah03 . Recently, attention has been again drawn to this galaxy as a consequence of the discovery of a powerful, wide-angle, kiloparsec-scale molecular outflow rupke11 . Mrk 231's optical spectrum shows extreme Fe 2 emission, undetected [ O 3 ] 4959,5007 , and somewhat broad and prominent Balmer lines.

Spatially-resolved emission in the broad absorption line trough suggests nearly full coverage of the continuum emission region. The broad absorption lines reveal higher velocities in the He 1 * lines (produced in the quasar-photoionized H 2 region) compared with the Na 1 and Ca 2 lines (produced in the corresponding partially-ionized zone). Cloudy simulations show that a density increase is required between the H 2 and partially-ionized zones to produce ionic column densities consistent with the optical and IR absorption line measurements and limits, and that the absorber lies 100 pc from the central engine. These results suggest that the He 1 * lines are produced in an ordinary quasar BAL wind that impacts upon, compresses, and accelerates the nuclear starburst's dusty effluent (feedback in action), and the Ca 2 and Na 1 lines are produced in this dusty accelerated gas.

sources.summary: The paper argues Mrk 231 is a heavily reddened nearby quasar/ULIRG rather than a milli-parsec binary system, with its infrared lines, SED, and far-UV properties favoring reddened AGN interpretations.

quotes.quote: Mrk 231 is a nearby quasar with an unusually red near-UV-to-optical continuum, generally explained as heavy reddening by dust e.g., leighly14 . yan15 proposed that Mrk 231 is a milli-parsec black-hole binary with little intrinsic reddening. We show that if the observed FUV continuum is intrinsic, as assumed by yan15 , it fails by a factor of about 100 in powering the observed strength of the near-infrared emission lines, and the thermal near and mid-infrared continuum. In contrast, the line and continuum strengths are typical for a reddened AGN spectral energy distribution.

Mrk 231 is a well-known, nearby ( z=0.0421 ) ultra-luminous infrared galaxy that has a Seyfert 1 optical spectrum sanders88 . The infrared emission is thought to be a combination of AGN and starburst activity e.g., and references therein farrah03 . Recently, attention has been again drawn to this galaxy after the discovery of a powerful, wide-angle, kiloparsec-scale molecular outflow rupke11 . Mrk 231 has an unusual spectral energy distribution (SED).

sources.summary: The paper models Mrk 231 as a high-SFR starburst AGN with a weak, nearly line-of-sight jet, a compact stellar disk, and modest stellar-wind mass loading.

quotes.quote: Mrk 231: The jet in Mrk 231 has a luminosity of L_ j = 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 reynolds2009 . There is evidence supporting a jet viewed nearly along the line of sight, with an inclination i <14^ and a high Lorentz factor e.g., reynolds2009, DavTac2004 ; we adopt i=10^ , and a Lorentz factor of =6 . The size of the collimated radio source is estimated at 70 pc taylor1999 , and its opening angle =0.1 . The stellar disk of Mrk 231 has a total thickness of 23 pc and a radius of 300 pc, and a nuclear SFR of 100 - 350 M_ yr ^ 1 DowSol1998,TenBra2014 . We take here the limit value of 350 M_ yr ^ 1 .

The total mass rate loaded inside the jet of Mrk 231 by stars and the total surface of interaction, as defined in Eq. ( eq:SigmaT ), are plotted in Fig. fig:McM231 as a function of jet height. At the total jet height, we have ratios M ^2 / L_j 0.17 and _T 0.09 . Unlike the case of M87, the jet of Mrk 231 seems to be only slightly mass-loaded and slowed down by the winds of massive stars, but in this case the loaded matter will spread inside the jet more efficiently due to a higher _T value.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Swift UV and X-ray monitoring of Mrk 231, finding significant UV variability, a power-law UV PSD, normal AGN-like variability behavior, and UV emission largely from the accretion disk.

quotes.quote: The ultra-luminous infrared galaxy Mrk 231 hosts the nearest quasar at a redshift of z=0.042 , where the AGN contribution of the bolometric luminosity is estimated to be 10^ 46 e.g., 2009ApJS..182..628V . Mrk 231 is also a broad absorption line quasar with low-ionization lines including Fe absorption troughs 1972ApJ...173L.109A , and thus is classified as an FeLoBAL. FeLoBALs contribute to 2 of Type I quasar population intrinsically 2012ApJ...757..180D . Mrk 231 is intrinsically X-ray weak as revealed by Nustar observations, the X-ray absorption column density is constrained to be heavily absorbed but Compton-thin ( = [ 1.2 0.3 ] 10^ 23 ), and the intrinsic X-ray spectrum is flat with a power-law photon index of =1.4 2014ApJ...785...19T . Overall, Mrk 231 exhibits many unusual features among quasars.

In this paper, we have analyzed the UV and X-ray observations of Mrk 231 core by Swift and found that its UV emission varies significantly based on the ^2 test result. The detection of UV variability for Mrk 231 is consistent with our previous analysis in the FUV band ( 1030 ) based on the archival IUE and HST data 2015ApJ...809..117Y , and in contrast with other analyses claiming that the UV flux of Mrk 231 is a constant. UV variability from the accretion disk is a characteristic of many AGN observed by Swift e.g., 2010ApJS..187...64G, 2012ApJS..201...10W, 2013A&A...550A..71V . The PSD of the UV light curve of Mrk 231 is well fit by a power law model, another characteristic of AGN variability, where the slope is measured to be 1.8 0.3 for frequencies below the flattening caused by white noise.

Combining the variability, PSD, and excess variance analysis, we conclude that a significant fraction of the observed UV emission of Mrk 231 in uvm2 band centered at 2246 \ is from the accretion disk.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Mrk 231 as the nearest quasar and luminous ULIRG, confirming a 403-day optical period that supports a subparsec binary black-hole interpretation.

quotes.quote: Recently, proposed an interesting SMBBH candidate (with a very short orbital period) in the nucleus of the nearest quasar Mrk 231 at redshift z = 0.0422 , based on detailed analysis of its unique optical-UV spectrum. Mrk 231 is the nearest quasar and the most luminous Ultra-Luminous InfraRed Galaxy in the nearby Universe 10.1051/0004-6361/201015164 . Lately, the emission feature in its spectra has been reported as the first detection of extragalactic molecular oxygen 10.3847/1538-4357/ab612d . Even the optical spectrum of Mrk 231 resembles mostly the quasar composite spectrum, it varies dramatically at the wavelengths around 3000 \ and becomes flat again at < 2500 .

They interpreted this unique spectrum of Mrk 231 as a consequence of emission from a BBH accretion system, with which the drop of the continuum at < 4000 \ is due to a gap or a hole opened by the secondary component of the BBH 10.1088/0004-637X/761/2/90 . The masses of the primary and the secondary SMBBHs were constrained as 1.5 10^8 M and 4.5 10^ 6 M , respectively. The semimajor axis of the system is estimated 590 AU, and its orbital period is just 1.2 years, where the corresponding gravitational wave emission would be on tens of nanohertz. If such periodicity could be confirmed from timing observations, this object might be a target for the next generation of gravitational wave detections.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 231 as a Seyfert type 1 BALQSO transitioning radio state, with VLA evidence for lobes, ordered fields, and composite jet/wind outflow.

quotes.quote: Mrk 231, at a redshift of 0.04217, is a well-studied Seyfert type 1 galaxy Sanders88b,Surace98 and a Broad Absorption Line Quasi-Stellar Object BALQSO; Boksenberg77,Smith95,Gallagher02 . It is also known to be transitioning from the RQ to RL state Reynolds17 . The top and bottom panels of Figure fig2 show the historical VLA images of Mrk 231 at 1.4 GHz and 4.9 GHz, respectively.

The 10 scale images detect a diffuse lobe-like emission to the south that extends to 55 kpc whereas the 1-4 scale images detect a poorly collimated jet extending 25-30 kpc to the south. The higher resolution images detect a region close to the core with inferred B-fields parallel to the local jet direction. The lower resolution images detect a transverse B-field all the way from the core to the edges of the southern lobe. This is similar to the case of III Zw 2, in that the poloidal inferred B-fields thread the spine of the weakly collimated jet sampled at higher resolution and toroidal inferred B-fields thread the AGN wind sampled at lower resolution. Thus, Mrk 231 also seems to host a composite jet and wind radio outflow.

sources.summary: UGC 8058 is the sample source with a radio-loud, variable AGN; the model fits it with two absorbed radio components, separating AGN-dominated high-frequency emission from lower-frequency star formation.

quotes.quote: UGC 8058 UGC 8058 (Mrk 231) is the only source in our sample with a radio loud AGN. Vega et al. (2008) found that the contribution of the AGN to the bolometric luminosity was 24 . The AGN is also variable, although the variability is seen only above 5 GHz (Ulvestad et al., 1999). Fig.  fig:fits shows that our model succeeds in fitting the radio data for this source perfectly well as two absorbed components. The discontinuity is probably due to radio emission from the AGN that is seen only above 4.8 GHz due to strong absorption. In this case the absorption may be synchrotron self-absorption or free-free absorption.

The spectrum implies that, at the epoch of observation of the data above 1.4 GHz (1995 November 17), the AGN contributed 66 to the radio emission at 8.4 GHz (see table  tab:turn ). The radio emission at lower frequencies is therefore derived almost entirely from star formation, and we can use this fact to estimate the star formation rate.

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 231 as an example ULIRG/Seyfert quasar outflow, linking its accretion disk, black hole mass, and observed wide-angle outflow to a derived disk surface-density profile.

quotes.quote: Mrk 231 is classified as ultra-luminous infrared galaxy 1972ApJ...176L..95R and Seyfert I galaxy 1973ApJ...183...29W . It hosts an accretion disc with a mass of 3.15 10^ 10 M _ , an outer radius of 1.7 kpc 1998ApJ...507..615D and a central unresolved mass of 8 10^ 6 M _ 2005MNRAS.364..353R , which we assume to be the central black hole's mass. For the parameter we adopt the value =0.005 from our reference model. 2011ApJ...729L..27R recently described a massive, wide-angle outflow from Mrk 231 and estimate an extension of the outflow of order 3 kpc , a velocity of 10^3 km s ^ 1 and a mass outflow rate More precisely, 2011ApJ...729L..27R estimate the mass outflow rate as M = 420 (R/3 kpc ) [ 0.1/(N( Na I )/N( Na )) ] M _ yr ^ 1 where N( Na I ) and N( Na ) are the column densities of Na I and Na, respectively, and R is the extension of the outflow.

For Mrk 231 the brackets in eq. eq:detq equal unity and by solving the equation g(q)=1 we can determine q 1.38 and thus the (time-averaged) surface density profile in Mrk 231 as s^ 1.38 . The corresponding mass flows (eqs. eq:massflow-anaI and eq:massflow-anaII ) we plot in Fig. fig:massflows (f) and compare them with numerical solutions of this model to show the agreement of numerical and analytical description also for these parameters. Additionally Fig. fig:massflows (f) shows that the Eddington limit is indeed much smaller than the disc's mass supply rate for not too large times as we assumed in the previous section.

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 231 as a high-polarization type-1 AGN case, linking its unexplained CO blue-wing extension and possible non-axisymmetric outflows to elevated polarization.

quotes.quote: However, it is noteworthy to point out that some peculiar type-1 AGN like Mrk 231 Gallagher2005 and many Warm Infrared Ultraluminous AGN in the survey of Hines1994 show a relatively high, up to 4 polarization degree. Our grid of models is actually not able to reproduce such high percentage of polarization despite the extended space of parameters. It indicates that the clue to this high polarization level must be different from usual optical depth and half opening angle considerations.

A third explanation may come from IRAM PdBI observations of the extended outflows in Mrk 231 Cicone2012 . They discovered a possible extension of the CO(1-0) transition blue wing a few arcseconds to the north which stays unexplained. If we assume that the ionized polar outflows sustain the same half opening angle and direction as the massive AGN-driven winds that can be considered as a naturally extended, dust mixed extension Antonucci1993, Cracco2011 , the polar outflows in Mrk 231 might be non axisymmetric, similarly to NGC 1068 Raban2009,Goosmann2010 . As the system is seen from the pole, the overall picture would be less symmetric due to the winds inclination and then create an higher polarization degree.

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 231 as a nearby comparison BALQSO, emphasizing its high UV polarization, proximity, high-frequency VLBA observations, and a resolved secondary close to the radio core.

quotes.quote: In order to pursue a study of polar BALQSO geometry, high resolution (high frequency), VLBI (Very Long Baseline Interferometry) observations with high sensitivity (because of the weak secondary components and jets) are required in order to see the jet direction near the accretion disk. Mrk 231 is the best choice. The proximity, z=0.042 , and the large flux density of 100 mJy at 22 GHz are two factors that greatly enhance the spatial resolution achievable in observations. We have examined high frequency VLBA (Very Long Baseline Array) radio observations of Mrk 231 and their geometric implications rey09 .

Mrk 231 is also a FeLoBALQSO with high near UV continuum polarization, 13 smi95 . The data for Mrk 231 is superior due to its proximity to Earth. In rey09 , we considered two epochs of 43, 22 and 15 GHz VLBA observations of the resolved core of Mrk 231 and also the time variability of the historic light curve at 22 GHz. A resolved secondary exists just 0.8 pc from the core (see Figure 3).

sources.summary: Markarian 231 is presented as the best-studied molecular wind case, with a 1 kpc outflow whose molecular mass declines outward and compact warm OH outflow gas consistent with CO/HCN trends.

quotes.quote: The best studied molecular wind so far (Markarian 231, Feruglio et al. 2015) has a size of 1 kpc. At this distance the mass in outflow strongly reduces, while its velocity remains nearly constant, suggesting that a large part of the molecular gas leaves the flow during its expansion. This molecular gas may rain back onto the nucleus or the disk, replenishing the gas reservoirs.

Gonzalez-Alfonso et al. (2014) present a detailed analysis of OH signal in Markarian 231. The blue wing of the absorption detected in the high-lying 65 m doublet with E_ lower =290 K, with high-velocity shifts > 1000 km/s, indicates that the excited outflowing gas is generated in a compact and warm (circum)nuclear region (diameter of a few hundred pc). Aalto et al 2012 and Cicone et al. 2012 found that the molecular outflow size in Markarian 231 decreases with the critical density (it is smaller for higher CO transition and for HCN). OH transitions may lie on this trend.

sources.summary: Mrk 231 is discussed as the nearest quasar with H I absorption from a rotating hundred-parsec disk and a fast outflow likely driven by a wide-angle nuclear wind.

quotes.quote: Another example of this is seen in high-resolution observations of the nearest quasar, Mrk 231, studied in detail by Carilli98a using both VLA and VLBA. They found a rotating disk with an east-west velocity gradient of about 110 \ traced by \ absorption against a diffuse component on scales of hundred parsecs. However, this absorption disappears when full resolution spectra are taken only from the core. Thus, also in this object the absorption is not against the pc-scale radio core, but against a more diffuse radio continuum component seen on hundred pc scales. A circumnuclear disk similar to those used by Gallimore99 in their modelling can be invoked to explain these findings, where in this case the \ is located at a few tens of parsec from the BH.

For example, in the case of Mrk 231 an \ outflow is present which reaches velocities up to 1300 \ blueshifted compared to the systemic velocity and which has properties very similar to outflows found in other radio galaxies. However, the energetics of the radio source, and the lack of a clear kpc-scale jet, suggest that the most likely origin of the \ outflow is a wide-angle nuclear wind, as earlier proposed for this object to explain the neutral outflow traced by Na I and by molecular gas.

sources.summary: The paper describes MRK 231 as an obscured, strong-AGN merger remnant with molecular and ultrafast outflows, complex X-ray absorption, thermal emission, and broad Fe K emission.

quotes.quote: MRK 231: Obscured source with a strong AGN, studied by 2010ApJ...725.1848T . A connection between MO and UFO is found in this source by 2015A&A...583A..99F . The authors confirm an energy conserving mechanism responsible for creating the molecular outflows from the UFOs. Chandra imaging and spectroscopy has been carried out by 2014ApJ...790..116V . A Nustar hard X-ray view of this source has been carried out by 2015ApJ...814...56T .

We found that this source has a complex spectrum which required one component of warm absorber, one component of thermal emission ( APEC ), a neutral intrinsic absorption as well as a broad Fe K emission line. The powerlaw slope is flat and its lower value is pegged at =1.5 .

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 231 as a late-stage ULIRG merger and nearby quasar whose molecular outflow contains dense gas complexes affected by UV-driven chemistry.

quotes.quote: Mrk 231 is a late-stage merger, classified as ultra-luminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG) ( L_ IR ( 8-1000 m)=1.3 10^ 46 erg s^ 1 ), likely on its path to becoming an elliptical galaxy Lipari+05 . At least 30 of the IR luminosity is due to dust heated by an active galactic nucleus (AGN) ( , but estimate a higher AGN contribution). Despite its weak emission in the hard X-ray band Teng+14, Piconcelli+13, Braito+04 , the elevated AGN bolometric luminosity ( L_ AGN 8 10^ 45 erg s^ 1 , Leighly+14 ) makes Mrk 231 the closest quasar known. However, most of the IR luminosity traces a young starburst (age < 250 Myr , Davies+07 ), forming stars at a rate of SFR 200 M_ yr^ 1 Taylor+99 , fed by 10^9 M _ of molecular (H _2 ) gas distributed in a kpc-size disk Downes+Solomon98 . The multi-wavelength and multi-scale emission of Mrk 231 is overwhelmingly affected by explosive phenomena, which are a manifestation of feedback from the star formation and quasar activity, as predicted by theoretical models Chevalier+Clegg85, Silk+Rees98, Murray+05, Faucher-Giguere+12, Zubovas+King12, Zubovas+King14, Nims+15, Thompson+16, Ishibashi+18 and `observed' in hydrodynamical simulations DiMatteo+05, Sijacki+07, Costa+15, Costa+18b, Biernacki+Teyssier18, Richings+18a, Schneider+18 .

Besides the BAL winds, which are confined within <10 pc, Mrk 231 hosts one of the best-studied galaxy-scale outflows, revealed for the first time in OH absorption by and in CO emission by . Using these earlier observations it was estimated that the outflow entrains 3 10^8 M _ of H _2 gas and extends out to at least 1 kpc into the interstellar medium (ISM) Cicone+12 .

sources.summary: The paper reports likely molecular oxygen emission in Mrk 231, associates it with AGN-driven molecular outflow gas, and confirms extended NOEMA emission far from the nucleus.

quotes.quote: The velocity of the O _2 emission in Mrk 231 coincides with the red wing seen in CO emission, suggesting that it is associated with the outflowing molecular gas, located mainly at about ten kpc away from the central AGN. This first detection of extragalactic molecular oxygen provides an ideal tool to study AGN-driven molecular outflows on dynamic time scales of tens of Myr. O _2 may be a significant coolant for molecular gas in such regions affected by AGN-driven outflows.

Thus, Mrk 231 is a good choice to search for extragalactic O _2 emission. At a distance of 172 Mpc Fischer2010 , Mrk 231 is the nearest QSO and the most luminous ULIRG (Ultra-Luminous InfraRed Galaxy, L_ IR >10^ 12 L_ ) in the local universe Feruglio2010 . The molecular mass outflow rate in Mrk 231 is estimated to be 700 M_ /year based on CO J=1-0 observations, which is higher than the star formation rate of 200 M_ /yr Feruglio2010 .
O _2 N_J = 1_1 1_0 emission detected with the NOEMA extends to 15'' (1 '' =0.87kpc for Mrk 231) from the center of galaxy (see Figure 4).

sources.summary: The paper presents VLA polarimetry of Mrk 231, describing it as a Seyfert/BALQSO/ULIRG with a broad one-sided outflow, weak jet or lobe, starburst, and AGN-driven winds.

quotes.quote: We present the results of a multi-frequency, multi-scale radio polarimetric study with the Very Large Array (VLA) of the Seyfert 1 galaxy and BALQSO, Mrk 231. We detect complex total and polarized intensity features in the source. Overall, the images indicate the presence of a broad, one-sided, curved outflow towards the south which consists of a weakly collimated jet with poloidal inferred magnetic fields, inside a broader magnetized ``wind'' or ``sheath'' component with toroidal inferred magnetic fields. The model of a kpc-scale weakly collimated jet/lobe in Mrk 231 is strengthened by its C-shaped morphology, steep spectral index throughout, complexities in the magnetic field structures, and the presence of self-similar structures observed on the 10-parsec-scale in the literature.

Mrk 231 ( aka UGC 8058 or VII Zw 490) at a redshift of 0.04217 is a well-studied Seyfert type 1 galaxy Sanders88b, Surace98 . In fact this optically identified source is known to be the most luminous object in the local ( z<0.1 ) Universe Sanders88b . It has alternately been classified as a Broad Absorption Line Quasi Stellar Object BALQSO; Boksenberg77, Smith95, Gallagher02 , and an Ultra Luminous InfraRed Galaxy ULIRG; Sanders88a .

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 231 among AGNs with the largest radius-luminosity deviations and describes its spectrum and inferred Eddington ratio as consistent with high accretion.

quotes.quote: Four targets, Mrk 1239, Mrk 231, IRAS 09149 6206, and PDS 456, show the most significant deviation from the relation. As discussed in Section ssec:mass , all of them, except for IRAS 09149 6206, are at or above or above their Eddington luminosity. The Eddington ratio of IRAS 09149 6206 (about 0.4) is also high among the typical AGNs (e.g. the rest of the sample). Thus the large deviations from the \ relation could be related to their high accretion rates Du2019 .

Four AGNs, Mrk 1239, Mrk 231, IRAS 13349+2438, and PDS 456, are close to or above the Eddington accretion. Their optical spectra (see their references in Col. (10) of Table tab:smp ) commonly show features of narrow widths of the broad \ lines, weak or no \ lines, and strong \ features. These features strongly indicate that these targets have high accretion rates ( Boroson1992,Shen2014 and references therein), consistent with our Eddington ratios.

sources.summary: The paper presents Markarian 231 as a luminous IRAS Seyfert 1 galaxy with an AGN jet and powerful OH megamasers whose line emission traces nuclear gas.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The pointing position for Mrk 231 (referred to hereafter as the reference position) was 12 56 14 2383 +56 52 25 210 (J2000).

quotes.quote: Markarian (Mrk) 231 is one of the most luminous and best studied IRAS galaxies, with (L_ FIR / L _ )=12.04 . It has a redshift of 0.042, yielding an estimated distance of 170 Mpc We adopt H_0=75 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 . At the distance of Markarian 231 one arcsec corresponds to 820 pc. . Optical spectroscopy shows broad lines characteristic of a Seyfert 1 nucleus . A radio jet is apparent in VLBI and VLA images on scales of sub-pc to hundreds of pc, with a weak counter-jet ( Ulvestad99a and references therein), providing unusually strong evidence for the presence of an AGN in an OH megamaser region.

Mrk 231 shows powerful OH megamaser emission first detected by Baan85 . Baan92 reported emission in all 4 ground-state transitions; the 1667-MHz line was stronger than the 1665-, 1720- and 1612-MHz lines by factors of approximately 2.3, 13 and 30, respectively. Using the Lovell telescope with a beam size of 10 arcmin, Stavely-Smith87 measured emission over 6.9 MHz and estimated the total velocity width of the 1667-MHz emission to be 760 100 km s ^ 1 with a peak maser flux density of 48 mJy.

sources.summary: Mrk 231 is described as an infrared quasar with AGN-dominated radio structure, a young compact jet source, substantial circumnuclear starburst luminosity, and absorbed hard X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: Mrk 231, in some senses the classical "infrared quasar", has been studied with the VLBI by ulv . Their images (see also Lonsdale et al. 2003a) show a triple structure, with a core and two lobes which classify it as a Compact Symmetric Object (CSO). It has been suggested that CSOs are young, << 10^6 yr, with the hot spots representing the working surface of a relativistic jet upon the ambient medium read96 . If the southern (primary) lobe/hot-spot in Mrk 231 is confined by ram pressure, lon2003 estimate a lobe advance speed, v_a 10^ 4 and an age for the jet/compact source, < 10^6 yr.
In Mrk 231, for example, several studies car,lon2003,far2 suggest that more than half of the total luminosity comes from a circumnuclear starburst in a molecular ring rather than from the AGN.

In Mrk 231 BeppoSAX revealed a highly absorbed ( N_H 2 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 ) power-law component bra and analysis of XMM-Newton data indicates that below 10 keV only scattered or reflected X-rays escape. In a 40ks observation of of Mrk 231, gal find the majority of the X-ray luminosity is emitted from an unresolved nuclear point source with a very hard spectrum, the majority of the flux emitted above 2 keV.

sources.summary: The paper presents Mrk 231 as a nearby ULIRG with a luminous AGN, intense star formation, a massive molecular disk, and X-ray-driven excitation of its inner gas.

quotes.quote: In this Letter, we discuss the first results of the HerCULES programme and present the SPIRE FTS spectrum of the nearby ULIRG 231 ( UGC 8058 , F12540 + 5708 ), the most luminous galaxy in the Revised IRAS Bright Galaxy Sample Sandersetal03 . Adopting z = 0.042170 as the heliocentric redshift of 231 , correcting for the local flow, and applying a flat 5-year WMAP cosmology ( H_0 = 70 Mpc , _ = 0.73 ) yields a luminosity distance D L =192 , with 1'' corresponding to 0.856 , as provided by NED http://nedwww.ipac.caltech.edu/ . The derived 8-1000 luminosity of 231 is then =4.0 10^ 12 . 231 contains a luminous, optically visible AGN, classified as a Seyfert 1 or a Broad Absorption Line QSO Boksenbergetal77 .

However, 231 also contains a kpc size disk harbouring intense star formation as shown by high resolution radio imaging Tayloretal99 . Interferometric imaging of CO J=1 0 and J=2 1 emission shows an inner disk of radius 520 , containing 45 of the total molecular gas mass, and embedded in a more extended and diffuse emission component; the total molecular gas mass is 5 10^9 DownesSolomon98 .

sources.summary: The paper presents CO observations of Mrk 231 showing broad, kpc-scale molecular outflow wings that can expel its cold gas reservoir and quench star formation.

quotes.quote: We used the IRAM PdB Interferometer to observe the CO(1--0) transition in Mrk 231, the closest quasar known. Thanks to the wide band we detect broad wings of the CO line, with velocities up to 750 km/s and spatially resolved on the kpc scale. Such broad CO wings trace a giant molecular outflow of about 700 M _ /year, far larger than the ongoing star-formation rate ( 200 M _ /year) observed in the host galaxy. This wind will totally expel the cold gas reservoir in Mrk 231 in about 10 ^7 yrs, therefore halting the star-formation activity on the same timescale.

In this paper we present new CO(1--0) observations of Mrk 231 obtained with the IRAM Plateau de Bure Interferometer (PdBI). Mrk 231 is the nearest example of a quasar object and it is the most luminous Ultra-Luminous Infrared Galaxy (ULIRG) in the local Universe (Sanders et al. 1988) with an infrared luminosity of 3.6 10^ 12 L _ (assuming a distance of 186 Mpc).

sources.summary: The paper discusses Mrk 231 as a ULIRG with molecular emission, connecting detected ions to high cosmic-ray ionization models while noting ambiguity with X-ray chemistry.

quotes.quote: Another interesting object which has been recently studied in molecular emission is Mrk 231, a ULIRG. A high resolution SPIRE FTS spectrum reveals the presence of ions such as OH ^+ , CH ^+ and H _2 O ^+ VanderWerf10 . While abundances are not derived, we can use our Table tab:3 to determine what type of model is able to produce high fractional abundances ( 10 ^ 10 ) of these three ions. We find that the only regime is an environment with low metallicity (0.1 solar), very high cosmic ray ionisation rates ( 10 ^ 16 s ^ 1 ) and low visual extinction.

VanderWerf10 explained the high abundances of these ions by involving XDR-chemistry. We find that, in agreement with Papa10a , determining the origin of molecular emission from ULIRGs such as Mrk 231 is not trivial when both sources of energy (CR and X-rays) are present.

sources.summary: The paper describes Markarian 231 as a nearby LoBAL quasar and ULIRG whose variable Suzaku X-ray spectrum is explained by partial-covering absorbers linked to BAL-wind shielding gas and outflow feedback.

quotes.quote: We report the results from a 2011 Suzaku observation of the nearby low-ionization BAL quasar/ULIRG Markarian 231. These data reveal that the X-ray spectrum has undergone a large variation from the 2001 \ and \ observations. We interpret this finding according to a scenario whereby the X-ray continuum source is obscured by a two-component partial-covering absorber with \ 10 ^ 22 and 10 ^ 24 2, respectively. The observed spectral change is mostly explained by a progressive appearance of the primary continuum at < 10 keV due to the decrease of the covering fraction of the denser absorption component.

The properties of the X-ray obscuration in Mrk 231 match well with those of the X-ray shielding gas predicted by the theoretical models for an efficient radiatively-driven acceleration of the BAL wind. In particular, the X-ray absorber might be located at the extreme base of the outflow.

sources.summary: The paper cites Mrk 231 as a key AGN/ULIRG case where Herschel molecular lines reveal SMBH accretion, star formation, X-ray-dominated CO excitation, and non-equilibrium chemistry.

quotes.quote: One of the lessons learned from the Science Demonstration Phase of Herschel was that the presence of both SMBH accretion and star formation in Mrk 231 can be deduced from the CO spectral line energy distribution (SLED). A SPIRE FTS spectrum with CO lines up to J=13-12 between wavelengths =303 - 670 m VdWerf2010 was obtained within the Open Time Key Program HerCULES (PI: Van der Werf). The spectrum shows CO, [ CI ] , and [ NII ] lines, and surprisingly also strong lines of H _2 O and the ionic species OH ^+ and H _2 O ^+ .

They showed that the excitation of the CO ladder in the circumnuclear molecular disk (CMD) is dominated by the nuclear X-ray radiation field, out to a radius of 160 pc. In a companion paper Gonzalez2010 the modeling was done for the H _2 O lines and the conclusions was that the gas excitation and chemistry in Mrk 231 is by affected shocks/cosmic rays, an XDR chemistry, and/or an ``undepleted chemistry'' where grain mantles are evaporated. Although some lessons were drawn from the model results for Mrk 231, it is not satisfactory to need separate modeling for, e.g., the CO and H _2 O lines.

sources.summary: The paper reports blazar-like radio flares in Mrk 231, arguing that this BAL quasar has suppressed but emerging relativistic jet activity.

quotes.quote: Radio monitoring of the broad absorption line quasar (BALQSO) Mrk 231 from 13.9 GHz to 17.6 GHz detected a strong flat spectrum flare. Even though BALQSOs are typically weak radio sources, the 17.6 GHz flux density doubled in 150 days, from 135 mJy to 270 mJy. It is demonstrated that the elapsed rise time in the quasar rest frame and the relative magnitude of the flare is typical of some of the stronger flares in blazars that are usually associated with the ejection of discrete components on parsec scales. The decay of a similar flare was found in a previous monitoring campaign at 22 GHz. We conclude that these flares are not rare.
The implication is that Mrk 231 seems to be a quasar in which the physical mechanism that produces the BAL wind is in tension with the emergence of a fledgling blazar.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 231 as a ULIRG and Seyfert 1 whose HCO+/HCN ratio resembles normal galaxies despite expectations for an AGN enhancement.

quotes.quote: Our selected galaxies, have been detected previously in HCN, and have different degrees of nuclear activity --- one normal galaxy (NGC 6946), the starburst prototype (M 82) and two ultraluminous infrared galaxies (ULIRGs, IRAS 17208-0014 and Mrk 231). We plotted our data in the HCO ^+ /HCN vs. HCN/ ^ 13 CO diagnostic diagram finding that NGC 6946 and M 82 are located close to other normal galaxies; and that both IRAS 17208-0014 and Mrk 231 are close to the position of the well known ULIRG Arp 220 reported by  Snell2011 . We found that in Mrk 231 -- a galaxy with a well known active galactic nucleus -- the HCO ^+ /HCN ratio is similar to the ratio observed in other normal galaxies.

M 82 and NGC 6946 are close to other normal galaxies reported by  Snell2011 . In that paper they also observed M 82 (marked with a D in the Figure) with the same receiver but mounted in the FCRAO 15 m antenna. The differences between the HCN, ^ 13 CO ratio can be explained by the different beam sizes between the LMT and the FCRAO and the relative distribution of the dense gas traced by HCN and the more diffuse gas traced by ^ 13 CO. The two ultra--luminous galaxies (ULIRGs) observed, IRAS 17208-0014 and Mrk 231, are close to the position of the well known ULIRG Arp 220, indicating the presence of high amounts of dense gas in both galaxies. Notice that Mrk 231, a well known active galaxy classified as a Seyfert 1, has not an HCO ^+ enhancement as predicted by the theoretical models. By using all the available data of this galaxy, which cover all the electromagnetic spectrum, we expect to better understand this discrepancy.

sources.summary: The paper presents NuSTAR and Chandra observations of Markarian 231/Mrk 231, finding a Compton-thin absorbed, intrinsically X-ray weak nearest BAL quasar in a ULIRG merger remnant with starburst and AGN activity.

quotes.quote: We present high-energy (3--30 keV) NuSTAR observations of the nearest quasar, the ultraluminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG) Markarian 231 (Mrk 231), supplemented with new and simultaneous low-energy (0.5--8 keV) data from Chandra . The source was detected, though at much fainter levels than previously reported, likely due to contamination in the large apertures of previous non-focusing hard X-ray telescopes. The full band (0.5--30 keV) X-ray spectrum suggests the active galactic nucleus (AGN) in Mrk 231 is absorbed by a patchy and Compton-thin (N _ H 1.2^ +0.3 _ 0.3 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ) column. The intrinsic X-ray luminosity (L _ 0.5-30 keV 1.0 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 ) is extremely weak relative to the bolometric luminosity where the 2--10 keV to bolometric luminosity ratio is 0.03 compared to the typical values of 2--15 .

Mrk 231 is a merger remnant, containing both an intense starburst and a luminous AGN. The starburst has a star formation rate (SFR) of 140 M _ yr ^ 1 rv13 , estimated from its total infrared luminosity.

sources.summary: The paper presents deep Chandra analysis of Mrk 231, describing its quasar/starburst merger nature, multiphase outflow, and extended hot X-ray nebula.

quotes.quote: Thomson Ave., Cambridge, CB3 0HE, UK 6 Kavli Institute for Cosmology, Madingley Road, Cambridge, CB3 0HA, UK 7 Max-Planck-Institut f ur extraterrestrische Physik, Postfach 1312, D-85741 Garching, Germany A deep 400-ksec ACIS-S observation of the nearest quasar known, Mrk 231, is combined with archival 120-ksec data obtained with the same instrument and setup to carry out the first ever spatially resolved spectral analysis of a hot X-ray emitting circumgalactic nebula around a quasar. The 65 50 kpc X-ray nebula shares no resemblance with the tidal debris seen at optical wavelengths. One notable exception is the small tidal arc 3.5 kpc south of the nucleus where excess soft X-ray continuum emission and Si XIII 1.8 keV line emission are detected, consistent with star formation and its associated element enhancement, respectively.

An X-ray shadow is also detected at the location of the 15-kpc northern tidal tail, implying a foreground hydrogen column density of at least 2.5 10 ^ 21 cm ^ 2 . The hard X-ray continuum emission within 6 kpc of the center is consistent with being due entirely to the bright central AGN and the wings of the Chandra point spread function. The soft X-ray spectrum of the outer ( > 6 kpc) portion of the nebula is best described as the sum of two thermal components of temperatures 3 and 8 million K with spatially uniform super-solar element abundances, relative to iron.

sources.summary: Mrk 231 is compared with Arp 220 as a ULIRG sharing isotope and dense-gas features, while its chemistry more closely resembles the survey's AGN hosts.

quotes.quote: Assuming that this ratio may be similar for the rest of the galaxies, the expected vibrational intensities in Mrk 231 would be 0.2 mK, which is well below our rms level. Thus, we cannot discard lower level vibrational emission in Mrk 231. There are also common features between the Mrk 231 and Arp 220 spectra as compared to the rest of the sample. In agreement with previous studies Greve09,Martin11,Henkel14 ^ 13 CO and C ^ 18 O are equally abundant in both ULIRGs. It can be seen in Fig. abundances that the ^ 13 CO/C ^ 18 O ratios in Mrk 231 and Arp 220 are a factor of 4 lower than in the rest of the galaxies.
Furthermore, the chemistry of Arp 220 points towards a more starburst-dominated environment, while that of Mrk 231 resembles more the AGNs of our sample.

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 231 as a well-studied late-stage merger quasar with massive molecular and neutral outflows, applying its observed parameters to constrain a small initial outflow radius.

quotes.quote: One of the best studied cases is Mrk 231, a nearby quasar in late stage merger, which is one of the most luminous ULIRGs in the local Universe Fischer_et_2010, Feruglio_et_2010, Rupke_Veilleux_2011 . Observations of OH features and CO lines indicate the presence of a massive molecular outflow, spatially resolved on kpc scale and with an outflow rate largely exceeding the star formation rate Fischer_et_2010, Feruglio_et_2010 . A powerful neutral outflow, with velocities reaching 1100 km/s and extending out to radii of 2-3 kpc, is also detected in this object Rupke_Veilleux_2011 .

As a specific example, we can consider the case of Mrk 231. Adopting the reported values for the velocity dispersion ( = 120 km/s), central luminosity ( L 10^ 46 erg/s), and outflowing mass ( M_ sh 3 10^8 M_ ) Veilleux_et_2013, Cicone_et_2014 , we obtain values of 10 at the radius of observation, consistent with the value of = 26/3 reported in Cicone_et_2014 , for an initial radius of R_0 10 pc. Thus the momentum flux may provide a constraint on the initial radius, requiring a quite small value of R_0 in this particular system (for a fiducial IR opacity).

sources.summary: Mrk 231 is presented as a merger remnant with a luminous Type 1 quasar, intense starburst, AGN-dominated infrared luminosity, intrinsic X-ray weakness, and ultrafast outflow.

quotes.quote: Mrk 231 is a merger remnant that contains both an intense starburst and a luminous quasar with a Type 1 optical spectral classification. It is also a rare iron low-ionization broad absorption line quasar FeLoBAL; e.g., aw72, g02, g05, vei13a . Continuum and emission-line diagnostics from Spitzer spectra indicate that the infrared luminosity of this source is dominated by an AGN vei09a . teng14 found that the AGN is Compton-thin and intrinsically X-ray weak, with the intrinsic 2--10 keV luminosity being only 0.03 of the AGN bolometric luminosity. The NuSTAR and Chandra X-ray data on this source were also analyzed by feruglio15 .
Their results suggest the presence of an ultra-fast outflow where the ionized wind reaches speeds of 2 10^ 4 .

sources.summary: The paper studies Mrk 231 as a ULIRG/IR quasar merger with AGN and starburst activity, finding chemically differentiated dense molecular outflow emission in HCN, HCO+, and HNC.

quotes.quote: We obtained high-resolution interferometric observations of HCN and HCO ^+ J=1 0 and J=2 1 of the ultraluminous infrared galaxy Mrk 231 with the IRAM Plateau de Bure Interferometer. We also use previously published observations of HCN and HCO ^+ J=1 0 and J=3 2 , and HNC J=1 0 in the same source. In the line wings of the HCN, HCO ^+ , and HNC emission, we find that these three molecular species exhibit features at distinct velocities which differ between the species.

The ultra-luminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG) Mrk 231 ( (L_ IR /L_ )=12.37 ), often referred to as the most nearby (175 Mpc) IR quasar (QSO), is a major galaxy merger and hosts both AGN activity and a young, dusty starburst with an extreme star formation rate of 200 M_ yr ^ 1 taylor99,gallagher02,lipari09 . Mrk 231 is well-known for its massive molecular outflow feruglio10,fischer10 . In previous IRAM Plateau de Bure Interferometer (PdBI) observations, aalto12 found an extremely high HCN/CO 1--0 line ratio (0.3--1) in the Mrk 231 outflow, which suggests that a large fraction of the molecular gas in the outflow is dense ( n 10^4 cm ^ 3 ).

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 231 as a broad-absorption-line quasar with a relativistic jet, recurrent radio flares, ejected VLBA components, and changing wind/X-ray behavior.

quotes.quote: Long term radio monitoring of the broad absorption line quasar, Mrk 231, at 17.6 GHz detected a strong flare in 2015. This triggered four epochs of Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) observations from 8.4 GHz to 43 GHz as well as three epochs of X-ray observations with NuSTAR and two with XMM over a 15 week period. Two ejected components were detected by the VLBA observations.
Mrk 231 is becoming more radio loud. We found that the putative parsec scale radio lobe doubled in brightness in 9 years.
Mrk 231, at a redshift of z = 0.042 , is a nearby radio quiet quasar (RQQ) with a relativistic jet rey09 .

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 231 as a strong local ULIRG outflow template whose OH and water-line absorption wings would guide SPICA detections of molecular feedback at high redshift.

quotes.quote: We adopt 3 local template ULIRGs to make predictions on the detectability of molecular outflows at high- z , by scaling the far-IR spectroscopic observations obtained with Herschel /PACS: IRAS 03158+4227, Mrk 231, and IRAS 23365+3604 (GA17). The IR luminosities ( 8-1000 m, L_ IR ) are listed in Table tab1 . All three sources, as well as many other local ULIRGs, show clear evidence for massive molecular outflows from P-Cygni line profiles in the ground-state OH119 and OH79 doublets e.g. fis10 . In the cases of IRAS 03158+4227 and Mrk 231, high-velocity ( 1000 ) absorption wings are also detected in the excited OH84 and OH65 doublets, indicating compact outflowing gas with high column densities (GA17).

Based on our Mrk 231 template and the expected SAFARI sensitivities, several H _2 O lines with _ rest <77 m would be detected up to z 2 with the LR mode of SPICA /SAFARI in only 2 hours of observing time. In Mrk 231, these lines peak at systemic velocities and show a blueshifted absorption wing, which would be too weak even for SPICA to detect at high redshifts.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes multi-epoch VLBI data for Mrk 231, finding a slow bent radio jet whose interaction with the ISM may power wide-angle shocks and outflows.

quotes.quote: Mrk 231 is the closest radio-quiet quasar known and one of the most luminous infrared galaxies in the local Universe. It is characterised by the co-existence of a radio jet and powerful multi-phase multi-scale outflows, making it an ideal laboratory to study active galactic nucleus (AGN) feedback. We analyse the multi-epoch very long baseline interferometry data of Mrk 231 and estimate the jet head advance speed to be 0.013 \ , suggesting a sub-relativistic jet flow.

The jet position angle changes from 113 in the inner parsec to 172 at a projected distance of 25 parsec. The jet structure change might result from either a jet bending following the rotation of the circum-nuclear disc or the projection of a helical jet on the plane of the sky. In the large opening angle ( 60 ) cone, the curved jet interacts with the interstellar medium and creates wide-aperture-angle shocks which subsequently dissipate a large portion of the jet power through radiation and contribute to powering the large-scale outflows.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 231 as an exceptional ultraluminous infrared BAL QSO with tidal disturbance and hard, variable nuclear X-ray emission from its active nucleus.

quotes.quote: We also present an unpublished FOS spectrum from the \ archive showing the broad \ absorption. galaxies: active --- galaxies: individual (Mrk 231, UGC 08058) --- galaxies: starburst --- X-rays: galaxies The extraordinary galaxy Markarian 231 was discovered in 1969 as part of a survey searching for galaxies with strong ultraviolet continua Ma1969 . With the first spectrum, it was identified as unique among the Markarian sample because of deep, blue-shifted Na D absorption evocative of broad absorption line quasi-stellar objects BAL QSOs; ArDiEs1971 . Soon after, AdWe1972 also identified broad absorption lines from \ H and K and \ at the same velocity as the Na D system. In addition, they first described the long tidal tails and disturbed morphology of the galaxy.

X-ray Variability and Spectral Results With this \ observation, we can demonstrate convincingly that the 2--8 keV X-ray luminosity is dominated by the unresolved active nucleus in Mrk 231. The hard-band radial profile is consistent with the \ PSF, and the significant, hard-band variability provides compelling evidence that \ is probing within light hours of the central black hole.

sources.summary: The paper maps hydroxyl megamaser emission in Mrk 231, interpreting it as a rotating dusty molecular torus in a luminous infrared Seyfert merger with tidal tails.

quotes.quote: Here we report observations of powerful hydroxyl (OH) line emissions that trace this obscuring material within the circumnuclear environment of the galaxy Markarian 231. The hydroxyl (mega)--maser emission shows the characteristics of a rotating, dusty, molecular torus (or thick disk) located between 30 and 100 pc from the central engine. We now have a clear view of the physical conditions, the kinematics and the spatial structure of this material on intermediate size scales, confirming the main tenets of unification models.

To study the distribution of the hydroxyl megamaser emission with respect to the nuclear engine at a resolution of tens of parsecs in Mrk 231, we performed very--long--baseline interferometry (VLBI) radio observations with the European VLBI Network (EVN). Mrk 231 is the most luminous infrared galaxy in the local universe (distance of 172 Mpc), and is undergoing a merger as indicated by the high dust content and the prominent tidal tails observed at optical wavelengths. The nuclear power source has been classified 1998ApJ...509..633B as a Seyfert 1, which in the unification scheme would suggest that the observer has a direct, and almost unobscured, line--of--sight view into the broad--line emission region at parsec scales.

sources.summary: The paper includes Mrk231 in its HCN SED comparison, noting only HCN(1-0) and HCN(4-3) data are published and that its dense gas excitation is lower than NGC253.

quotes.quote: We have used our new HCN observations as well as data from the literature to compare the excitation of the dense gas via the HCN SEDs in NGC253, M82, NGC4945, NGC6240, Mrk231 and Arp220 (references are given in the caption of Fig. fig:normseds ). We restrict ourselves here to HCN because it provides the most complete data set among tracers of the dense gas for these active galaxies. All galaxies except Mrk231 have been measured in HCN(1 0), HCN(3 2), and HCN(4 3). For Mrk231, only the HCN(1 0) and HCN(4 3) have been published in the literature.
Interestingly, NGC6240 and Mrk231 show a lower excitation of the dense gas phase compared to NGC253 (Fig. fig:normseds ) despite their much higher FIR luminosities (Fig. fig:hcnhco_vs_lir ).

sources.summary: Mrk 231 is presented as a lo-BAL QSO/BLS1 ULIRG with strong silicate absorption and luminous 6.2 micron PAH emission indicating a significant, though not dominant, starburst.

quotes.quote: Two of them (5-15 m spectra are shown in Figure fig10 ), are the only objects showing a strong 9.7 m silicate absorption: a NLS1 (IRAS 11119+3257) and a BLS1 (Mrk 231), both characterized by ULIRG infrared emission. Mrk 231 is a well known low-ionization broad absorption line quasar (lo-BAL QSO), with a broad line region optically detectable even if the nucleus is heavily obscured in X-rays (Gallagher et al. 2005). Its MIR spectrum shows not only the silicate absorption related to such an absorbed ULIRG, but also a luminous 6.2 m PAH emission (L(6.2,PAH) 3*10^ 42 erg/s) pointing out a significant starburst, even if not dominant (R= 0.17 10^ 2 ). From the comparison of their low resolution spectra, IRAS 11119+3257 appears as the optical narrow line counterpart of Mrk 231. The only difference being the relative intensity of the Starburst, which in IRAS 11119+3257 is about twice the Mrk 231 values.

sources.summary: The paper presents high-frequency VLBI observations of BALQSO Mrk 231, resolving a compact double radio nucleus and linking a strong core flare to Doppler-boosted relativistic ejecta.

quotes.quote: We report on the first high frequency VLBI observations of the nearby broad absorption line quasar (BALQSO), Mrk 231. Three epochs of observations were achieved at 15 GHz and 22 GHz, two of these included 43 GHz observations as well. The nuclear radio source is resolved as a compact double. The core component experienced a strong flare in which the flux density at 22 GHz increased by > 150 (45 mJy) in three months. Theoretical models of the flare imply that the emission is likely enhanced by very strong Doppler boosting of a highly relativistic ejecta with a kinetic energy flux, Q 3 10^ 43 ergs/sec .

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 231 as a ULIRG in a transitory late-merger phase, with starburst activity, unresolved jets, and radio, optical, and X-ray variability.

quotes.quote: * 1254+571 (Mrk 231) (quasar, z=0.042 ): This object is an ULIRG and is positioned in the transitory region of the color-color diagram. It exhibits starburst activity (see Sect. sec:discussion for more details). There is no jet resolved on either VLBA or VLA scales. Mrk 231 fits well into the evolutionary scenario proposed by Sanders1988 occupying a transitory phase between a merging system and an active galaxy. Combined with its variability (radio, optical, and X-rays), we argue that this is a late merger object, moving into the post-merger phase.

sources.summary: Mrk 231 is a local ULIRG with multiple Herschel water lines whose excitation implies a compact, warm far-infrared source and high nuclear water column density.

quotes.quote: The Ultra Luminous InfraRed Galaxy (ULIRG) Mrk 231 reveals up to seven rotational lines of water (H _2 O) in emission, including a very high-lying ( E_ upper =640 K) line detected at a 4 level, within the Herschel/SPIRE wavelength range ( 190 < ( m )< 640 ), whereas PACS observations show one H _2 O line at 78 m in absorption, as found for other H _2 O lines previously detected by ISO. The absorption/emission dichotomy is caused by the pumping of the rotational levels by far-infrared radiation emitted by dust, and subsequent relaxation through lines at longer wavelengths, which allows us to estimate both the column density of H _2 O and the general characteristics of the underlying far-infrared continuum source. Radiative transfer models including excitation through both absorption of far-infrared radiation emitted by dust and collisions are used to calculate the equilibrium level populations of H _2 O and the corresponding line fluxes. The highest-lying H _2 O lines detected in emission, with levels at 300-640 K above the ground state, indicate that the source of far-infrared radiation responsible for the pumping is compact (radius =110-180 pc) and warm ( T_ dust =85-95 K), accounting for at least 45 of the bolometric luminosity. The high column density, N( H_2O ) 5 10^ 17 cm ^ 2 , found in this nuclear component, is most probably the consequence of shocks/cosmic rays, an XDR chemistry, and/or an ``undepleted chemistry'' where grain mantles are evaporated.

sources.summary: Mrk 231 is used as a bright BAL-quasar ULIRG example with substantial AGN luminosity, reflected 2-10 keV emission, hard X-ray continuum, and outflow-linked obscuration.

quotes.quote: In ULIRGs the situation is even more complex: a remarkable example is represented by Mrk 231, a bright ULIRG which is optically classified as a Broad Absorption Line quasar, and whose fairly unabsorbed Spitzer IRS spectrum suggests a relative AGN contribution to the bolometric luminosity of the galaxy of 30 per cent at least. The 2--10 keV emission of Mrk 231 reveals a reflection component only (Gallagher et al. 2002), while the direct AGN continuum shows up at 15--60 keV in the BeppoSAX data (Braito et al. 2004). This mismatch between the optical and X-ray classification based on the obscuration degree in each band is not infrequent, and suggests a scarce gas/dust coupling around the AGN. Moreover, the case of Mrk 231 is indicative of the incidence of outflows: massive gas inflows are needed to sustain nuclear accretion, yet the possible link of the X-ray absorbing gas with AGN-driven winds and outflows has far-reaching implications in terms of the AGN feedback and the ULIRG/quasar evolutionary pattern. According to this scenario, ULIRGs (or at least a significant fraction of them) are a transitory phase of galactic lifetime preliminary to the optically-bright quasar stage, during which the merger-driven inflows provide the gas reservoir for starburst and buried nuclear activity, until the AGN feedback disrupts the obscuring shell of dust and gas, quenching the circumnuclear star formation and sweeping the line of sight to the optical quasar (Sanders et al. 1988; Hopkins et al. 2008).

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 231 as the closest quasar with a >1000 km/s molecular outflow, independently confirmed in CO with mass outflow far above its star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: Our recent OH-absorption observations have revealed a > 1000 km/s molecular outflow in the closest quasar known, Mrk 231 (Fischer et al. 2010). Independent, spatially resolved CO-emission observations of Mrk 231 with the IRAM/PdB mm-wave interferometer (Feruglio et al. 2010) have confirmed this outflow with inferred mass outflow rates of 300--2200 M _ /yr, significantly larger than the current star formation rate (SFR 100 M _ /yr) in the host galaxy. We now show that molecular outflows are indeed a common phenomenon in many of the luminous major mergers in our sample, reaching outflow velocities of 1000 km/s and outflow rates up to 1000 M _ /yr in some of them.

The Mrk 231 spectrum is taken from Fischer et al. (2010) and is repeated here for completeness. In all cases we detect P-Cygni profiles typical of outflows, with blue shifted absorption and red shifted emission features, of more than 1000 km/s in some cases.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 231 as a local ULIRG whose bolometric luminosity is attributed partly to AGN activity and partly to starburst emission in a molecular ring or disk.

quotes.quote: Mrk 231 Mrk 273 are local ULIRGs. It is suggested that 1/3 the bolometric luminosity of Mrk 231 is contributed by AGN, and 2/3 by starburst in the molecular ring or disk downes98,farrah03 . With respect to Mrk 273, radio observations have revealed an extreme compact starburst toward its northern region downes98,carilli00 . The HCN/HCO ^+ intensity ratios of Mrk 231 and Mrk 273 are larger than unity, whereas HCN/HCO ^+ < 1 in other galaxies. Our results are consistent with gc08 , and the intensity ratios of HCN/HCO ^+ and C _2 H/HCN are all consistent with costagliola11 . At > 1 level, we might tentatively detect HNCO(4-3) in Mrk 231.

sources.summary: The paper associates Mrk 231 HI absorption with a face-on disk, detects an additional polarized absorption feature, and links that feature to a radio-jet outflow.

quotes.quote: Mrk 231 The deep absorption trough is associated with the face-on disk of the galaxy carilli98, ulvestad99 . Our GBT full intensity HI profile of this sources mostly agrees with these previous observations, but we detect an additional absorption feature centered at relative velocity -300 km s ^ 1 that was first suspected by morganti11 . This feature is slightly polarized, with the XX spectrum having lower intensity. Although the polarization is not as high as those seen in Figure fig:polspecs , if we give the spectra of Mrk 231 the same treatment detailed in Section sec:decompose , we find that this feature has FWHM 1000 km s ^ 1 corresponding to a mass outflow rate of 0.3 M_ yr ^ 1 in the jet scenario. The polarized feature is thus likely associated with the radio jet carilli98, ulvestad99 .

sources.summary: Mrk 231 is identified as an AGN-rich LIRG with lower mid-J CO-to-infrared ratio, significant hot CO at J > 10, and an inferred AGN luminosity fraction near 52%.

quotes.quote: Of the galaxies used here, only two have f_ AGN > 40 (i.e., 56 for Mrk 231 and 60 for IRASF 05189-2524).

On the other hand, both Mrk 231 and NGC 1068 have a lower R_ midCO in Fig. 4. They both have significant hot CO gas emitting at J > 10 (Gonz alez-Alfonso et al. 2014; Hailey-Dunsheath et al. 2012), likely associated with XDR (van der Werf et al. 2010; Spinoglio et al. 2012). Furthermore, the model prediction that the XDR-associated CO emission occurs mainly at higher J levels is also supported by the observation that, with comparable C(60/100) , M 82 and Mrk 231 have almost identical CO SLEDs for J 10 (Pereira-Santaella et al. 2013).
For example, the observed R_ midCO of 4.45 for Mrk 231 implies that L^ AGN _ IR /L_ IR 52 ( 10 ), in good agreement with f_ AGN 56 from our mid-IR diagnostics.

sources.summary: The paper studies Markarian 231 as a quasar, ULIRG, merger, starburst, and AGN host whose dense molecular gas and massive outflow shape its evolution.

quotes.quote: We use the CS(2--1) measurements to derive a dense gas mass within Mrk 231 of 1.8 0.3 10^ 10 M_ , consistent with previous measurements. The CS(2--1) data also seem to indicate that the molecular disk of Mrk 231 is forming stars at about normal efficiency.

Mrk 231 has both a rapidly accreting, powerful AGN braito+04 and a starburst, with a star formation rate of 170 M_ yr ^ 1 veilleux+09 , and is classified an ultraluminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG; sanders+mirabel96 ).

Recently, it has also been shown that Mrk 231 hosts a massive outflow, observed in molecular gas fischer+10,feruglio+10,aalto+12 , neutral gas rupke+05,rupke+11,teng+13 and warm ionized gas lipari+09,rupke+11,veilleux+13 . These properties make Mrk 231 the ideal laboratory to test the ways in which dense molecular gas, star formation, and an AGN intermingle within a merging system, shedding light on the fate of the starforming material, and ultimately, the galaxy as a whole, after a merger.

sources.summary: The survey models Mrk 231 as a luminous infrared galaxy with QSO-like nucleus, starburst disk, diffuse halo, and compact hot AGN-related H2O component.

quotes.quote: Mrk 231 Mrk 231 is the most luminous infrared galaxy in the local universe, with L_ TIR 3.2 10^ 12 sanders2003 . Multi-wavelength observations reveal a QSO-like nucleus e.g., soifer2000, gallagher2002 and a compact starburst disk e.g., carilli1998, tacconi2002 , embedded in a more extended ( 1 kpc scale) diffuse disk taylor1999 . Our favored model for Mrk 231 consists of a cold ( T_ dust 30 K), extended ( R_s 1000 - 1500 pc) diffuse halo, a warm ( T_ dust 50 - 60 K) starburst disk of radius 300-550 pc and a highly obscured, compact ( Rs 60 - 70 pc) hot ( T_ dust 160 - 180 K) dense ( n( H ) 10^6 cm^ 3 ) component.

Unlike normal LIRGs of our sample, Mrk 231 shows intense high-excitation H_ 2 O features (with E_ up 600 - 700 K) in both emission and absorption, which can not be explained by the warm component. Our model reveals these high-excitation lines emerge from a very compact component with extremely high dust temperature and gas density, which is most likely related to the AGN dominated region.

sources.summary: UGC 08058, explicitly identified with Mrk 231, is the sample's most luminous ULIRG and QSO; its AGN continuum weakens line detections, but elevated line ratios appear southeast of the nucleus.

quotes.quote: UGC 08058 UGC 08058, or Mrk 231, is the most luminous ULIRG in GOALS, and hence, in this KOALA sample. As a bona fide QSO, it has been found to host molecular outflows as an indicator of AGN feedback in previous infrared and submillimeter work . With velocities well exceeding 500 km s ^ 1 1kpc away from the center, one might expect to see high velocity gas close to the ionizing source in the central kpc region. However, the SNR S/N in our emission lines are weak as the near-infrared spectrum is dominated by the AGN continuum. Thus, we were not able to directly detect outflow signatures in the or line kinematics close to the center, though a coherent region 250 pc SE of the nucleus is seen to feature elevated / ratios.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 231 as a QSO galaxy with a dense molecular outflow traced by HCN, enhanced line wings, shock-compressed gas, and no HCO+ line wings.

quotes.quote: A similar trend was also reported in the molecular outflow of the QSO galaxy Mrk 231. The detection of the outflow in HCN(1-0) by aalto12 covers the same velocity range ( 750 km/s ) of the CO(1-0) outflow fer10 , and they found a high ratio of the HCN/CO 0.3-1 in the outflow, higher than in the line core. The HCN is enhanced in the line wings by factors of 2-5, and they suggest that the outflow is mostly entrained in dense gas n 10^4 cm^ 3 , which is consistent with the molecular gas being compressed and fragmented by shocks aalto12 . High resolution observations of HCN and HCO ^+ in the higher J=3 2 transition exhibit prominent, spatially extended line wings for HCN(3-2) in Mrk 231 aalto15 . In Mrk 231 there were no line wings detected in HCO ^+ (3-2), while in NGC 613, there is some indication of high-velocity gas.

sources.summary: The paper infers a highly excited far-infrared component in Mrk 231, with high infrared surface density, nuclear luminosity near 2e12 solar luminosities, and predicted HCN ratios.

quotes.quote: Mrk 231 : A highly excited component was inferred from high-lying OH absorption in the far-IR gon14b , with an estimate radius of 55-73 pc at 192 Mpc. We thus adopt =(1.1-1.9) 10^ 2 arc sec ^2 , which with the observed HCN flux of 1.6 Jy km s ^ 1 aal15a yields F_ HCN 2 3-2 / =80-150 Jy km s ^ 1 arc sec ^ 2 . This component is not expected to have the extremely high column densities of the previous sources, but N_ H2 (0.7-2) 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 gon14b . This is consistent with a high _ IR =(1.1-2.2) 10^8 L _ pc ^ 2 (Fig. hcngreenhousecolH2 ), which translates into a luminosity of (1.8-2.1) 10^ 12 , the expected luminosity of the nucleus. The HCN _2=1^f J=4-3 line has not been observed, and our model predicts a _2=1^f J=4-3 to J=3-2 flux ratio significantly higher than for the other sources, 2.5 .

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk231 as the comparison ULIRG/AGN template, noting its SED fit to WISE-J0301, lack of similar MIR variability, radio variability, and black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: While the Mrk231 mid- and far-infrared SED can be scaled to fit the observed photometry of WISE-J0301 extremely well, our extracted WISE photometry of Mrk231 does not show long-term MIR variability like WISE-J0301 with a W1 and W2 brightness of 10.1 and 9.5 mags (AB), respectively. Mrk231 does show extreme variability at radio frequencies ( Mrk231_radio_2013 ) with a range in 20 GHz flux densities of 75-275 mJy. We currently do not have any multi-epoch radio observations for WISE-J0301 to ascertain its radio variability.

The black hole at the center of AGN Mrk231 has an estimated mass of 2.3 10 ^ 8 M_ Leighly_2014 . As stated before, we estimate a scaling of 0.45 for AGN Mrk231 to fit the observed MIR luminosity of WISE-J0301 .

sources.summary: The paper presents Mrk 231 as a nearby BAL quasar with a powerful radio jet, tracking a historic 2017 flare with AMI, VLBA, and NuSTAR to estimate jet power.

quotes.quote: Mrk 231 is a nearby quasar (redshift of z = 0.042 ) that has all of the extreme properties of the quasar population in one object. It has a high-luminosity accretion flow, a radio jet that can rival the power of other well-known nearby extragalactic radio sources and a broad absorption line (BAL) high velocity outflow. All of this combined in a nearby object provides an excellent laboratory to study the interplay between these phenomena.
In November 2017, Mrk 231 reached a historic high flux density in terms of our 17.6 GHz to 22 GHz monitoring. This triggered our 4 epoch 8.4 GHz, 15.3 GHz, 22.2 GHz and 43.1 GHz VLBA observations.

sources.summary: The review presents Mrk 231 as a nearby wind-dominated quasar and gas-rich merger with AGN-driven outflows detected from the accretion disk to galactic scales.

quotes.quote: Mrk 231 is the nearest quasar known ( z = 0.0422; d 180 Mpc; 1 \ = 0.867 kpc) and arguably the best example of a ``wind-dominated'' fast-accreting ( L/L_ Edd 1 ) quasar where the signatures of the AGN-driven outflow are detected on virtually all spatial scales, from the AGN accretion disk to the CGM. As such, Mrk 231 offers a unique laboratory to study a phenomenon that might be more common in the early universe during the epoch of formation and fast growth of SMBHs. Mrk 231 is the end-product of a (locally) rare infrared luminous gas-rich galaxy merger which provided the right conditions to trigger both a luminous AGN and a powerful circumnuclear starburst M _* 140 M _ yr ^ 1 ; this starburst accounts for about 30 of the bolometric luminosity of the system; e.g., VEILLEUX2009b .

A slower ( 1500 km s ^ 1 ), galactic-scale ( 0.1--3 kpc) cool-gas outflow has been detected in Mrk 231 using multiple techniques (Fig. fig:mrk231 b-g): neutral-gas absorption line tracers Na I D: in HI RUPKE2002,RUPKE2011,RUPKE2017,MORGANTI2016 , molecular-gas FIR absorption line tracers FISCHER2010,STURM2011,GONZALEZ-ALFONSO2014,GONZALEZ-ALFONSO2017b , and molecular-gas mm-wave emission line tracers FERUGLIO2010,FERUGLIO2015,AALTO2012,AALTO2015c,CICONE2012,ALATALO2015a,LINDBERG2016 . The inferred cool-gas mass outflow rate ranges from 100--200 M _ yr ^ 1 Na I D tracer; RUPKE2017 to 500--1000 M _ yr ^ 1 OH and CO tracers; GONZALEZ-ALFONSO2017b,FERUGLIO2015 , and the corresponding kinetic energy rate of the cool outflow is 0.2 (Na I) and 1--3 of the AGN bolometric luminosity.

sources.summary: The paper includes Mrk 231 in its comparison sample, adopting black-hole, bolometric and X-ray wind measurements, and treating CO outflow estimates as representative of its large-scale outflow.

quotes.quote: Mrk 231 . As for PDS 456, M_ BH is derived using the 5100 luminosity and the \ FWHM from NardiniZubovas18 . We take L_ BOL from Veilleux+13 , and the X-ray wind velocity and N_ H,UFO from Feruglio+15 . The molecular (CO) outflow energetics come from Lutz+20 , who used data originally from Cicone+12 . A large-scale outflow is also observed in OH Gonzalez-Alfonso+17 , but with properties that are compatible with those of the wind detected in CO. Following NardiniZubovas18 , we assume the CO-based estimates as representative for the large-scale outflow.

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 231 as a polar-scattered Seyfert comparison with high-covering-factor partial absorbers, similar optical polarization, and no significant inner-region reprocessing emission.

quotes.quote: A similar covering factor was found in Mrk 231, another polar-scattered Sy1. For instance, piconcelli13 found that Mrk 231 shows two partial-covering absorbers with N_ H 10^ 22 and 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 . A very high covering factor of more than 0.9 and 0.8 are respectively found for these two absorbers piconcelli13 . Interestingly, smith04 found that the optical polarisation spectra of \ and Mrk 231 are very similar too: their continuum polarisation rises at shorter wavelengths and peaks at the red wing of their broad lines.
Besides, as demonstrated in this work and piconcelli13 , Mrk 231 and \ show no significant evidence of reprocessing emission from the innermost region, e.g. disc reflection (see Section relxill ).

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 231 as the sample's most luminous stage-D ULIRG merger, with multiphase outflows, BAL systems, and a hard X-ray AGN.

quotes.quote: IRAS F12540+5708 (Mrk 231): Mrk 231 (= UGC 8058) is the most luminous ULIRG (log L_ IR /L_ = 12.57 ) in our sample, classified as a stage-D merger with a single nucleus. Previous multiwavelength studies reveal the presence of multiphase outflows, that is, UFO e.g., Mizumoto2019 , ionized outflow e.g., Fluetsch2019,Fluetsch2021 , and strong molecular outflow with > 500 km s ^ 1 Sturm2011,Cicone2014 . Mrk 231 also shows spatially unresolved BAL systems in the optical and near-UV spectra Boksenberg1977,Smith1995,Gallagher2002,Gallagher2005,Rupke2002 . We identify Mrk 231 as the hard X-ray detected AGN. The source has been detected with Chandra e.g., Ptak2003,Grimes2004,Gallagher2002,Gallagher2005,Iwasawa2011 , XMM-Newton e.g., Franceschini2003,Turner2003,Braito2004 , and NuSTAR e.g., Teng2014,Feruglio2015 .

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 231 as a counterexample: its AGN-driven outflow has energy-driven rates, spherical radial structure, and a nuclear connection, so it is not fossil.

quotes.quote: Mrk 231 : this galaxy has a very thoroughly investigated AGN-driven outflow. Its energy and momentum rates agree very well with the simple energy-driven model with an implicit assumption that the AGN has been shining throughout the evolution of the outflow Cicone2012AA . The radial structure of the outflow shows spherical symmetry and a clear connection to the nucleus Feruglio2015AA . All of these properties agree with each other and show that the outflow is not a fossil. The low ionized mass fraction can be explained as a result of high gas density rather than prolonged cooling.

sources.summary: The paper compares Mrk 231 as an AGN whose circumnuclear disk has multiphase gas, broad absorption on arcsecond but not milliarcsecond scales, and star-formation-related synchrotron continuum.

quotes.quote: Mrk 231 is one of the very few objects for which the multi-phase structure of the CND, including the continuum emission from its star formation, has been studied in detail. In Mrk 231, broad \ absorption is also detected on arcsecond scales, but not on milliarcsecond scales. Based on this, suggested that the absorption comes from \ in the circum-nuclear disc which has a size of about 60 pc, instead of being caused by clouds in front of the radio jet. concluded that diffuse synchrotron emission is present in Mrk 231, which is most likely related to star formation activity in the CND. were able to confirm their scenario by directly detecting the \ absorption against the continuum emission from the star-forming CND by using the short baselines of the VLBA (including the phased-up VLA).

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 231 as a comparison object with CN detected in molecular outflows, enhanced CN/CO line wings, and higher CN bright/faint ratios suggesting optically thin CN.

quotes.quote: CN (1-0) was detected in molecular gas outflows in Mrk 231 Cicone2020 with the IRAM PdBI telescope, and in nearby galaxies with the IRAM 30 m telescope Henkel1998, Watanabe2014, Aladro2015 .

Cicone2020 measured total CN/CO line luminosity ratios in Mrk 231 and found they are enhanced by a factor of 3 in the line wings of the outflow. They attribute this enhancement in the CN/CO ratio in the outflow to stronger UV radiation fields present in the gas, perhaps from the formation of massive stars in the outflow.

CN opacities have also been measured in three U/LIRGs. observed Mrk 231 and found an integrated intensity ratio for (CN bright)/(CN faint) of 2 , but do not quote an optical depth. measure the CN (1-0) line in the molecular outflows of Mrk 231 and found a (CN bright)/(CN faint) intensity ratio of 1.83 .
