The measured (CN bright)/(CN faint) ratios in Mrk 231 are higher than the average values we find for the ULIRGs in our sample, suggesting this galaxy has more optically thin CN emission. used ALMA to observe CN (1-0) in NGC 1068 and found a (CN bright)/(CN faint) ratio of 1.7 and an optical depth of 0.7 .

sources.summary: The paper compares Mrk 231 as another quasar with outflowing absorption, describing slab-model densities, an ionization-front density jump, and host-ISM wind acceleration.

quotes.quote: The only other quasar with outflowing \ absorption that has been studied in detail is Mrk 231, which has \ outflowing at v 4000-5000 \ 2014ApJ...788..123L . 2014ApJ...788..123L find that a constant-pressure slab can explain the observed \ in Mrk 231 as arising in a region with density n 10^6 cm ^ 3 and temperature T 10^3 K. They find that a jump in density at the hydrogen ionization front is needed to simultaneously explain the observed columns of higher-ionization gas at higher velocities (traced by ) and lower-ionization gas such as \ at lower velocities. They interpret this finding as evidence for a high-velocity wind impacting, compressing, and accelerating preexisting dusty gas in the host galaxy ISM. In their models, the absorption is produced in a region of thickness 0.027 pc at a distance of 13-230 pc from the quasar.

sources.summary: The paper reports spectroscopy of Mkn 231, resolving a roughly 300 pc stellar cluster, finding strong CO dilution by hot dust, and modeling a warped central disk.

quotes.quote: Fig. fig:mkn231 summarises the spectroscopic results for Mkn 231 (see for a more complete analysis). The left hand panels show the spatial profile of the 1.62 \ CO 6-3 stellar absorption, which has been fit with a double Gaussian. The stellar cluster is therefore resolved over a scale of 300 pc.

A full analysis of the mass of the nuclear stellar cluster has not yet been been carried out, but based on these numbers it is already clear that the CO feature is hugely diluted by hot dust associated with the AGN (and perhaps also to some extent with the star cluster itself): even in the H-band the on-axis dilution is a factor 20. This model matches the observations well, and tends to confirm that there is indeed a warp in the central 100--150 pc of Mkn 231.

sources.summary: The paper describes Markarian 231 as a broad-absorption-line Seyfert/ULIRG with a circumnuclear starburst, strong silicate absorption, weak PAHs, and an unusual 8 micron emission excess.

quotes.quote: Markarian 231 is a Seyfert 1 because of the broad Balmer lines, but it is the AGN with the strongest optical signature of absorbing material, having very deep, blueshifted sodium absorption aw72 , and it was also one of the first ultraluminous infrared galaxies discovered rl72 . It is the low redshift prototype for a "broad absorption line" AGN, with extensive X-ray studies gal02 . It has a luminous circumnuclear starburst considered responsible for most of the ULIRG luminosity sol92 . The IRS spectrum in Figure 7 shows only weak PAH features and is dominated by very strong silicate absorption; there are no detectable emission lines in low resolution, although the fringing may obscure some lines. The long wavelength spectrum is steeper than for any of the AGN previously discussed, perhaps attributable to cooler dust associated with the starburst.

sources.summary: The paper presents Mrk 231 as a prototypical ULIRG/QSO with completed CO and HCN observations, finding diffuse warm low-J CO gas plus denser high-excitation gas that dominates its molecular mass.

quotes.quote: The prototypical ULIRG/QSO Mrk 231 is the first object in our sample for which the full set of line observations has been completed, allowing for a unique view into the global excitation conditions of its molecular gas. Analysis of the relative strengths of the CO J=1--0, 2--1, 3--2, 4--3, 6--5 and HCN J=1--0, 4--3 lines (and ^ 13 CO J=2--1 from the literature, ) finds the low-J CO lines dominated by diffuse ( n few 10^2 cm^ 3 ), and warm ( T_k 90-100 K ) gas, while the high-excitation CO J=6--5, 4--3 and the HCN transitions trace a denser ( (1-3) 10^4 cm^ 3 ) phase with somewhat lower temperatures ( T_k 50-70 K ). The latter dominates the total molecular gas mass in Mrk 231, and its total CO line cooling is similar to that of the CII line at 158 m (Figure 2).

The high-J CO and the HCN lines detected in Mrk 231 provide good constraints on radiative transfer models which then allow a good CO SLED to be constructed. Its comparison with objects at high redshifts shows that despite the high excitation of its dense molecular gas its CO SLED stands as rather average (Figure 3).

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 231 as a nearby ULIRG whose CO ladder needs diffuse and dense gas components, with dense gas dominating both molecular mass and cooling radiation.

quotes.quote: The first high-quality CO flux distribution for a ULIRG was presented by Papadopoulosetal07 for the nearby ULIRG Mrk 231 . These authors conclude that the CO ladder requires a two-component model: one component of fairly diffuse gas dominating the low- J lines and one component of dense gas dominating at J>4 . Remarkably, it was found that the dense component dominates the molecular gas mass as well as the total cooling radiation from Mrk 231 . The total cooling by this component approaches that by the [ ] line, implying a totally different thermal balance than in for instance the Milky Way.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 231 as a Seyfert 1 galaxy in the IRAS 1 Jy ULIRG sample whose infrared absorption features make it an additional interesting AKARI spectroscopy target.

quotes.quote: Finally, Mrk 231 is a galaxy optically classified as Seyfert 1, and is listed in the IRAS 1 Jy ULIRG sample kim98,vei99 . Our basic sample selection of ULIRGs excludes optically Seyfert 1 galaxies, because our primary scientific aim is to study obscured AGNs, and the presence of unobscured AGNs is obvious in optical Seyfert 1 galaxies. However, despite its optical Seyfert 1 classification, the infrared spectrum of Mrk 231 shows absorption features lah07,dar07,bra08 , making it an enigmatic object. Obtaining a high-quality AKARI IRC 2.5--5 m spectrum should help to determine the true nature of Mrk 231. Thus, Mrk 231 is placed in the category of "additional interesting sources", and is included in our observations.

sources.summary: The review presents Mrk 231 as an extreme infrared galaxy whose black hole likely powers high-excitation molecular emission; Herschel/SPIRE detections indicate X-ray-driven heating and chemistry in its nucleus.

quotes.quote: Even more extreme conditions are found in the galaxy Mrk 231, where a supermassive black hole is probably responsible for the high infrared luminosity. An FTS spectrum of the Mrk 231 nucleus, taken with SPIRE on Herschel, shows emission in high- J lines of CO and , in the \ 1115 GHz line, and in the \ 1_0-0_1 , 1_2-0_1 and 1_1-0_1 lines near 909, 972, and 1033 GHz vdwerf:mrk231 . While the ISO-LWS spectra of Mrk 231 and Arp 220 show hints of \ lines gonzalez:mrk231 , the \ detection is the first outside the Solar System, where Herzberg identified \ in cometary spectra herzberg:h2o+ . The high brightness of the CO lines in Mrk 231 arising from levels up to 460 K above ground, and the significant lower limits of 2 10 for the \ and \ abundances indicates that X-ray emission from the black hole vicinity is driving the heating and the chemistry in the nucleus of Mrk 231.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 231 as a Seyfert sample source with radio variability, a flat-spectrum radio core, and a jet-producing central engine inconsistent with starburst-only radio power.

quotes.quote: Mrk 348 and Mrk 231 show radio variability Lal02 . Mundelletal09 using VLA at 8.4 GHz have shown that five sources from their sample of 12 optically selected, early-type Seyfert galaxies show radio variability. In this regard, we have attempted to use data that were obtained as near in time as possible to ours, and with angular resolution as close as possible to ours when calculating spectral indices using our own data or data from the literature. Mrk 231 between 1.4 and 5.0 GHz, Mrk 348 between 1.4 and 8.4 GHz, MCG 8-11-11 between 5.0 and 15 GHz, and NGC 5929 between 1.4 and 15 GHz show flat spectrum ( 0.4) radio cores.
Ulvestadetal99B , on pc-scales, confirmed that Mrk 231 and also Mrk 348 are jet-producing central engine systems.
Note that SLL98B have argued that Mrk 231 cannot be powered by starburst alone.

sources.summary: The review describes Mrk 231 as a prototype AGN/starburst outflow system with Herschel OH and water absorption, broad CO wings, and a massive neutral gas ejection rate.

quotes.quote: Recently, more evidence has been obtained of gas outflows in AGN/starburst galaxies. Many examples of OH and H _2 O line absorptions have been detected with the Herschel satellite, a prototype being Mrk 231 (Fischer et al 2010). In the same object, wide wings ( 800 km/s) in the CO emission have been detected with the IRAM interferometer (Feruglio et al 2010). The ejected gas amounts to 700 M _ /yr, more than 3 times the star formation rate of 200 M _ /yr.

sources.summary: The review cites Mrk 231 as a low-redshift quasar/merger where AGN-driven outflows are mapped, with high velocity, high outflow rate, and LoBAL absorption.

quotes.quote: An important object where both the AGN and outflow are seen is the low redshift, z=0.04, quasar/merger Mrk 231. Rupke Veilleux (2011; Fig. 1) map a strong outflow in it with a velocity of 1100 and an outflow rate of 420 , several times greater than the star formation rate. The outflow power is about one per cent of the bolometric luminosity of the AGN (see also Ferruglio et al 2010; Fischer et al 2010). The optical/UV spectrum of Mrk231 shows that it is a low ionization BAL (LoBal) quasar with strong additional absorption (Smith et al 1995).

sources.summary: The paper presents Mrk 231 as a prototypical system where both a nuclear starburst and AGN drive a massive, dense molecular outflow energetic enough to affect the galaxy.

quotes.quote: In the prototypical Mrk 231 both a nuclear starburst and the AGN contribute to the gas outflow, of 700 M _ /yr (Feruglio et al. 2010). Some outflows have been resolved with a size of a few kpc, significant to impact the galaxy, and provide some quenching. The kinematic power is of the order of 2 10 ^ 44 erg/s, and the energy of the AGN is required. High density is traced in the outflows through HCN, HCO ^+ (Aalto et al. 2012).

sources.summary: The paper models Mrk 231 with quiescent, high-velocity, and extended OH components, finding gas outflowing above 1300 km/s and mass-flow rates broadly consistent with corrected CO measurements.

quotes.quote: Mrk 231 : Our 3-component model fit is consistent with the more detailed, 4-component model reported in GA14: we fit a ``quiescent'' component (QC) that accounts for the high-lying absorption at systemic velocities, a compact component with gas outflowing up to >1300 \ (HVC), and an extended component (EC) with intermediate velocities. The HVC and EC have associated M 400 and 250 yr ^ 1 , respectively. These values are roughly consistent with the inferred values from CO (2-1) by once their values are corrected to match our definition. Tentatively, we find in the current work that the QC is also expanding at 100 \ and with 450 yr ^ 1 , matching the velocity of peak absorption in the OH84 doublet though not that of the OH65 doublet.

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 08058/Mrk 231 as the sample’s most powerful infrared source, a known AGN, and a possible pre-elliptical merger-stage object.

quotes.quote: If we assume the gas fraction in the center of the sources is closer to 1 and neglect supernovae feedback, we observe 5 systems that are super-Eddington including Mrk 231 (UGC 08058), Arp 220, and CGCG 448-020. As noted earlier, Mrk 231 is known to host a strong AGN, and if this drives the IR luminosity then this Eddington calculation for a starburst disk does not apply.

UGC 08058 : This is the most powerful IR source in our sample. It is known to host an AGN and potentially represents the stage before becoming an elliptical galaxy according to the evolutionary model proposed by Sanders88 .

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 231 as an example where a 1 kpc molecular outflow, declining radial energetics, and high Eddington-rate accretion favor radiation trapping over luminosity decay.

quotes.quote: For instance, the molecular outflow in Mrk 231 has a size of 1 kpc Feruglio_et_2015 , and may have been launched a few million years ago.

In Mrk 231, the mass outflow rate, and to a lesser extent the kinetic power, are observed to decrease with increasing distance from the nucleus Feruglio_et_2015 . Such radially decreasing trends would be qualitatively consistent with the trapping of reprocessed radiation.

The radiation trapping scenario is particularly favoured for Mrk 231, as the source is currently accreting at a very high rate, close to the Eddington limit, hence the luminosity decay argument is unlikely to apply.

sources.summary: The paper compares Mrk 231 outflow parameters, finds its energy-transfer rate exceptionally exceeds unity, and cites alternate Chandra and NuSTAR UFO measurements.

quotes.quote: fer17 compared outflow parameters of three targets, IRAS F11119+3257, Mrk 231, APM 08279+5255, and argued that types of shock between two outflows are not unique among the three targets.

We can see that the kinetic energies in almost all the targets (except for Mrk 231) are lost between the two outflows, which supports the idea that the molecular outflow is an accumulation of the ambient gas swept by the shock fronts UFOs have created and that radiative cooling occurs before the outflow gas reaches the shock front. The energy-transfer rate of Mrk 231 exceptionally exceeds unity; this is probably because our data picked up weak UFOs.

fer15 analyzed the different data set of Mrk 231 (Chandra and NuSTAR observations), and derived the parameters of v_ UFO =2.0_ 0.2 ^ +0.3 10^4 km s ^ 1 , P _ UFO /(L_ AGN /)=0.2-1.6 , shown in the opened square point in figure fig:pv and fig:ev .

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 231 as a comparison ULIRG with parsec-to-kiloparsec radio emission and a large southern radio lobe that may be fueled by its AGN.

quotes.quote: One such study investigated the ULIRG Mrk 231. Radio emission on scales of parsecs to tens of kiloparsecs was detected in this object Baum1993a,Ulvestad1999 . Morganti2016 studied the large southern radio lobe of tens of kiloparsecs size and detected a plateau of emission near the nucleus and poorly collimated bridge structure a few kiloparsecs south of the nucleus (see their Fig. 6). They concluded that the radio lobe emission could either be due to fuelling of fresh electrons by the central AGN, or in situ acceleration of electrons, or both.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 08058 as a post-merger Seyfert 2 with parsec-scale double-lobed radio structure, modeled with two radio SED components and revised turnover behavior.

quotes.quote: UGC 08058: classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy Veilleux95 in a post-merger stage Pasparialis2021 , this is a well-known AGN having a double-lobed radio structure at parsec scales, revealed from VLBI imaging Ulvestad99 . Both our SED modeling and Clemens10 SED modeling fits this galaxy with a two-component model where Clemens10 fits find turnovers at 210 MHz and 3.9 GHz. Our best-fit radio SED covers more data at low frequency (54 MHz from LoLLS) end and the first component of our best-fit model is without a turnover. In contrast, the turnover frequency for the second component is smaller than given in Clemens10 .

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 231 as a type-1 AGN-dominated ULIRG and fits its optical-to-infrared SED with direct central-source visibility and constrained torus/starburst components.

quotes.quote: One of the three objects, Mrk 231, was chosen because it represents a type-1 AGN-dominated ULIRG, while NGC 1068 was chosen as a prototype type-2 AGN-dominated source. Mrk 231 This object is one of the most luminous ULIRGs in the local universe soifer84 , and also one of the most studied at all wavelength. To reproduce the observed optical-to-IR SED (shown in Fig. fig:Mrk 231 ) we need a direct view of the central power source.

For three well-known nearby active galaxies (Circinus, NGC 1068 and Mrk 231), the ISO mid-IR spectrum was also available, in which cases both the torus model and the starburst contributions were strongly constrained.

sources.summary: The paper presents Mrk 231 as a single-nucleus, very luminous local ULIRG/Seyfert 1 whose multiwavelength observations reveal a dominant AGN.

quotes.quote: Mrk 231 (z = 0.042) is a well studied, single nucleus ULIRG and is the most luminous object (L _ IR 10 ^ 12.5 L _ ) in the local universe. It is an optically known AGN classified as a Seyfert 1 galaxy vei99 , but it displays absorption features in the infrared spectra roc83,arm07 and X-rays bra04 possibly originating in the broad absorption line (BAL) clouds bok77 . The presence of a very luminous and probably energetically dominant AGN is revealed by various observations (e.g., Soifer et al. 2000; Imanishi Dudley 2000; Braito et al. 2004; Imanishi et al. 2006a).

sources.summary: The PCA decomposition identifies Mrk 231 as having hot dust, weak PAHs, strong mid-infrared continuum, and AGN dominance consistent with its Seyfert 1 classification.

quotes.quote: Mrk 231 has a large negative PC1 implying the presence of hot dust. It also has a large negative contribution from both PC2 and PC3 resulting in very weak PAHs and strong mid-infrared continuum. All of these suggest than it is AGN-dominated which is in agreement with its optically classification as Seyfert 1 (broad-line AGN).

sources.summary: The paper treats MRK 231 as a highly polarized, heavily reddened polar BALQSO example showing that polarization and reddening do not require equatorial BAL winds.

quotes.quote: Secondly, two of the most polarized BALQSOs, FIRST 1556+3517, bro97 , and MRK 231, smi95 , have polarizations 13 in the near UV and are now know to be polar objects gho07,rey09 . Furthermore, both of these polar sources are heavily reddened naj00,smi95 . Thus, these two well studied examples show that one can not use the enhanced polarization of BALQSOs and reddening of the continuum as an argument for equatorial BAL winds.

sources.summary: The simulation paper uses Mrk 231 as an extreme ULIRG comparison, quoting its molecular gas surface density and infrared flux and arguing infrared radiation could drive its dusty wind.

quotes.quote: Note that the velocities obtained by radiation-pressure-acceleration in Figure velocity1 are far below the observed velocities of cold clouds in nearby starbursts such as M82 ( Walter02 ; Leroy15 ), NGC 253 ( Bolatto13 ; Walter17 ), Mrk 231 ( Rupke11 ; Gonzalez14 ; Feruglio15 ), or other star-forming galaxies (e.g., Heckman00 ; Veilleux05 ; Rupke02, Rupke05a, Rupke05b, Rupke05c ; Martin05 ; Weiner09 ; Chen10 ; Erb12 ; Kornei13 ), which can reach hundreds or even thousands of km s ^ 1 .

Moreover, an extreme case is given by the ULIRG Mrk 231 (UGC 08058) with _ mol 1.7 10^ 5 M_ pc ^ 2 and F_ IR 2.6 10^ 14 L_ kpc ^ 2 , corresponding to f_ E,* 0.8 and _ * ^ max 2.3 10^ 2 . Although Mrk 231 has active galactic nucleus activities (e.g., Rupke13 ), these results suggest that infrared radiation alone could drive a powerful dusty wind in Mrk 231.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 231 as the sample’s most infrared-luminous ULIRG, but with a relatively low modeled AGN infrared fraction because far-infrared data are fit by dust emission.

quotes.quote: Mrk 231 : Mrk 231 is a ULIRG that is the most IR-luminous system in the sample. We measure the AGN contribution at 17 of the IR luminosity, the lowest value in the late-stage sample, lower than the value found by ros15 by almost a factor of 5, and lower than that from fri06 by almost a factor of 2. This low fraction is because the data at wavelengths greater than 60 \ are well fit by the dust model with no AGN contribution needed.

sources.summary: The paper compares Mrk 231 as a broad absorption line quasar with red continuum behavior, UV variability, and competing BSBH-notch versus reddened-AGN interpretations.

quotes.quote: Similarly, Mrk 231 also shows a strong broad \ but a weak broad \ and a red continuum. In addition, Mrk 231 is a broad absorption line quasar with UV variability Yang2018 . Yan2015 has proposed Mrk 231 as a candidate BSBH whose red SED is driven by a notch in circumbinary accretion disc, while Leighly2016 suggests that it is a reddened AGN.

sources.summary: The paper discusses Mrk 231 as a high-luminosity, highly accreting AGN whose ALMA HCN/HCO+ observations show mass outflow rates matching the dusty-wind model.

quotes.quote: On the higher-luminosity end, Aal15 presented ALMA HCN and HCO ^+ observations of Mrk 231. For their potentially highly accreting AGN ( _ Edd 0.5 ) with a luminosity L_ AGN 10^ 46 erg/s, an outflow rate of M _w 250 M_ /yr is estimated from eq. ( eq:mdotwind ). The authors report a mass outflow rate as 80-800 M_ /yr, consistent with the presented model of a dusty wind being driven off the pc-scale environment.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 231 as cold-absorbed, with a flat power-law continuum and column density near 10^22 cm^-2, while prior work suggests heavy absorption and reflection.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1218 and Mrk 231 favour the cold absorption scenario, resulting in column densities on the order of 10^ 21 \ cm^ 2 .

Mrk 231 This is a well studied source and, among the many publications, Br2004 argues that the resulting flat photon index suggests a heavily absorbed spectrum consisting of a scattered power law component and a reflected component, and even a reflection dominated scenario. We do not consider any reflection components, but our results are consistent with a continuum well fitted by a power law with a flat slope and a column density of the order of 10^ 22 cm^ 2 .

gmw_00000390.name: J1131-1231
gmw_00000390.overview: J1131-1231 is a strongly lensed active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0801.2698] and quasar at redshift z=0.658 [arXiv:1403.4973]. The background source is quadruply imaged by a foreground elliptical lens galaxy [arXiv:1403.4973] and features an extended, rotating carbon monoxide disk with ongoing star formation [arXiv:1701.07830].

sources.summary: The paper finds RX J1131 1231 has anomalous cusp-pair and distant-pair time delays, strengthening earlier claims that its lens contains significant substructure.

quotes.quote: Morgan_1131 report the time delays among the close images A, B, and C for the cusp lens 1131, along with an estimate of the time delay to the distant image D. They note that the time delays among the close images are much longer than expected for a smooth mass distribution, and suggest that the time delays indicate the presence of a massive 5 10^ 10 M_ clump near image A. Keeton-tdel highlight a second peculiarity of the 1131 time delays, namely that the minimum image B is observed to lead the minimum image C, whereas smooth models predict the reverse. They suggest that a population of clumps could reverse the temporal ordering of the two minimum images in a cusp lens.

We find that the time delays for the cusp pairs BA and CA are anomalous: accounting for 3 errorbars, the P-value for BA is strictly zero, while the pair CA falls just short of a strong anomaly (0.006 compared with the threshold of 0.005). This strengthens the conclusion by Morgan_1131 that the observed time delays are not at all consistent with lensing by a reasonable smooth mass distribution. Interestingly, we also find that the distant image pair BD has an (almost strongly) anomalous time-delay ratio, even though its scaled time delay is not anomalous. We note that this anomaly is caused by the observed value being smaller than most of the predicted values; this might be understood in terms of the BA time delay, which appears in the denominator of the BD time-delay ratio, being anomalously longer (in absolute value) than expected.

sources.summary: The paper uses lensing and X-ray reflection spectra of 1RXS J113151.6-123158 to infer a compact emitting region and a rapidly spinning supermassive black hole at z=0.658.

quotes.quote: 1RXS J113151.6-123158 (hereafter ) is a quadruply imaged quasar at redshift z=0.658 hosting a supermassive black hole ( M_ BH 2 10^8 ) gravitationally lensed by an elliptical galaxy at z=0.295 . X-ray and optical observations exhibited an intriguing flux-ratio variability between the lensed images, which was subsequently revealed to be due to significant gravitational micro-lensing by stars in the lensing galaxy . Taking advantage of gravitational micro-lensing techniques, augmented by substantial monitoring with the \ X-ray observatory, a tight limit of the order of 10 gravitational radii ( r_g = GM/^2 ) was placed on the maximum size of the X-ray emitting region in , indicative of a highly compact source of emission ( 3 billion km , or 20 AU ). The lensed nature of this quasar provides an excellent opportunity to study the innermost regions around a black hole at a cosmological distance (the look-back time for \ is 6.1 billion years), and to this effect \ and \ have to-date accumulated nearly 500 \ of data on .

In all cases, we obtain consistent estimates for the black hole spin, which imply \ hosts a rapidly rotating black hole (Extended Data Fig. 3 and Extended Data Tables 1,2). Finally, in order to optimise the S/N and obtain the best estimate of the spin parameter we fit the combined \ and \ data of \ simultaneously with the baseline-reflection model and find a=0.87^ +0.08 _ 0.15 Jc/GM^2 at the 3 level of confidence ( a>0.66 Jc/GM^2 at the 5 level; Figure 3). The tight constraint on the spin of the black hole in this gravitationally lensed quasar represents a robust measurement of black hole spin beyond our local universe. The compact nature of the X-ray corona returned by the relativistic reflection model used herein confirms the prior micro-lensing analysis , and hence moves the basic picture of X-ray emission in quasars away from large X-ray coronae that may blanket at least the inner disk, and more towards a compact emitting region in the very innermost parts of the accretion flow, consistent with models for the base of a jet .

sources.summary: The paper treats RXJ1131-1231 as a quadrupole strong-lens system, using HST imaging and source-reconstruction scales to study mass-sheet degeneracy and extract cosmographic information.

quotes.quote: The quadrupole lens system RXJ1131-1231 (Figure fig:original_images ) was discovered by and the redshift of the lens z_l = 0.295 and of the background quasar source z_s = 0.658 was determined spectroscopically by . The lens was modeled extensively by with single band images. We use the archival HST ACS WFC1 images in filter F814W and F555W (GO 9744; PI: Kochanek). The filter F814W was also used for lens modeling in , and . We make use of the MultiDrizzle product from the HST archive.

In this work we applied the newly developed source reconstruction technique of to the strong lens system RXJ1131-1231 to extract cosmographic information. We showed how different source reconstruction scales probe different regimes in the MST even when the lens model is not fully transformable through the MST. This work is built on the modeling and the data of and the systematics analysis of .

sources.summary: The paper simulates microlensing time-delay perturbations for RXJ 1131 1231, specifying its lens and source redshifts, black-hole mass, disk scale, magnification maps, and delay maps.

quotes.quote: We demonstrate the effects of microlensing on gravitational lens time delays using numerical simulations for two of the lensed quasars, RXJ 1131 1231 and HE 0435 1223, used by and to constrain H_ 0 . RXJ 1131 1231 is a four image lensed quasar with z_ s = 0.658 and z_ l = 0.295 Sluse2003 . Based on the H line width from , estimated the black hole mass to be (1.3 0.3) 10 ^ 8 M_ .

Based on Equation 5, the accretion disk sizes for RXJ 1131 1231 and HE 0435 1223 are R_0 = 7.34 10^ 14 cm (0.28 light days) and R_0 = 9.37 10^ 14 cm (0.36 light days), respectively, in the observed R band (6586 ) for an Eddington ratio of L/L_ E = 0.1 and a radiative efficiency of = 0.1. We created magnification maps for each lensed image using the ray-shooting method described in . The microlensing parameters, given in Table tab:microparam , correspond to a macro model with a stellar mass fraction of 0.2 relative to a pure de Vaucouleurs model Dai2010 . The magnification maps have dimensions of 8192 8192, an outer scale of 20 R_ e , and a pixel scale of 0.00244 R_ e , where R_ e is the Einstein radius at the source plane.

sources.summary: The paper describes RXJ1131 1231 as a low-redshift lensed source whose line-ratio anomalies constrain substructure and indicate microlensing of image C.

quotes.quote: RXJ1131 1231 is unique in its low redshift of a source image z_ S =0.658 lensed by an elliptical at z_ L =0.295 . The lens shows three roughly co-linear images, A, B, and, C, being characteristic of a cusp singularity, where A is brightest (Figure 2). The observed flux ratios, (B + C) / A 2.1 in the V band and 2.2 in the R band, deviate significantly from the rule (B + C) / A =1 .

Figure 2 shows the Kyoto 3DII spectra of RXJ1131 1231 for three bright images A, B, and C. It is interesting to remark that images B and C in [ OIII ] show nearly the same fluxes relative to image A, as expected from a smooth model at its cusp singularity. Thus, the absence of substructure lensing effects on this NLR [ OIII ] sets important limits on the mass of any substructures along the line of sight, as M_ E < 10^5 M _ . In contrast, the H line emission, which originates from the BLR, shows an anomaly in the flux ratio between images B and C, i.e., a factor two smaller C / B ratio than predicted by smooth-lens models.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes single-epoch spectra of the lensed quasar J1131-1231, finding microlensing of images A and C and using it to probe continuum and Balmer-line emitting regions.

quotes.quote: The second target, J1131-1231, is one of the nearest gravitationally lensed quasar. The lensing galaxy at z_l = 0.295 splits the light rays from the source at z_s = 0.658 into four macro-images: three bright images (A-B-C) separated on the sky by typically 1'' and a fainter component (D) located at 3.6'' from A (Sluse et al. 2003). We study microlensing between the three brightest images which also happen to have negligible differential time delays.

The analysis of the single-epoch spectra of images A-B-C of J1131-1231 has revealed microlensing de-amplification of the quasar images A and C. In these two cases, the continuum and the BELs are microlensed. However, a larger fraction of the BELs is microlensed in image C, indicating that the Einstein radius of the microlens is larger for that image. Microlensing of the BELs confirms that the size of the emission line region is anti-correlated with the FWHM of the corresponding line component.

sources.summary: The paper repeats the time-delay lens distance analysis for RXJ1131 1231, using its redshifts, image positions, effective radius, velocity dispersion, and time delays to infer distance and precision.

quotes.quote: RXJ1131 1231 In this section we repeat the same analysis as above, but with another well-studied strong lensing time-delay system, RXJ1131 1231, using data from refs. for the time delays and the redshifts, respectively, and from ref. for the other quantities. The data for this system are z_ L =& 0.295 z_ s =&0.658 _ G =&(4.411'',4.011'') _ A =& (2.383'',3.412'') _ D =& (5.494'',4.288'') R_ eff =&1.85'' ' =& 1.95 ^ +0.05 _ 0.04 _p^2 _ ap ^ 1/2 =& 323 20 km/s t_ AB =& 0.7 1.4 days t_ DB =& 91.4 1.5 days t_ AD =& t_ AB t_ DB = -90.7 2.1 days . We use the AD pair. Using these values, we find D_A(EL) = 813.33 Mpc , and D_A(EL) from the best-fit WMAP 7-year parameters is D_A(EL) = 876.5 Mpc .

The total uncertainty in D_A(EL) is S_ D_A = D_A 5.36 10^ 4 +1.53 10^ 2 +1.46 10^ 3 = 0.13 D_A. The velocity dispersion alone gives S_ D_A =0.12D_A . Therefore, we expect the existing data on these systems to yield D_A(EL) with 13-14 precision per object, assuming the isotropic velocity dispersion.

sources.summary: The review describes RX J1131 1231 as a monitored lensed quasar whose microlensed Fe K lines constrain emitting radii, ISCO, inclination, and black-hole spin.

quotes.quote: In Dai et al. 2010 and Chartas et al. 2009 we analyze the light-curves of z = 0.658 quasar RX J1131 1231. We find the X-ray and optical half-light radii to be 2.3 10 ^ 14  cm and 1.3 10 ^ 15  cm, respectively. These sizes correspond to 26 r_ g and 147 r_ g , respectively.

The spectral models used to fit the X-ray spectra of RX J1131 1231 consist of a power law with Galactic and neutral intrinsic absorption and Gaussian lines to model the iron lines. For producing the ^ 2 confidence contours the only model parameter that was frozen in the spectral fit was the Galactic column density of N_ H = 3.6 10 ^ 20 cm ^ 2 (Dickey Lockman 1990). We find that the iron line is detected in 58 out of the 152 spectra (38 epochs 4 images) at 90 confidence and in 18/152 spectra at 99 confidence. There is considerable shift of the energy of the iron line due to microlensing.

sources.summary: The review highlights RXJ1131 as one of the best-measured time-delay lenses, noting external shear modeling and blinded cosmological inference from its analysis.

quotes.quote: In the analysis of RXJ1131, Suy++13 also took into account the inferred external shear from the lens model when deriving the prior for _ ext , noting a significant improvement in precision (as well as a marked shift in the PDF centroid). Since these initial analyses, a number of improvements have been suggested and investigated.

In Figure fig:current-constraints we reproduce the current constraints on cosmological parameters, from the two best-measured systems, B1608 + 656 and RXJ1131 Suy++14 . When this figure was made, the available precision from just these two lenses was about the same as that from SDSS DR7 Baryonic Acoustic Oscillations PercivalEtal2010 or the ``Constitution'' set of Type Ia supernovae HickenEtal2009 .

One important feature of the cosmological parameter inference carried out in the RXJ1131 analysis of Suy++13 is that it was blinded. Following the simple methodology suggested in the blind Type Ia supernova analysis of Con++06 , all cosmological parameter PDFs were plotted with centroids offset to the origin until the team agreed (after notably lengthy discussions about systematic errors) to ``open the box,'' just before publication. Importantly, the authors agreed to publish the unblinded results, no matter what.

sources.summary: The paper describes RXJ1131-1231 as a quadruply imaged lensed quasar whose improved time-delay distance and lens assumptions agree with cosmic distance-duality tests.

quotes.quote: The RXJ1131-1231 system is a quadruply imaged quasar lensed with z_s=0.6304 and z_l=0.295 , discovered by Sluse et al. (2003). By using more flexible gravitational lens models with baryonic and dark matter components, Suyu et al. (2014) obtained time-delay distance to RXJ1131-1231 with a 6.6 total uncertainty. By combining the improved time-delay distance measurements with the WMAP9 and Planck posteriors in an open CDM model the curvature parameter was constrained to be _k = 0.00^ +0.01 _ 0.02 (68 .l.) .

We obtain to B1608+656 and RXJ1131-1231, respectively: _ obs =1.046 0.074 and _ obs =0.955 0.108 . In both methods, the error bars are obtained simply by the trivial error propagation technique. The results are given at 1 .l. and they are in full agreement with CDDR validity. Our results show that the spherical geometry and isotropic velocity dispersion hypotheses to B1608+656 and RXJ1131-1231 systems are completely reasonable .

sources.summary: The paper models RXS J1131 1231 as a strongly lensed quasar host with an extended rotating CO disk, substantial gas and stellar mass, ongoing star formation, and an assembling bulge.

quotes.quote: We report observations of and line emission towards the quadruply-lensed quasar RXS J1131 1231 at z = 0.654 obtained using the (PdBI) and the (CARMA). Our lens modeling shows that the asymmetry in the double-horned line profile is mainly a result of differential lensing, where the magnification factor varies from 3 to 9 across different kinematic components. The intrinsically symmetric line profile and a smooth source-plane velocity gradient suggest that the host galaxy is an extended rotating disk, with a CO size of R_ CO 6 kpc and a dynamical mass of M_ dyn 8 10 . We also find a secondary CO-emitting source near RXS J1131 1231 whose location is consistent with the optically-faint companion reported in previous studies. The lensing-corrected molecular gas masses are M_ gas (1.4 0.3) 10 and (2.0 0.1) 9 for RXS J1131 1231 and the companion, respectively.

We thus find no evidence for quenching of star formation in RXS J1131 1231. This agrees with our finding of an elevated M_ BH / M_ bulge ratio of > 0.27 0.11 0.08 compared to the local value, suggesting that the bulk of its black hole mass is largely in place while its stellar bulge is still assembling.

sources.summary: The paper applies a DRW-constrained microlensing analysis to the lensed quasar RX J1131 1231, finding that source-variability likelihoods do not significantly change disk-size estimates.

quotes.quote: We used the damped random walk (DRW) model for quasar variability to evaluate the likelihood of the source variability and applied the revised algorithm to a microlensing analysis of the lensed quasar RX J1131 1231.
We then applied the modified algorithm to RX J1131 1231, a z_s = 0.658 quasar lensed into four images by a z_l = 0.295 galaxy Sluse2003 .

We applied the modified algorithm to the lensed quasar RX J1131 1231 using the R band optical light curves from that span almost nine years. We estimated the physical size of the quasar disk R_S and the average stellar mass M_* for three cases: (i) without using the DRW model, (ii) using the DRW model and averaging over a range of values, and (iii) using the DRW model with a fixed = _ int value estimated from the observed light curves. We found that adding the DRW model has negligible effects on the physical parameters and that the results are consistent with those obtained without a DRW model. The likely explanation for the minimal effect is that an unlikely source model is produced only when the microlensing fits are already poor and so already contribute little statistical weight to the posterior probability distributions. At least for RX J1131 1231, adding the information on the likelihood of the source light curve does not significantly impact the estimate of the source size.

sources.summary: The paper reconstructs the lensed quasar host in RXS J1131-1231, finding an 8 kpc structured source with compact emission regions and strong lens constraints from extended images.

quotes.quote: We present a source and lens reconstruction for the optical Einstein ring gravitational lens system RXS J1131-1231. We resolve detail in the source, which is the host galaxy of a z=0.658 quasar, down to a resolution of 0.045 arc seconds (this is the size of the smallest conclusively resolved structures, rather than the pixel scale), using a Bayesian technique with a realistic model for the prior information. The source reconstruction reveals a substantial amount of complex structure in the host galaxy, which is 8 kpc in extent and contains several bright compact substructures, with the quasar source residing in one of these bright substructures.

Additionally, we recover the mass distribution of the lensing galaxy, assuming a simply-parameterised model, using information from both the quasar images and the extended images. This allows a direct comparison of the amount of information about the lens that is provided by the quasar images in comparison to the extended images. In this system, we find that the extended images provide significantly more information about the lens than the quasar images alone, especially if we do not include prior constraints on the central position of the lens.

sources.summary: J1131-1231 has single-epoch spectra showing broad-emission-line microlensing, host-galaxy contamination corrections, and robust line microlensing despite systematic uncertainties.

quotes.quote: (o) J1131-1231 ( z_l=0.295 , z_s=0.657 ): presented single epoch spectra of this system obtained in April 2003. They identified ML of the broad emission lines for images A and C and presented MmD of the and emission lines using the image pairs A-B and C-B . They also identified contamination of the spectra by flux from the host galaxy and empirically corrected for this effect.

In image C , only the core of the emission line is not microlensed while microlensing affects the broad component of the lines in image A . Because of the uncertainty on the host contamination, exact values of M and may be more prone to systematic uncertainties Because the narrow lines are resolved and possibly macro-magnified by a different amount than the broad lines, their use to set-up M might be another source of systematic errors. than in other systems. Nevertheless, the microlensing of the emission lines is a robust result which qualitatively remains even if M or are under/over-estimated by up to 40 .

sources.summary: The paper models RXJ 1131 1231 as a gravitationally lensed quasar whose X-ray microlensing line shifts can be explained by planet-mass objects in the foreground lensing galaxy.

quotes.quote: We show that a population of unbound planets between stars with masses ranging from Moon to Jupiter masses is needed to explain the frequent \ line energy shifts observed in the gravitationally lensed quasar RXJ 1131 1231 at a lens redshift of z=0.295 or 3.8 billion light-years away. We constrain the planet mass fraction to be larger than 0.0001 of the halo mass, which is equivalent to 2,000 objects ranging from Moon to Jupiter mass per main sequence star.

In this paper, we show that the lensing effects from planets can explain some of the observational data of the gravitational lens RXJ 1131 1231. Throughout the paper, we assume a flat cosmology with the cosmological parameters H_0 = 70 km s^ 1 Mpc^ 1 , _ m = 0.3 , and _ = 0.7 . \ is a quadruple lens systems with source and lens redshifts of z_s = 0.658 and z_l = 0.295 , respectively sluse03 . The central black hole mass is measured to be M_ 1131 = (1.3 0.3) 10^ 8 dai10 , and thus the hole has a gravitational radius r_g = GM/^2 = 1.9 10^ 13 cm.

sources.summary: The paper uses RXS J1131 1231 as a strong-lensing distance calibrator, incorporating its lens-galaxy velocity dispersion, effective radius, anisotropy modeling, and distance posterior.

quotes.quote: We show images of the two lensing systems, CLASS B1608+656 (hereafter B1608+656) and RXS J1131 1231 (hereafter RXJ1131 1231) in fig:lenses A-B, and schematics of the system configurations in fig:lenses C. The foreground galaxy that dominates the deflection is defined as the main lens, while the deflections caused by any other structure along the line of sight external to the lens is parameterized by the external convergence, _ ext . When the source brightness is variable, the arrival time difference between photons from different images (i.e. the time delay) can be measured. Physically, two effects contribute to the difference in photon arrival time: the projected gravitational potential of the enclosed mass, and the difference in geometric path lengths between images, which are summarized as the Fermat potential .

The velocity dispersion of RXJ1131 1231 is estimated via spectroscopy with a rectangular aperture of area 0.81'' 0.7'' , where the center of the aperture is placed at the center of the lens galaxy. The effective radius of the lens galaxy is R_ eff = 1.85'' ; thus, the half width of the aperture is 20 of the effective radius.

sources.summary: RXJ1131 is modeled as an extended lensed host with identifiable bulge and disk components; the authors fit two profiles and compare magnitudes to previous reconstructions.

quotes.quote: RXJ1131 A lens model of RXJ1131 based on ACS/F814W data has been presented by  Suyu2013 . The host galaxy of this system is lensed to an extended arc. A clear bulge and disk component can be identified. Thus, we describe the host galaxy with two \ profiles with index values n fixed to 1 and 4 , respectively, to mimic the light distribution of the disk and bulge.

The photometry of the RXJ1131 host galaxy is fitted with a set of 4 initial PSFs. The results are summarized in Table  tab:host_measure , with the best-fit result shown in Figure  fig:image_inference (b).

sources.summary: The paper treats J1131-1231 as a nearby gravitationally lensed AGN and uses image-dependent microlensing to probe compact broad-line and continuum regions.

quotes.quote: J1131-1231 is one of the nearest gravitationally lensed AGN. The lensing galaxy ( z_l = 0.295 ) splits the light rays from the source ( z_s = 0.66 ) into four macro-images: three bright images (A-B-C) separated on the sky by typically 1 and a fainter component (D) located at 3.6 from A (Sluse et al. SLU03 ). The present study focuses on the lensed images A-B-C.

For J1131-1231, a solar mass Einstein radius has a size similar to the usually assumed size of the BLR, meaning that regions as large as the BLR can be amplified by micro-lensing. Because micro-lensing occurs independently in each macro-lensed images, we can track for spectral differences between multiple images of a lensed quasar.

sources.summary: The thesis presents 1RXS J1131-1231 as an optical Einstein Ring with complex source structure whose extended images provide important constraints on the lens mass distribution.

quotes.quote: Unlensing HST Observations of the Einstein Ring 1RXS J1131-1231: A Bayesian Analysis Hofstadter's Law: It always takes longer than you expect, even when you take into account Hofstadter's Law. - Douglas Hofstadter A second optical Einstein Ring observed with the HST is the topic of this third paper. This system posed a number of technical challenges to the technique that was used on 0047-2808. The relatively large number of pixels in the image (121 121), complex source structure and presence of light from the lensing galaxy and the AGN of the source galaxy were all issues that made the inference more difficult than expected.

Application of our method to RXS J1131-1231 also produced some different conclusions to previous work, especially regarding the constraining power of the extended images compared to pointlike images. In this system, the large amount of substructure in the extended source is extremely valuable for constraining the lens mass distribution, besides being of interest in its own right.

sources.summary: The paper compares Dai et al.'s RX J1131-1231 microlensing result with its fiducial and observed-parameter predictions, finding a 26 percent stellar convergence contribution in both cases.

quotes.quote: Dai et al. (2010) carry out a joint X-ray and optical analysis of multi-epoch data for RX J1131-1231. They allow explicitly for errors in the model magnifications. Under their less conservative priors they find a stellar contribution to the convergence of 30 at 1.50 r_e . This is consistent with the prediction of 26 for our fiducial galaxy. Using instead the observed effective radius of 5.24 kpc and a proxy dispersion of 349 km/s, we find again a 26 prediction for the stellar component.

sources.summary: The paper describes RXJ1131-1231 as a galaxy-scale strong lens, giving its discovery context, lens or source properties, image configuration, and relevant environmental or model information for the survey.

quotes.quote: RXJ1131-1231 (herein RXJ1131) is a four-image lens with an Einstein ring discovered serendipitously by sluse . The source and lens redshifts are z_s=0.658 0.001 and z_l=0.295 0.002 . The lens galaxy has a characteristic early-type spectrum. RXJ1131 has anomalous flux ratios by a factor of two in the optical and three to nine in the X-ray blackburn , possibly explained by substructure morgan2006 . RXJ1131 is also a time delay lens with measured delays of t_ AB =0.7 1.4 days, t_ CB =-0.4 2.0 days, and t_ DA =91.4 1.5 days morgan2006,tewes13 .

sources.summary: The paper applies basis-set lens modeling to RXJ1131-1231, using HST data to model its lens, background quasar source, main lens galaxy, companion galaxy, and possible substructure.

quotes.quote: We apply our method to a HST image of the strong lens system RXJ1131-1231 and show that our method finds a reliable solution and is able to detect substructure in the lens and source planes simultaneously.

In the following, we test our method on the gravitational lens RXJ1131-1231. This lens was discovered by and the redshift of the lens z_l = 0.295 and of the background quasar source z_s = 0.658 was determined spectroscopically.

For the lens light we follow and use two elliptical Sersic profiles with common centroids and position angles to describe the main lens galaxy and a circular S ersic profile with n_ sersic = 1 for the small companion galaxy. Figure fig:RXJ1131 shows our result of the fitting process to the HST image.

sources.summary: The paper uses RXJ1131-1231 as the long-time-delay quadruply lensed quasar example, finding microlensing time-delay effects negligible for its distance inference.

quotes.quote: RXJ1131-1231 The system is a quadruply-lensed quasar discovered by SluseEtal03 , who also measured the spectroscopic redshifts of lensing galaxy and the background source to be at =0.295 and =0.658 . Because of the long time delays ( 90.5 days) of this lens system, TewesEtal13a can measure the time delay of image D , with a fractional uncertainty of 1.5 ( 1 ) while the delays between the three close images A , B , and C are compatible with being 0 days (i.e., _ BA =0.5 1.5 days, _ CA =-0.5 1.5 days, _ DA =90.5 1.5 days).

We show the constraint on in different accretion disc configurations in fig:rxj and the marginalized posteriors of the microlensing time delays in tab:rxj_micro . As expected, the microlensing time-delay effect on the lens with longer time delays has less impact. In the case of , the impact by microlensing time-delay effect is negligible.

sources.summary: The paper models RXJ1131-1231 as a quad lens with long time delays, extensive cosmographic modeling, a satellite inside the Einstein radius, and substructure constraints.

quotes.quote: RXJ1131-1231 This quad was discovered by Sluse++03 , and consists of source at z = 0.658 lensed by a galaxy at z = 0.295 Sluse++03,Sluse++07 . The time delays are measured at 1.6 precision by Tewes++13 , and exhibit a relatively long maximum delay of 90 days. This system has been modeled extensively with the aim of constraining cosmology Suyu++13,Suyu++14,Birrer++16,Chen++16,Chen++18 . The lens mass model includes a satellite galaxy inside the Einstein radius. Perturbations from substructure in the lensed arc of this system were analyzed by Birrer++17 , and were used to place a lower limit on the mass of a thermal relic dark matter particle of 2.2 keV .

sources.summary: The paper includes RXJ1131-1231 as a TDCOSMO lens with discovery, HST and Keck AO imaging work, time-delay measurement, and a 323 20 km/s Keck-LRIS velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: RXJ1131-1231: The discovery is presented by and . The imaging modeling is presented by (for HST ) and (for Keck Adaptive Optics data). An independent analysis of the HST data was performed by . The time-delay measurement is presented by . The velocity dispersion measurement of 323 20 km/s presented by is based on Keck-LRIS spectroscopy and includes systematics.

sources.summary: The paper uses optical flux-ratio measurements of 1RXS J1131 1231 to infer a strong anomaly that is insensitive to simulation input choices.

quotes.quote: 1RXS J1131 1231 Sluse et al. \ (2003) present three measurements of the optical flux ratios of 1RXS J1131 1231. Observations from 2 May 2002 yield =0.350 0.021 in V-band, while observations from 18 December 2002 yield =0.353 0.031 in V-band and 0.367 0.031 in R-band. It is interesting that the total flux of the system varied by 0.29 0.04 mag between May and December, yet the flux ratios and values are essentially identical. The fact that is the same at different epochs and in different passbands indicates that there are no large systematic uncertainties due to the time delays or electromagnetic effects. The weighted average of the values is 0.355 0.015 . joint shows that this value lies well outside the predicted distributions, implying a strong flux ratio anomaly, and that this conclusion is insensitive to changes in the input data in the Monte Carlo simulations.

sources.summary: The paper cites the lensed quasar J1131-1231 as microlensing evidence for two BLR components, with broader emission arising from a more compact region.

quotes.quote: A piece of supportive evidence for the existence of IMLR comes from the study of the micro-lensing of the Broad Line Region in the lensed quasar J1131-1231 (Sluse et al. 2007, 2008). In this work they found evidence that the H emission line (as well as H ) is differentially microlensed, with the broadest component (FWHM 4000 km/s) being much more micro-lensed than the narrower component (FWHM 2000 km/s). The emission line can be well decomposed into two components and the emission line' profile has been significantly changed after microlensed compared to the original line. Because the amplitude of micro-lensing depends on the size of the emitting region, it can be naturally explained as the broadest component of the emission line comes from a more compact region than that of the narrower component.

sources.summary: The paper treats RX J1131 1231 as a time-delay gravitational lens, using published all-pair time delays and including it among systems modeled with PixeLens.

quotes.quote: Recently, using time delay measurements from high-quality optical and radio light curves, deep and high-resolution imaging observations of the lensing galaxies and lensed AGN host galaxy, and the measurement of stellar velocity dispersion of the lens galaxy to perform detailed modeling, Suyu2013 report a H_0 of 75.2 ^ +4.4 _ 4.2 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 through the study of two gravitational lenses namely RX J1131 1231 and CLASS B1608+656.

- RX J1131 1231: Tewes2013b measured time delays between all pairs of light curves using three different numerical techniques. The time delay value listed in the table for each pair of light curves is for the technique that resulted in the smallest uncertainty.

To perform mass-modeling of the remaining ten systems -- HE 0435 1223, SBS 0909+532, RX J0911.4+0551, Q0957+561, SDSS J1004+4112, HE 1104 1805, RX J1131 1231, SBS 1520+530, CLASS B1608+656 and WFI J2033 4723 -- to infer H_0 , we used the publicly available PixeLens http://www.physik.uzh.ch/ psaha/lens/pixelens.php code Saha2004 , which builds an ensemble of pixellated mass maps compatible with the input data for a given system, which is comprised of the redshifts of the quasar and the lensing galaxy, the arrival-time order of the images, their astrometry relative to the center of the main lensing galaxy, and the known time delays between the images adjacent to each other in terms of arrival-time order.

sources.summary: RXJ1131 has prior ACS F814W observations and published lens models; its brighter host galaxy gives the smallest simulation bias across the lensed AGN sample.

quotes.quote: RXJ1131 RXJ1131 was observed with ACS through the F814W filter. A detailed lens model with \ has already been published by Suy++13,Suy++14 . The host galaxy is much brighter than in the other cases (see Table para_config ). Thus, the results of RXJ1131 have the smallest bias in the entire sample, as summarized in Fig. fig:bias_RXJ1131 .

sources.summary: The paper models RX J1131-1231 as the main strongly lensed quasar example, generating lensing and time-delay simulations to forecast Muztagh-Ata observing performance.

quotes.quote: We generate mock strongly lensed quasar systems and light curves with microlensing effects based on five known strongly lensed quasars, i.e. , RX J1131-1231, HE 0435-1223, PG 1115+080, WFI 2033-4723 and SDSS 1206+4332. In particular, RX J1131-1231 is generated with lens modeling in this work.

According to simulations, for RX J1131-like systems (wide variation in time delay between images) the TDSL measurement can be achieved with the precision about t=0.5 day with 4 seasons campaign length and 1 day cadence.
In this paper, we pick RX J1131-1231 as a working example to demonstrate the capability of Muztagh-Ata 1.93m telescope of measuring time delays.

sources.summary: RXJ1131-1231 is used as a cusp-caustic quasar lens with three bright images and one faint image, modeled with [OIII] flux ratios from its lensed images.

quotes.quote: The cusp-caustic lens RXJ1131-1231 consists of three bright images A, B, C, and a faint image D. The quasar at z_S=1.72 lensed by a foreground galaxy at z_L=0.31 sluse2003 . We use the [ OIII ] flux ratios of images A (saddle), B (minimum) and C (minimum) (A/B and C/B) measured by sugai2007 .

gmw_00000391.overview: The source 1045+352 is a compact, radio-loud broad absorption line quasar at a redshift of z=1.604 [arXiv:1106.5916, arXiv:astro-ph/0612490]. It features a complex, multi-directional radio jet that exhibits at least three distinct phases of activity, likely caused by accretion disk precession [arXiv:1010.2762, arXiv:1212.6499].

sources.summary: The paper summarizes 1045+352 as a compact radio-loud BAL quasar/CSS source whose weak X-rays and complex jet morphology suggest absorption, precession, and jet-ISM interaction.

quotes.quote: BAL quasar 1045+352 - summary 1045+352 is a compact radio-loud CSS source. The spectral observations have shown 1045+352 to be a quasar with a redshift of z=1.604 . It has also been classified as a HiBALQSO based upon the observed very broad C IV absorption, and it is a very luminous submillimetre object with detections at both 850 m and 450 m . The radio luminosity of 1045+352 at 1.4 GHz is high (log L_ 1.4 GHz =28.25 W Hz^ 1 ), making this source one of the most radio-luminous BAL quasars.

The Chandra X-ray observation of 1045+352 show that the X-ray emission of 1045+352 is very weak in comparison to the other radio-loud BAL quasars which, together with the high value of the optical X-ray index _ ox =1.88 , suggests a presence of an X-ray absorber close/in the BLR region. The result of the SED modeling of 1045+352 indicates the X-ray emission we observe from 1045+352 can be mostly due to X-ray emission from the relativistic jet, while the X-ray emission from the corona is absorbed in a large part.

The radio morphology of 1045+352 is dominated by the strong radio jet resolved into many sub-components and changing the orientation during propagation in the central regions of the host galaxy. The presence of the dense environment and material outflows can have a significant impact on the radio jet morphology of 1045+352.

sources.summary: The paper reports 6C 1045+3513 as a compact, reddened BAL quasar, detected at 850 and 450 m, with high far-infrared luminosity and broad C IV absorption.

quotes.quote: 6C 1045+3513 6C 1045+3513 is detected at both 850 and 450 m, with a submillimetre spectral index of 1.3 0.7 . On the basis of the 850 m observations we find L_ FIR > 2 10^ 13 L_ .

In the Appendix we present new optical spectroscopy of this object, showing it to be a reddened quasar, similar to 3C 318. The spectrum also shows a CIV broad absorption feature. 6C 1045+3513 is the smallest radio source of the entire sample of quasars and radio galaxies with a projected linear size of just 1.7 kpc.

There is very high equivalent width C IV absorption on the blue wing of the emission line. This feature is very broad with a width of at least 6000 km s ^ 1 . There is no corresponding broad absorption of Mg II , so 6C 1045+3513 is classified as a high-ionization broad absorption line (BAL) quasar.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1045+352 as a young CSS HiBAL quasar whose complex, misaligned radio jet is better explained by disk precession than by activity cycles alone.

quotes.quote: 1045+352 is a CSS object and a HiBAL quasar at a medium redshift. Its linear size ( 4 kpc) indcate it is a young object in the early phase of quasar evolution. The radio morphology of 1045+352 is dominated by the strong radio jet resolved into many sub-components and changing the orientation during propagation in the central regions of the host galaxy. As a consequence we observe at least three phases of jet activity indicate different directions of the jet outflow: components A_ 2 A_ 3 as the oldest one, structure A_ 1 B as the younger one, and the jet A as the current activity direction (Fig.1).

The results of the applied accretion disk instability model ( ) is not in agreement with the 1045+352 structure and size: (a) the calculated duration of the activity phase of the quasar is too short to enable the source grow to the observed size, (b) it cannot reproduce misalignment between the young and old radio structures. Such misalignment could be the result of the changed direction of the jet axis between the activity episodes - precession. In the case of 1045+352 we considered the precession of the innermost accretion disk due to internal instabilities within the accretion flow ( ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1045+352 as a z=1.604 HiBAL quasar with SDSS coordinates; MERLIN/VLBA maps show multi-direction extended radio structure, a steep-spectrum core, and asymmetric jet emission.

quotes.quote: According to SDSS/DR5, 1045+352 is a galaxy at RA= 10^ h 48^ m 34 242 , Dec= +34^ o 57 24 95 , which is marked with a cross in the MERLIN map (Fig. 1045+352_maps ). However, the spectral observations have shown 1045+352 to be a quasar with a redshift of z=1.604 . It has also been classified as a HiBAL QSO based upon the observed very broad C IV absorption, and is a very luminous submillimetre object with detections at both 850 m and 450 m .

Our MERLIN and VLBA maps of 1045+352 (Fig. 1045+352_maps ) show this source to be extended in both NE/SW and NW/SE directions. The central compact feature with a steep spectrum visible in all the maps is probably a radio core. The VLBA image at 1.7 GHz shows radio structure extended in a NE/SW direction, with the SW jet being weaker than that in the NE. The NE/SW emission is visible in the 5-GHz MERLIN image as a few compact features.

sources.summary: The paper presents BAL quasar 1045+352 as having complex morphology from intermittent activity, with a strong changing-orientation radio jet and at least three jet-activity phases.

quotes.quote: An intermittent activity scenario can explain also the complex morphology of broad absorption line (BAL) quasar 1045+352 . The radio structure of 1045+352 is dominated by the strong radio jet resolved into many sub-components and changing the orientation during propagation in the central regions of the host galaxy. As a consequence, we observe at least three phases of jet activity indicate different directions of the jet outflow. The source image is shown in Figure fig:2a , where the radio structures labelled by C, A, A_ 1 , A_ 2 and B are the radio core and the parts of the jet, respectively. We suggest that the current activity direction is the jet A emerging from the core to the east, while the other components probably originated from the past activity episodes.

sources.summary: The paper describes J104834.24+345724.9 as 4C +35.23, a compact steep-spectrum radio-loud quasar with a C IV BAL, strong radio loudness, and hard Chandra X-ray spectrum.

quotes.quote: J104834.24+345724.9 (also 4C +35.23) is a CSS RLQ with a C IV BAL identified by Willott et al. (2002). It is radio luminous, and even after correcting for some intrinsic reddening it remains notably radio-loud (Kunert-Bajraszewska et al. 2007). It was targeted by Chandra in a 5 ks ACIS-S observation (ObsID 9320; PI Kunert-Bajraszewska) and is detected with a hard X-ray spectral shape.

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1048 + 3457 as a likely BAL QSO whose subarcsecond radio structure may be core-jet, CSO-like, or shaped by interaction with dense ISM.

quotes.quote: J1048 + 3457 is a likely C IV broad-absorption-lines (BAL) QSO at z=1.6 willott:2003 . Without spectral information for the VLBA components, we cannot distinguish a core-jet structure from a CSO type structure in this source. The multi-component sub-arcsec scale structure might indicate interactions with a dense ISM at an early stage of its radio activity kunert-bajraszewska:2007, kunert-bajraszewska:2010 .

gmw_00000392.name: Leo V
gmw_00000392.overview: Leo V is an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy located in the Local Group [arXiv:0902.3003, arXiv:1708.00009] at a distance of approximately 169 to 180 kpc [arXiv:1802.01581, arXiv:2402.07976]. It features an old, metal-poor stellar population [arXiv:1708.00009] and may share a common group origin with the nearby dwarf galaxy Leo IV [arXiv:0911.5352, arXiv:2404.16110].

sources.summary: The paper presents spectroscopy of Leo V, identifying seven likely members, estimating its velocity dispersion, mass and metallicity, and arguing that outer members indicate mass loss despite uncertain dark matter content.

quotes.quote: From stellar spectra obtained with the MMT/Hectochelle spectrograph we identify seven likely members of Leo V. Five cluster near the Leo V center ( R < 3 ) and have velocity dispersion 2.4_ 1.4 ^ +2.4 km s ^ 1 . The other two likely members lie near each other but far from the center ( R 13 700 pc) and inflate the global velocity dispersion to 3.7_ 1.4 ^ +2.3 km s ^ 1 . Assuming the five central members are bound, we obtain a dynamical mass of M= 3.3_ 2.5 ^ +9.1 10^5M_ ( M/L_V= 75_ 58 ^ +230 [ M/L_V ] _ ). From the stacked spectrum of the five central members we estimate a mean metallicity of [ Fe/H ] = -2.0 0.2 dex.

Thus with respect to dwarf spheroidals of similar luminosity, Leo V is slightly less massive and slightly more metal-rich. Since we resolve the central velocity dispersion only marginally, we do not rule out the possibility that Leo V is a diffuse star cluster devoid of dark matter. The wide separation of its two outer members implies Leo V is losing mass; however, its large distance ( D 180 kpc) is difficult to reconcile with MW tidal stripping unless the orbit is very radial.

sources.summary: The paper treats Leo V as one of two Milky Way dwarf galaxies whose apparent stellar bridge is reanalyzed; deeper Subaru data yield structural parameters and suggest the overdensity overlaps rather than connects the satellites.

quotes.quote: Box 9513, 2300 RA Leiden, The Netherlands ^5 Institute of Astronomy, University of Cambridge, Madingley Road, Cambridge CB3 0HA, UK We present a revised analysis of a speculated stellar bridge between the Milky Way dwarf galaxies Leo IV and Leo V. Using data acquired with Subaru/Suprime-Cam over a 1 ^ 4 ^ field encompassing the two satellites and the region in between, we confirm our previous detection of a stellar overdensity between Leo IV and Leo V 2010ApJ...710.1664D . The larger area coverage and improved depth of our current dataset allow for an improved analysis of the stellar overdensity that had previously appeared to bridge the two galaxies.

The constellation that boasts the largest number of Milky Way satellites is the realm of Leo, and two of its five dwarf-galaxy inhabitants form the subject of our current study. Discovered by , Leo IV and Leo V are close neighbours not just in projection --- separated on the sky by a mere 3 ^ -- but also spatially, sharing residence of the Galactic halo beyond 150 kpc. Targeted observations for studying these Milky Way satellites and their surrounding environment by using the LAICA imager on the 3.5m telescope at the Calar Alto observatory led to the unexpected detection of an overdensity of stars, seemingly bridging the two galaxies. However, the loss of one of three planned fields due to bad weather unfortunately left us with only a tentative detection of the possible stellar bridge, whose true nature unfortunately could not be resolved from these data alone. Follow-up observations using Subaru/Suprime-Cam aimed at obtaining a wider and deeper dataset has enabled us to shed clearer light on the nature of this stellar overdensity, as well as allowing for an improved set of structural parameters to be derived for Leo IV and Leo V.

sources.summary: The paper reports the first RR Lyrae stars in Leo V, deriving a 173 kpc distance and describing the ultra-faint galaxy as old, metal-poor, and possibly tidally disturbed.

quotes.quote: The location of these stars agrees with them belonging to the Leo V ultra-faint satellite galaxy, for which no variable stars have been reported to date. The heliocentric distance derived for Leo V based on these stars is 173 5 kpc. The pulsational properties (amplitudes and periods) of these stars locate them within the locus of the Oosterhoff II group, similar to most other ultra-faint galaxies with known RR Lyrae stars.

Leo V was discovered in SDSS data by belokurov08a . It is a faint system, M_V=-4.4 Sand2012 , composed of an old, metal-poor ( [ Fe/H ] =-2.48 ; Collins2016 ) stellar population. Based on the observed horizontal branch (HB), the estimates of the distance to Leo V have been set between 175 to 195 kpc belokurov08a,deJong2010,Sand2012 . Its closeness with Leo IV in both location in the sky and radial velocity has suggested a possible common origin for both galaxies belokurov08a,deJong2010,Blana2012 . It has also been suggested that the galaxy is undergoing tidal disruption belokurov08a,deJong2010,Collins2016 although Sand2012 did not find evidence for extra-tidal features in their data.

sources.summary: The paper reassesses Leo V disruption claims, finding the candidate stream likely foreground/background contamination, weakened velocity-gradient evidence, an updated proper motion, and only plausible distant members remaining.

quotes.quote: The ultra-faint dwarf galaxy Leo V has shown both photometric overdensities and kinematic members at large radii, along with a tentative kinematic gradient, suggesting that it may have undergone a close encounter with the Milky Way. We investigate these signs of disruption through a combination of i) high-precision photometry obtained with the Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ), ii) two epochs of stellar spectra obtained with the Hectochelle Spectrograph on the MMT, and iii) measurements from the Gaia mission. Using the HST data, we examine one of the reported stream-like overdensities at large radii, and conclude that it is not a true stellar stream, but instead a clump of foreground stars and background galaxies. Our spectroscopic analysis shows that one known member star is likely a binary, and challenges the membership status of three others, including two distant candidates that had formerly provided evidence for overall stellar mass loss.

Taking an error weighted average of these six stars, we find ( _ , _ )= (0.009 0.560 , 0.777 0.314) mas yr ^ 1 . This proper motion is very close to the one reported by Fritz2018 . In a search for Leo V members using Gaia DR2, we find two new plausible candidates (without spectroscopy) with consistent colors and proper motions (see Table tab:maybemembers ). Interestingly, both candidates are located towards LeoV-99, and if confirmed would imply tidal disruption.

sources.summary: The paper observes Leo V with WINERED as a dwarf spheroidal dark-matter target, using sky-subtracted near-infrared spectra to set stringent limits on eV dark-matter line-photon decay.

quotes.quote: Applying this concept, we have performed spectrographic observations of dwarf spheroidal galaxies (dSphs) Leo V and Tucana II using WINERED by utilizing an object-sky-object nodding observation technique for background subtraction. We present the first result from this dark matter search. Employing zero consistent flux data after the sky subtraction, we have established one of the most stringent limits to date on dark matter decaying into line photons in the mass range of 1.8-2.7 eV. Our data can also be applied to constrain other spectra of photons from the dSphs.

Observations were conducted using the WINERED attached to the 6.5m Magellan Clay telescope on July 6th and November 2nd in 2023. We used the WIDE mode with a 0.29 arcsec slit that allows us to cover the wavelength region of = 0.92 -1.35 m with a spectral resolution of R=28,000 . The observation targets were Leo V (on 6th, Jul, 2023) and Tucana II (on 2nd, Nov, 2023), which are dSphs located at a distance of 178 kpc and 57 kpc, respectively. The high-level emission originating from eV dark matter is expected due to the high central concentration of these targets ( _ max 0.07^ and 1.0^ ), see Ref. .

sources.summary: The paper discusses Leo V as a nearby satellite possibly associated with Leo IV, finds no confirmed bridge, and notes evidence that Leo V is disrupting and physically ambiguous.

quotes.quote: Recently, the MW satellite Leo V ( M_V 4.3 0.3 ) has been discovered, separated by only 2.8 degrees on the sky and 40 km/s from Leo IV leov . With a Leo V distance of 180 kpc, this close separation in phase space led leov to suggest that the Leo IV/Leo V system may be physically associated. This argument was bolstered by leovspec , who spectroscopically identified two possible Leo V members 13 arcminutes from the satellite's center (Leo V's r_ h is 0.8 arcminutes) along the line connecting Leo IV and Leo V, suggesting that Leo V is losing mass. A recent analysis by leoivleov of two 1 square degree fields situated between Leo IV and Leo V shows tentative evidence for a stellar 'bridge' between the two systems with a surface brightness of 32 mag arcsec ^ 2 .

We do not have the combination of image depth and area necessary to confirm the stellar bridge, with a surface brightness of 32 mag arcsec ^ 2 , recently reported in between Leo IV and Leo V leoivleov . Indeed, Leo V is almost certainly disrupting, as discussed by leovspec , due to the presence of two member stars 13 arcminutes ( >13 r_ h ) from Leo V's center along the line connecting the putative Leo IV/Leo V system. The nature of Leo V is still very ambiguous, with the kinematic data being consistent with it being dark matter free -- suggesting that perhaps Leo V is an evaporating star cluster leovspec .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Leo V with stellar spectra, measuring its velocity, metallicity spread, and velocity gradient, and argues it is a dwarf galaxy near dissolution.

quotes.quote: We present a chemodynamical analysis of the Leo V dwarf galaxy, based on Keck II DEIMOS spectra of 8 member stars. We find a systemic velocity for the system of v_r = 170.9^ + 2.1 _ 1.9 , and barely resolve a velocity dispersion for the system, with _ vr = 2.3^ +3.2 _ 1.6 , consistent with previous studies of Leo V. The poorly resolved dispersion means we are unable to adequately constrain the dark matter content of Leo V. We find an average metallicity for the dwarf of [ Fe/H ] = -2.48 0.21 , and measure a significant spread in the iron abundance of its member stars, with 3.1 [ Fe/H ] 1.9 dex, which cleanly identifies Leo V as a dwarf galaxy that has been able to self-enrich its stellar population through extended star formation. Owing to the tentative photometric evidence for tidal substructure around Leo V, we also investigate whether there is any evidence for tidal stripping or shocking of the system within its dynamics.

We measure a significant velocity gradient across the system, of d v d = -4.1^ +2.8 _ 2.6 per arcmin (or d v d = -71.9^ +50.8 _ 45.6 kpc ^ 1 ), which points almost directly toward the Galactic centre. We argue that Leo V is likely a dwarf on the brink of dissolution, having just barely survived a past encounter with the centre of the Milky Way.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Leo V members, measures metallicity properties, leaves its velocity dispersion unresolved after removing binaries, and finds only a weak gradient inconsistent with tidal disruption.

quotes.quote: Leo V We identify eleven member stars in Leo V, including four new members. Using this membership catalogue, we calculate a systematic velocity of 173.1 ^ +1.0 _ 0.8 km s ^ 1 , systematic metallicity of 2.29 ^ +0.14 _ 0.17 dex and metallicity dispersion of 0.30 ^ +0.14 _ 0.09 dex . We also provide further evidence that one Leo V member star (Leo5 1038) is a binary, as suggested by mut2020 , and identify a new possible binary star (Leo5 1034) . We do not resolve the velocity dispersion when the two binaries are excluded.

We identify a Leo V velocity gradient of 0.98 ^ +0.48 _ 0.50 km s ^ 1 per arcmin, 4 smaller than the gradient calculated by col2017 . The gradient is consistent with zero within 2 uncertainty and is likely caused by the small sample size. This indicates that Leo V is not tidally disrupted.

sources.summary: The paper measures Leo V proper motion with HST, discusses its pairing with Leo IV, and finds bound-orbit solutions compatible with Crater-Leo group infall despite large uncertainties.

quotes.quote: Properties Leo IV and Leo V were first proposed as a pair to explain both their spatial proximity ( <3 ) and small differences in their line-of-sight distances ( <20 kpc) and velocities ( <40 kms ^ 1 ) jong_enigmatic_2010 . Their closeness is especially unusual since the outer halo of the MW is mostly empty, with only 20 of all currently known dwarfs inside a 400 kpc radius located beyond 150 kpc from the Galactic centre Torrealba2016 . Following the discoveries of Crater 1 belokurov_atlas_2014 and Crater II Torrealba2016 , the possible association between these objects was further explored. Together with Leo II, these objects align along a common great circle (see left panel of Figure fig:positions ), which is highly unlikely. Torrealba2016 estimated the probability of this happening randomly - without sharing a common group origin - using random Monte Carlo realisations of satellite distributions on the sky and determining the largest group of satellites that are strongly aligned for each realisation. They find that having apparent aligned groups with five or more members by random chance has a probability of only 0.0045 . In addition, the objects show a monotonic distance gradient (see middle panel of Figure fig:positions ), moving up in declination from Crater II ( D_h = 116.6 6.6 kpc) to Leo II ( D_h = 216.8 11.0 kpc). To make this argument even more compelling, the line-of-sight velocities of these objects seem to prefer a common value of about zero in the Galactic rest frame The root mean square (RMS) scatter is 40 km/s, which can be compared to the larger one-dimensional velocity dispersion of 100 km/s for the MW satellite population as a whole riley_velocity_2019 . When we exclude Crater II, this RMS scatter becomes 25 km/s. (see right panel of Figure fig:positions ), with Crater II being the most offset in velocity.
