quotes.quote: Chandra observations revisited figure 1 ^ a see Sect. sec:specana With a size of 8 9 9 2 (corresponding to 10.0 kpc 10.4 kpc) the diffuse X-ray distribution of NGC 4449 is impressively large. Its morphology is similar to the \ distribution of NGC 4449 (see Fig. fig:olay4449 b ). To the west, however, the X-ray emission is more extended. We detect 18 point sources in NGC 4449 (see Fig. fig:point4449 a ).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4449 as a nearby starburst dwarf with extensive radio-continuum observations, a high SFR likely triggered by a lower-mass companion, and a low-frequency radio halo.

quotes.quote: Another such nearby template of a starburst dwarf galaxy, NGC 4449, has been observed in the radio continuum by 24.5 GHz klein86 , 4.9, 8.5 GHz 2000A&A...355..128C , 2.7, 4.9 GHz chyzy11 , 150, 325, 610 MHz 2014MNRAS.443..860S , and by 350 MHz purkayastha14 . This galaxy, too, is characterized by a high star formation rate (SFR), which was most likely triggered by the close passage of another - yet lower-mass - galaxy. NGC 4449 also possesses a low-frequency radio halo.

gmw_00000398.name: XID-392
gmw_00000398.overview: XID-392 is an isolated, nearly face-on barred spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.353 [arXiv:1505.01153]. The host galaxy is a massive, star-forming system that harbors a heavily obscured, luminous active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1505.01153].

sources.summary: The paper identifies XID-392 as a z=0.353 heavily obscured luminous AGN in an isolated barred spiral host, with massive star formation and multiwavelength Compton-thick diagnostics.

quotes.quote: The extreme properties of the source XMMUJ095910.4+020732 in the \ catalogue (Cappelluti et al. 2007) \ ID: 669, Elvis et al. (2009); optical RA=09:59:10.32, DEC=+02:07:32.3, z=0.353 , Brusa et al. (2010); Civano et al. (2012). , XID-392 hereafter, were serendipitously discovered by comparing the intrinsic (absorption-corrected) 2-10 keV luminosity ( ) obtained from the automated spectral fit of all the sources in the \ catalogue (Lanzuisi et al. 2015, L15 hereafter), to the bolometric luminosity ( ) computed by fitting the spectral energy distribution (SED) for the Herschel detected sources in the same field (Delvecchio et al. 2014, 2015). Figure 1 shows the distribution of the two quantities for all the sample of \ sources detected by Herschel . The \ of Fig. 1 (left) represents the total intrinsic luminosity of the accretion disk, derived from the AGN model of the best-fitting SED solution. The SED-fitting procedure uses a grid of torus models that were computed by solving the radiative transfer equation for a smooth dusty structure irradiated by the accretion disc emission (Fritz et al. 2006; Feltre et al. 2012). This \ does not include the X-ray emission above 1 keV, which is negligible in the total budget, however. The two quantities plotted in Fig. 1 are related by the X-ray bolometric correction k_ Bol ( = k_ Bol ). The blue and red curves in Fig. 1 represent the dependent k_ Bol relations derived in Lusso et al. (2012, L12 hereafter) for type 1 and type 2 AGN in the COSMOS field, respectively. Strikingly, source XID-392 is the only source in the sample (out of 394) that is more than two orders of magnitudes away from these relations, and 1.5 order of magnitudes away from the closest observed point for a given \ or . We verified that the source identification is correct and that the IR/optical and X-ray photometry is not contaminated by nearby sources. Figure 1 (right) shows the HST-ACS 20 20 cut-out of the source (Koekemoer et al. 2007). The host galaxy is an isolated barred spiral galaxy seen nearly face-on, and the closest source is at a distance of more than 7 , which means that the IR/optical photometry is not contaminated.

The galaxy is massive (Log( M_* )=11.4 ), with a derived star formation rate (SFR), after subtracting the AGN contribution in the IR, of SFR =22.2 \ yr ^ 1 . The host galaxy lies at the massive end of the main sequence (MS) of star-forming galaxies (e.g. Whitaker et al. 2012) at z=0.353, having a specific SFR (sSFR=SFR/ M_* ) of sSFR =9.3 10^ 2 Gyr ^ 1 .

gmw_00000399.name: NGC 922
gmw_00000399.overview: NGC 922 is a peculiar, barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2302.03112] and a collisional ring starburst [arXiv:1212.1124] undergoing recent star formation [arXiv:2010.11107]. The galaxy features an expanding ring of star formation [arXiv:1212.1124], complex tidal streams [arXiv:2401.02025], and a population of variable ultraluminous X-ray sources [arXiv:1212.1124].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 922 as a near-solar-metallicity collisional ring starburst with ongoing star formation and a ULX population consistent with its star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: In this paper we report on \ observations of the starburst galaxy NGC 922. NGC 922 is a drop-through ring galaxy with an expanding ring of star formation, similar in many respects to the Cartwheel galaxy. The Cartwheel galaxy is famous for hosting 12 ULX, most of which are in the star forming ring. This is the largest number of ULX seen in a single system, and has led to speculation that the low metallicity of the Cartwheel (0.3 ) may optimize the conditions for ULX formation. In contrast, NGC 922 has metallicity near solar.

NGC 922 has a population of bright X-ray point sources, including 9 ULX, defined by L _x >10^ 39 ergs s ^ 1 , in the 0.3-8.0 keV band (there are 7 ULX in the 2-10 keV band). Count rates for both the March and October observations are shown in Table data . Many of the sources are variable.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 922 as a peculiar SBcd host of two Type II supernovae, with tailored-EPM distances that are fully consistent within one sigma despite timing uncertainties.

quotes.quote: By filtering the catalogue, we found that SN IIP pairs in four host galaxies met all the conditions that we described above: NGC 772, NGC 922, NGC 4303 (M 61), and NGC 6946 (Fig. fig:hosts ). To retrieve the data for the supernovae in these hosts, we made use of the OSC and WISeREP https://www.wiserep.org/ wiserep .

NGC 922 The peculiar SBcd type galaxy NGC 922 (Fig. fig:hosts ) hosted two Type II supernovae six years apart: SN 2002gw and SN 2008ho. SN 2002gw was discovered on JD 2452560.8 2002gw . Although the latest non-detection occurred too far from the discovery to be useful for constraining the explosion date, the fitting of the early light curve (including the unfiltered CCD observations obtained by Itagaki2002 and 2002gw ) gave an estimate of JD 2452556.58^ +0.97 _ 1.40 (Fig. fig:2002gw-2008ho_lc ), which is consistent with the value obtained through performing the EPM regression in .

We suspect that the majority of the offset between the distances of the two SNe can be attributed to the relatively large uncertainties on the times of explosion. Nevertheless, the two distances are fully consistent within 1 .

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 922 metallicity and X-ray emission, finding ring-bulge metallicity differences, young star-forming regions, and an anti-correlation between X-ray luminosity and metallicity.

quotes.quote: We present a systematic study of the metallicity variations within the collisional ring galaxy NGC 922 based on long-slit optical spectroscopic observations. We find a metallicity difference between star-forming regions in the bulge and the ring, with metallicities ranging from almost solar to significantly sub-solar ( [ 12+ (O/H) ] 8.2 ). We detect He I emission in the bulge and the ring star-forming regions indicating ionization from massive stars associated with recent ( <10 Myr) star-formation, in agreement with the presence of very young star-clusters in all studied regions.

We find an anti-correlation between the X-ray luminosity and metallicity of the sub-galactic regions of NGC 922. The different regions have similar stellar population ages leaving metallicity as the main driver of the anti-correlation. The dependence of the X-ray emission of the different regions in NGC 922 on metallicity is in agreement with similar studies of the integrated X-ray output of galaxies and predictions from X-ray binary population models.

sources.summary: The paper simulates NGC 922 redshifted to z=0.7 and z=1.2, finding its near-infrared dust-penetrated morphology remains describable by low-order Fourier modes.

quotes.quote: In this contribution, we quantitatively determine the dust-penetrated structures of high redshift galaxies such as NGC 922 in their near-infrared restframes. We show that such optically peculiar objects may readily be classified using the dust penetrated z 0 templates of blockpuerari99 blockpuerari99 and butablock01 butablock01 .

In this contribution, the images of NGC 922 are simulated at redshifts z=0.7 and z=1.2 in the K ' restframe. The original image of NGC 922 was obtained at the NASA Infrared Telescope Facility with NSFCam (more details can be found in Block et al. blocketal01 ). The bidimensional Fourier analysis shows that the near-infrared morphology of NGC 922 remains the same when the galaxy is moved out to redshifts z=0.7 and z=1.2 (see Fig. 1). The analysis also confirms that only two low-order m components ( m=1, 2 ) are sufficient to describe the dust-penetrated morphology of NGC 922 (see Fig. 2).

sources.summary: The paper argues that NGC 922's ring is hard to form by a dry minor collision in MOND and may instead reflect assembly, gas accretion, or merger-related structures.

quotes.quote: For instance, the ring galaxy NGC 922 is in the process of assembly and the observed ``ring'' could actually be a Fourier m=1 mode component Block+2001 . Instead of a tidal origin, such a strongly lopsided component in disc galaxies is more likely to be induced by cosmological gas accretion on the disc plane Bournaud+2005 . The secular evolution-induced ring-like structure can exist for a longer time scale, compared to the collision-induced scenario. Another possible promising mechanism is that the rings form through other interactions, such as galaxy mergers. The observations by Elagali+2018 and Martinez-Delgado+2023 found complex tidal HI and stellar streams around NGC 922, which is not connected with the previously suggested companion dwarf galaxy Wong+2006 that induces the ring.

sources.summary: The paper applies the Q_b bar-torque method to NGC 922, finding its bar remains identifiable in simulated high-redshift K-band images and keeps bar class 2.

quotes.quote: We have applied our Q_b method to NGC 922, which may serve as an excellent morphological interface between the low and high redshift universe (see Block et al. 2001 for full details). The results are indicated in Fig. 9. Even in the presence of significant image degradation of NGC 922 when this galaxy is moved out to a redshift of z =1.2 (the eye now cannot easily distinguish the boundaries of a bar, see Fig. 9) -- the four locations where the ratio of the tangential force to the mean axisymmetric radial force reaches a maximum (in modulus) may readily be identified. NGC 922 in the groundbased as well as in the simulated image (Fig. 9) belongs to bar class 2.

gmw_00000400.name: NGC 3109
gmw_00000400.overview: NGC 3109 is a gas-rich, low-metallicity dwarf irregular galaxy located at the periphery of the Local Group [arXiv:astro-ph/0605243, arXiv:1812.07796]. Viewed nearly edge-on, it features a warped neutral hydrogen disk that suggests a past tidal interaction with the neighboring Antlia dwarf galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0605243, arXiv:1209.4179].

sources.summary: The paper studies nebulae across dwarf irregular NGC 3109, finding a chemically homogeneous ISM, enhanced oxygen in planetary nebulae, and evidence that low-metallicity PN progenitors dredged up oxygen and neon.

quotes.quote: We present deep spectrophotometry for a sample of 8 planetary nebulae (PNe) and 12 HII regions distributed throughout the dwarf irregular galaxy NGC 3109, in order to analyze the chemical composition of both types of nebulae. We describe the observations and data reduction, and present line intensities for the nebular emission lines detected. The physical conditions and the abundances of He, O, Ne, N, S and Ar are derived, using the classical T _ e based method. We confirm our previous identification of PNe and HII regions based on photometry, except for one object, which we argue is a compact HII region rather than a planetary nebula. We find that the chemical composition of the interstellar medium in NGC 3109, as sampled by its HII regions, is remarkably uniform.

The oxygen abundance is log O/H + 12 = 7.77 0.07 in this galaxy, as compared to 8.05 0.09 for the Small Magellanic Cloud (for which we rederived the metallicity in a homogeneous way). PNe show significantly higher oxygen abundances in NGC 3109: log O/H + 12 = 8.16 0.19. Similarly to what has been suggested for some of the PNe in the Magellanic Clouds and other metal-poor galaxies, we argue that oxygen in the PNe in NGC 3109 is affected by dredge up in their progenitors. This could also be the case for neon, although the uncertainties for this element are bigger.

Therefore, it seems so far that the best explanation for the difference in chemical composition in PNe and HII regions in NGC 3109 is that oxygen and neon have been synthesized and dredged-up during the evolution of the progenitors of these low metallicity PNe. To confirm this hypothesis, it would be fruitful to observe a larger sample of PNe over a larger wavelength range in order to derive more reliable S and Ar abundances. In addition, the icfs for these crucial elements need to be improved. The excitation patterns of the PNe in NGC 3109 and the SMC are very different.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3109 as a gas-rich dwarf irregular near the Local Group, with low metallicity, a warped H I disk, dark-matter-dominated rotation, and AGB populations.

quotes.quote: NGC 3109 is a small galaxy, classified as SB(s)m deVaucouleurs_1991 or dIrr McConnachie_2012 that is associated with a group of dwarf irregular galaxies including Sextans A, Sextans B, Antlia and possibly Leo P. NGC 3109 is outside the virial radius of the Local Group and is probably not bound to it, though it may have been near the Milky Way about 7 -- 9 Gyr ago Pawlowski_2014 . It was nevertheless included in our infrared search at SAAO for long period variables in the Local Group see e.g. Menzies_2015 .

The galaxy is gas-rich, with an extensive H i envelope Jobin_1990, Carignan_2013 . The H i is warped in the outer disk towards the neighbouring Antlia galaxy to the south-west suggesting an interaction in the past, though not at present Carignan_2013 ; the disk is seen almost edge-on with an inclination of 75 . The rotation curve is consistent with a dark-matter halo that dominates at all radii. Demers_2003 found more than 400 C stars in the galaxy, mostly confined to the plane of the disk, and there does not appear to be an extensive halo. They find a C/M ratio of 1.75, typical for a metal-poor galaxy such as this see e.g. Cioni_2009 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3109 as an edge-on dwarf irregular, resolves its stellar populations, selects halo stars, and measures a TRGB distance of 1.27 Mpc.

quotes.quote: NGC 3109 is another dwarf irregular galaxy, classified Sm IV, at the edge of the Local Group. It has a flattened disk region apparently viewed nearly edge-on. Our Keck observations have brilliantly resolved the galaxy into stars.

The red giant branch is now much more apparent as is its tip. The luminosity function of halo stars with 0.7 (V-I) 1.6 mag is shown in Fig.  figure:NGC3109_halo_ccut_lf . The two TRGB detection methods agree quite well and we find I_ TRGB = 21.63 0.05 mag for the maximum likelihood analysis, as shown in Fig.  figure:NGC3109_halo_ccut_like , and I_ TRGB = 21.60 0.08 mag for the logarithmic edge--detector. The best estimate of the TRGB color is (V-I)_ 0 , TRGB = 1.46 0.02 mag, with [ Fe/H ] = 1.69 0.06 dex. For the maximum likelihood method and using an extinction value of A_I = 0.129 mag we find the distance modulus (m-M)_ = 25.52 0.06 (internal) 0.18 (systematic) mag, which corresponds to a distance from the Sun of 1.27 0.04 Mpc.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3109 as a Magellanic dwarf whose carbon stars lie near its disk, indicating a truncated disk galaxy without an extensive intermediate-age stellar halo.

quotes.quote: We present a CFH12K wide field survey of the carbon star population in and around NGC 3109. Carbon stars, the brightest members of the intermediate-age population, were found nearly exclusively in and near the disk of NGC 3109, ruling out the existence of an extensive intermediate-age halo like the one found in NGC 6822. Over 400 carbon stars identified have M_I = -4.71 , confirming the nearly universality of mean magnitude of C star populations in Local Group galaxies.

Star counts over the field reveal that NGC 3109 is a truncated disk shaped galaxy without an extensive stellar halo. The minor axis star counts reach the foreground density between 4 ' and 5 ' , a distance that can be explained by an inclined disk rather than a spheroidal halo. We calculate a global C/M ratio of 1.75 0.20, a value expected for such a metal poor galaxy.

NGC 3109, a Magellanic dwarf on the outskirts of the Local Group, is a spectacular object seen through a large telescope. With a diameter of nearly 30 ' , NGC 3109 is one of the largest and brightest galaxy of the southern hemisphere.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 3109 as a nearby low-metallicity, dark-matter-dominated dwarf, using blue-supergiant spectroscopy to trace rotation and measure young-population oxygen abundances.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: optical centre of the galaxy =  10 ^ h 03 ^ m 06 6, =   26 ^ o 09 ' 32 '' , J2000.0

quotes.quote: We have obtained multi-object spectroscopy of luminous blue supergiants in NGC 3109, a galaxy at the periphery of the Local Group at 1.3 Mpc. We present a detailed catalog including finding charts, V and I magnitudes, spectral classifications, and stellar radial velocities. The radial velocities are seen to trace the rotation curves obtained from studies of the H  gas. From quantitative analysis of eight B-type supergiants we find a mean oxygen abundance of 12  +  log(O/H) = 7.76 0.07 (1-sigma systematic uncertainty), with a median result of 7.8. Given its distance, we highlight NGC 3109 as the ideal example of a low metallicity, dark-matter dominated, dwarf galaxy for observations with the next generation of ground-based extremely large telescopes (ELTs).

NGC 3109 is a Magellanic-like spiral galaxy of type SBm dv at the outer edge of the Local Group, at a distance of 1.3 Mpc cpb92,lee93,3109phot2 . Its relative proximity and low foreground reddening bh84 make it well-suited to exploring galaxy formation and evolution in dwarf irregulars beyond the Magellanic Clouds. Photometric studies have suggested that the main body of NGC 3109 has a low, SMC-like metal abundance ef85,g93 .

sources.summary: The paper adopts a Cepheid distance for NGC 3109, confirms it with infrared Cepheid work, uses I-band and ESO LV photometry, and notes the rotation-curve construction is complicated.

quotes.quote: NGC 3109 For this galaxy a Cepheid distance is available of 1.36 0.10 Mpc by Musella et al. (1997). These authors use the same distance to the LMC as the HST Cepheid distance key project and is in that sense comparable. Yet there may be small systematic differences between the two methods. Recently, Soszy nski et al. (2006) determined a distance of 1.30 0.04 Mpc by extending the Cepheid observations to the infra-red. This confirms the value of Musella et al. and the difference is so small that we maintained the value of 1.36 Mpc.

Uncalibrated I-band photometry has been obtained by Jobin Carignan (1990) of which the radial profile was used to calculate the rotation of the disc. A total R-band luminosity was derived from the ESO LV catalogue (Lauberts Valentijn 1989) by extrapolating the R-band aperture value at R _ 26 equal to the B-band functionality. The construction of the rotation curve is somewhat comp li cated.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3109 as a Local Group dwarf irregular affected by tidal interaction, and argues its observed rotation curve agrees well with Debye Gravitational Theory predictions.

quotes.quote: NGC 3109 dwarf galaxy NGC 3109 is a dwarf irregular galaxy, like the small Magellanic nebula. NGC 3109 is the most distant galaxy from the solar system, within the galaxies from the Local Group. NGC 3109 is located about 1.3 to 1.6 Mpc from the solar system, in the Hydra constellation. NGC 3109 is possibly the fifth largest galaxy in the local group, ranking behind the fourth largest, the Great Magellanic Cloud dwarf galaxy. In addition to suffering the influence of the Milky Way, NGC 3109 is not an isolated galaxy, is subjected to tidal effect, due to neighboring, the Antly dwarf elliptical galaxy, causing a warp in the disk of NGC 3109.

The solid red curve in Fig ngc_3109 shows the DGT prediction for the rotation curve of NGC 3109 and the black square points are from the VLA observations of . Except for the three first points at very small radii ( < 1 kpc), DGT rotation curve is in excellent agreement with the observed curve, and it is like a nonlinear fit on the data, but it is a theoretical prediction from DGT.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 3109 as a low-metallicity dwarf irregular whose planetary nebulae are oxygen- and neon-enriched relative to H II regions, possibly tracing central-bar populations.

quotes.quote: This limit is 12+log(O/H) 7.7 ( ). have recently shown that the PNe of NGC 3109 are not only enriched in O ( ), but they are also enriched in Ne. In this galaxy the average O abundance of the H ii regions is 12+log(O/H)=7.74, and significantly higher (by 0.43dex) in PNe. Ne/H are 3 higher in the PNe than in the H ii regions of NGC 3109. NGC 3109 is one of the two lowest Z galaxies included in Magrini Gon calves (2009) and in Figure f3du .

Perhaps somewhat surprisingly, Flores-Dur an et al. (2017) detailed model of another dIrr galaxy, NGC 3109, have made very clear that oxygen and neon are enriched in its PN population, as compared to in the H ii regions. So in this galaxy the 3du is stronger for Ne than for O. This behaviour is interpreted as an indication that younger and intermediate-age objects of NGC 3109 belong to different populations, probably located in the galaxy central stellar bar.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3109 as the most massive Antlia-Sextans dIrr, notes its large optical extent and metal-poor old stars, and finds narrow PNe/HII oxygen abundances.

quotes.quote: NGC 3109, at a distance of 1.33 Mpc, is the most massive dIrr galaxy in Antlia-Sextans group. Compared to galaxies of similar total luminosity (e.g., the SMC), the optical extent is approximately two times larger. The H I gas, stellar content and star formation history of this galaxy have been studied, e.g., by . The underlying old stars are metal-poor ( [ Fe/H ] = 1.7 dex), as measured by . reported an oxygen abundance for one HII region with the T_ e method, although the S/N in the [ OIII ] 4363 line was low ( 2.5).

Our preliminary measurement of O/H for this HII region is quite consistent with their value, but with higher S/N=6 for the [ OIII ] 4363 line. We found that oxygen abundances of PNe and HII regions are all in a narrow range near 12+log(O/H) 7.65 0.15.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 3109 with DDO 154 as nearby dwarf galaxies whose resolved rotation curves favor similar low-cusp, zero-stellar-mass best-fit kinematic models.

quotes.quote: In order to contrast the results for NGC 247 with dwarf galaxies, we have applied the same analysis to DDO 154 and NGC 3109. These galaxies, observed by Carignan Beaulieu (1989) and Jobin Carignan (1990) respectively, were shown by Moore (1994) to be inconsistent with a dark matter halo that follows a Hernquist (1990) profile. Both galaxies are nearby and have been observed with good spatial resolution (see Table tab:data ). For NGC 3109 R _1 = 5.2 and R _2 = 27.8 such that it has one of the best resolved HI velocity fields. While DDO 154 has only R _1 0.7 , the radius where the rotation curve changes slope is significantly larger than the beam size.
The best fit kinematic models of both galaxies are remarkably similar. Both have = 0 ( 0.5 at the 99.73 percent confidence level), 12 and _B = 0 .

sources.summary: The paper evaluates NGC 3109 as a possible Hermeian galaxy, comparing its high recessional velocity, distance, and mass with simulated two-host flyby halos.

quotes.quote: Also, like backsplash haloes, most of Hermeians are receding from their second targets, so they may be considered as a possible explanation for galaxies with `strange' peculiar velocities, like NGC 3109 Banic:Zhao:2017,Banik:Haslbauer:Pawlowski:2021 .

The observed Galactocentric distance and radial velocity of NGC 3109 are 1.3 Mpc and 170 s^ 1 km , respectively Soszynski:Gieren:Pietrzy:2006, Dalcanton:Williams:Seth:2009, Banik:Haslbauer:Pawlowski:2021 . Nevertheless, like Banik:Haslbauer:Pawlowski:2021 , we require that NGC 3109 analogues be 1.2 Mpc away from their second targets. We compare velocity of NGC 3109 and simulated Hermeian haloes with the circular velocity of MW halo, to get rid of dimensional velocity.

The obtained velocity distribution admits that NGC 3109 can be Hermeian, consistent with the suggestion by that this galaxy could have perform two gravity assists. Distance to the second targets The obtained mass-distance distribution (Fig.  fig: distance to the second targets ) shows that all Hermeian haloes, whose second targets were MW analogues, are less massive and located at smaller distances from the second target than NGC 3109 .

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3109 has a symmetric carbon-star luminosity function, classifies it as SMC-like, and derives a CS distance modulus of 25.59 mag.

quotes.quote: WLM NGC 3109 WLM and NGC 3109 show highly symmetric CS luminosity functions with skew values of s_ binned =-0.10 and s_ MLE =0.01 for WLM and s_ binned =-0.03 and s_ MLE =-0.01 for NGC 3109. All these values are greater than the limit specified in equation eq:dm , therefore, both galaxies are classified as "SMC-like" galaxies. Using the most suitable calibrator for these two galaxies we obtain distance moduli of _0 = 25.03 0.04 (statistical) for WLM and _0 = 25.59 0.03 (statistical) for NGC 3109. Two other studies using CS report a distance modulus for NGC 3109 and three for WLM.

NGC 3109 Independent distance moduli determinations for NGC 3109 are presented in Table tab:dist_n3109 . Of the fourteen estimates found in the recent literature, ten are in very good agreement with with our results.

sources.summary: The paper maps the H I environment of NGC 3109, measuring its gas mass, identifying a low-surface-brightness warped disk/plume, and proposing that a mild past encounter with Antlia drew out neutral gas.

quotes.quote: As part of a continuing survey of nearby galaxies, we have mapped the neutral gas content of the low surface brightness, Magellanic-type galaxy 3109 -- and its environment, including the Antlia dwarf galaxy --- at unprecedented velocity resolution and brightness sensitivity. The \ mass of 3109 is measured to be (3.8 0.5) 10^8 . A substantial warp in the disk of 3109 is detected in the \ emission image in the form of an extended low surface brightness feature.

Since the plume (or warp) lies at exactly the radial velocity of Antlia, we speculate that Antlia is this very companion, and that some mild interaction has occurred between 3109 and Antlia in the distant past. For a current separation of 50 , and a differential velocity of 50 , an interaction may have taken place 1 ago. The encounter would need to have been prolonged enough to draw out a substantial mass of neutral gas (of order 10^8 ) but mild so as not to cause massive star formation -- the plume is presently undetected optically.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3109 as the most massive Antlia-Sextans galaxy, possibly Magellanic or dwarf spiral, with warped H I, high dark matter, and measured distances.

quotes.quote: NGC 3109 NGC 3109 is the most massive galaxy in the nearby, extremely poor Antlia-Sextans group vdb99,tully02 . While considered as dI by some workers, this galaxy may be better described as a Magellanic spiral or, perhaps, even a dwarf spiral grebel01a,grebel01b . Studies of the H I gas and stellar content have been well documented for this galaxy.

For example, and obtained optical and H I observations and showed that: (1) compared to similar galaxies (e.g., SMC), the total galaxy luminosity is comparable, but the optical extent is roughly two times larger; (2) the outer parts of the H I gas is warped, likely due to an interaction with the Antlia dwarf galaxy; (3) model fits to the rotation profile show that there is roughly ten times more dark matter than luminous matter; and (4) that the gas is a good tracer of the dark matter distribution.

and have obtained distances of 1.36 Mpc and 1.33 Mpc using observations of Cepheid variable stars and the tip of the red giant branch, respectively. Also, obtained a distance of 1.33 Mpc from the magnitude of the tip of the red giant branch using HST WFPC2 observations.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3109 as a Local Group dwarf/irregular galaxy and derives a precise Cepheid-based distance, reddening, and internal extinction estimate from optical and near-infrared photometry.

quotes.quote: We present near-infrared J and K band photometry of 77 Cepheid variables in the Local Group galaxy NGC 3109. Combining our data with the previously published optical V and I band photometry of Cepheids in this galaxy we derive an accurate distance and interstellar reddening to NGC 3109. Adopting a distance modulus of 18.5 mag for the Large Magellanic Cloud, we obtain a true distance modulus to NGC 3109 of (m-M)_0=25.571 0.024 mag (random error), corresponding to a distance of 1.30 0.02 Mpc.

NGC 3109 is a well-studied galaxy on the periphery of the Local Group. It has been classified as an irregular galaxy Irr; san61 , a dwarf spiral Sm; st81 or a dwarf barred spiral SBm; dev91 . The galaxy is seen almost edge-on at an inclination of 75^ 2 and a position angle of the disk of 93^ 2 jc90 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3109 as a Magellanic-type irregular, dominant in a metal-poor dwarf group, suited to chemical-evolution work, with an LMC-like PN count per luminosity.

quotes.quote: Here and in a forthcoming paper, we propose studying the PN and HII region populations in NGC 3109 (DDO 236), a Magellanic-type irregular classified as a late-type spiral SB(s)m (de Vaucouleurs et al. 1991). NGC 3109 is the dominant member of the small group of metal-poor dwarf galaxies that also includes Sextans A, Sextans B, and Antlia, just at the edge of the LG. Due to its distance, NGC 3109 is particularly well-suited to chemical evolution studies because it does not seem to have been affected by tidal effects from the massive galaxies of the LG, so it should not have lost a significant amount of gas.

Comparing this fit with a fit to the available data for the LMC shows that NGC 3109 has about the same number of PNe per unit luminosity as the LMC. By assuming a true distance modulus of 25.62 mag and an extinction A _ 5007 =0.13 mag for NGC 3109, a peak value of 4.57 mag is derived for the PNLF. This value is high compared both with the canonical 4.48 mag found for galaxies with higher metallicity and with the peak magnitude expected at the metallicity of NGC 3109.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 3109 in a three-galaxy low-metallicity dwarf sample mapped with Spitzer/IRS to study spatially resolved PAH emission, star-forming environments, and metallicity-dependent PAH behavior.

quotes.quote: The three galaxies chosen for this sample are NGC 55, NGC 3109, and IC 5152 (see Table galaxysample ). All three are low-metallicity (12 Z _ Z 49 Z _ ; see below) dwarf galaxies located within 2 Mpc. These systems were chosen for their proximity (maximizing spatial resolution) and for their ongoing star formation. Further, the metallicities of the galaxies in this sample bracket the aforementioned empirical PAH transition metallicity. While our sample size is modest, the spatially resolved data for each system provide new insights into the variations of PAH intensities in metal-poor environments.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3109 as a nearby low-metallicity dwarf target, selecting one bright O-type star and reporting its oxygen abundance and unexpectedly high wind strength.

quotes.quote: We present a spectroscopic analysis of VLT/X-Shooter observations of six O-type stars in the low-metallicity ( Z 1/7 Z_ ) galaxies IC 1613, WLM and NGC 3109. The stellar and wind parameters of these sources allow us, for the first time, to probe the mass-loss versus metallicity dependence of stellar winds below that of the Small Magellanic Cloud (at Z 1/5 Z_ ) by means of a modified wind momentum versus luminosity diagram. The wind strengths that we obtain for the objects in WLM and NGC 3109 are unexpectedly high and do not agree with theoretical predictions. The objects in IC 1613 tend towards a higher than expected mass-loss rate, but remain consistent with predictions within their error bars.

The resulting abundances are given in Table tab:basic , and although abundances derived with the strong line method can have uncertainties up to 0.8 dex see e.g. bres09 , our measures agree well with the literature values of 12 + log(O/H) = 7.90 0.08 IC 1613; bres07 , 7.80 0.07 WLM; urba08 and 7.76 0.07 NGC 3109; evan07 derived from B- and A-type supergiants.

In order to observationally constrain stellar and wind parameters at sub-SMC metallicities, we have used the X-Shooter spectrograph mounted on ESO's Very Large Telescope (VLT) UT2 Vernet et al. 2011, in press dodo06 to secure intermediate-resolution spectra of some of the most massive stars in three nearby dwarf galaxies, IC 1613, WLM and NCG 3109, each having a metallicity of Z 1/7 \ Z_ .

sources.summary: NGC 3109 anchors an extended dwarf-galaxy association whose original members plus Leo P form an unusually tight, elongated Local Group configuration with a velocity gradient and possible tidal or filamentary origin.

quotes.quote: We re-consider the association of dwarf galaxies around NGC 3109, whose known members were NGC 3109, Antlia, Sextans A, and Sextans B, based on a new updated list of nearby galaxies and the most recent data. We find that the original members of the NGC 3109 association, together with the recently discovered and adjacent dwarf irregular Leo P, form a very tight and elongated configuration in space. All these galaxies lie within 100 kpc of a line that is 1070 kpc long, from one extreme (NGC 3109) to the other (Leo P), and they show a gradient in the Local Group standard of rest velocity with a total amplitude of 43 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , and a .m.s. scatter of just 16.8 km s ^ 1 . It is shown that the reported configuration is exceptional given the known dwarf galaxies in the Local Group and its surroundings. We conclude that (a) Leo P is very likely an additional member of the NGC 3109 association, and (b) the association is highly ordered in space and velocity, and it is very elongated, suggesting that it was created by a tidal interaction or it was accreted as a filamentary substructure.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3109 as the most massive member of its Local Group dwarf association, with high H I relative to stellar mass, an edge-on Magellanic spiral classification, extended gas, and ongoing star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 3109 is the most massive of the galaxies in this “group”, with M_* + M( H 1 ) = 6.7 10^8 M_ , or about 10^ 2 of the equivalent Milky Way value, although with the important difference that the H 1 mass is significantly larger than the stellar mass Car2013 , as M_* 8 10^7 M_ . It is classified as a Magellanic-type spiral that is close to being edge-on, and with a rotation curve that is flattening by 8.25 kpc (their last data point), with an implied dynamical mass of at least 8.6 10^ 9 M_ . The H 1 gas is more extended than the stellar disk, which is typical of a field spiral, and it has ongoing star formation at a rate of 5 10^ 3 M_ yr^ 1 .

NGC 3109, Sextans A, and Sextans B are at nearly the same distance and part of the sky, which might be coincidental, but showed that many, if not most of the dwarfs in the Local Group lie in associations, and sometimes in well-defined planes Libe2015 . The association containing NGC 3109, Sextans A, and Sextans B has at least two other confirmed and five other possible nearby members Tully2006, Bellaz2013 .

sources.summary: NGC 3109 is modeled as an SMC-mass, low-density dwarf host whose halo contains Antlia and Antlia B, allowing tests of starvation quenching in low-mass environments.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we explore quenching in the lower density environment of the Small-Magellanic-Cloud-mass galaxy NGC 3109 (M _* 10^8 M _ ), which hosts two known dwarf satellite galaxies (Antlia and Antlia B), both of which are deficient compared to similar galaxies in the field and have recently stopped forming stars. Using a new semi-analytic model in concert with the measured star formation histories and gas masses of the two dwarf satellite galaxies, we show that they could not have been quenched solely by direct ram pressure stripping of their interstellar media, as is common in denser environments. Instead, we find that separation of the satellites from pristine gas inflows, coupled with stellar-feedback-driven outflows from the satellites (jointly referred to as the starvation quenching model), can quench the satellites on timescales consistent with their likely infall times into NGC 3109's halo.

NGC 3109 itself is similar in stellar mass to the Small Magellanic Cloud (SMC) but has not yet been accreted by a larger galaxy, making this a very low-density environment. NGC 3109 is known to host two classical dwarf analogs, the Antlia and Antlia B dwarf satellite galaxies (see section:obsprop for more discussion of the observational properties of the system).

sources.summary: NGC 3109 is described as a low-luminosity spiral-like galaxy with possible abundance gradients; the paper reports six bright-line abundances and recommends deeper nebular spectra.

quotes.quote: NGC 3109 The optical appearance (e.g., carignan85,jc90 ) is suggestive of a low-luminosity spiral galaxy. From the data reported previously by lee03south , the authors suggested the presence of an abundance gradient in NGC 3109, which had been previously hinted by grebel01a ( grebel01a , grebel01b ). We did not measure \ in any of the spectra shown here. The subsequent mean of six bright-line abundances is 7.94 0.2 , which is larger than the value by about 0.2 dex, but is in better agreement with other dwarfs at comparable optical luminosity. Since this galaxy is known to contain multiple \ regions and planetary nebulae (i.e., bresolin93,pena06 ), deep spectra of these nebulae would be valuable to confirm the presence or absence of a radial gradient in oxygen abundance.

sources.summary: The paper derives a JAGB distance for NGC 3109 from carbon-star J and K photometry, then compares it with nine normalized TRGB estimates.

quotes.quote: The averaged true TRGB distance modulus then becomes < _o (TRGB)> = 24.67 0.07 mag. NGC 3109 JAGB stars: Battinelli Demers (2009) published J and K magnitudes for 157 carbon stars in NGC 3109. They are plotted in Figure fig:CMDmontage . The luminosity function is found to peak at a mean value of J = 19.41 mag, leading to an apparent distance modulus of 25.61 mag.

sources.summary: The paper corrects NGC 3109 nebular spectra, removes PN7, finds reliable H-line behavior, and notes exceptions in luminosity and data-quality diagnostics.

quotes.quote: We have performed this correction in the spectra of the PNe of the following galaxies: M 31 kwitter12, balick13, corradi15 , M 33 magrini09, bresolin10 , NGC 300 stasinska13 , NGC 3109 pena07a , LMC leisy06 , SMC leisy06, shaw10 , NGC 4449 annibali17 and NGC 6822 richer07, hdezmtnez09 ; all these spectra have a spectral resolution greater than 2--3 \ and a detected line at 4711 . A more detailed discussion on this correction has been done in Section nature . Lines of [ O ii ] 3726+29 are summed in the tables even if observations allow one to separate the lines. The only PNe where this doublet is resolved are: one object in NGC 3109 observed by pena07a and the sample studied by floresduran17 , the PNe in the LMC and SMC reported by tsamis03 , and the SMC sample observed by shaw10 .

The H / line ratio reported in the figure is in agreement with the theoretically expected value for some of the galaxies: M 31, NGC 300, NGC 3109, LMC, SMC, either because the reddening was obtained using these lines precisely or because the observers paid special attention to it. But it is very far from it in NGC 205, M 32, M 81, and many objects in M 33. The first five galaxies have a value of the weighted standard deviation of H / divided by its theoretical value 0.01 while this value is 0.03 for NGC 205, M 32, M 81, and M 33.

The worst cases are M 31, M 33, LMC, NGC 3109, NGC 4449, and NGC 6822 in Figure fig:quality1 and M 33, M 81, and NGC 3109 in Figure fig:quality2 . Note that many of the galaxies have a few outliers in Fig. fig:quality1 . They should be considered with caution in the forthcoming papers. Note that data from bresolin10 for M 33 PNe are not reported in the plots of [ O iii ] 4959/ 5007 and [ N ii ] 6548/ 6584 because the weakest lines of these doublets are not listed in the original paper, but as can be judged from the spectra shown in that paper, the data are of excellent quality.

sources.summary: The paper retains NGC3109 as a nearby star-forming dwarf data point, using BSG-based iron metallicity, H II oxygen abundance, stellar mass, and SFR while noting BSG-method caveats.

quotes.quote: Local star forming galaxies Figure fig: data includes the present-day [ O/Fe ] and sSFR estimates for nearby star forming dwarf galaxies: Small and Large Magellanic Clouds (SMC and LMC, respectively), Sextans A, NGC3109.

We note that for the NGC3109 (big light green circle in Figure fig: data ) Z_ Fe estimate is also based on BSG Hosek14 . However, in this case BSG metallicity is also interpreted as such by the authors and we decide to include it in further analysis.

For NGC3109 we use the blue supergiant based metallicity derived mainly from Fe-group elements lines (and therefore suitable as current iron abundance probe) provided by , direct method HII region based oxygen abundance from and the stellar mass and SFR from .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC3109 as one of the Local Group field dwarfs whose radius-velocity distribution gives a high likelihood of past passage through the Milky Way.

quotes.quote: Comparison of the radius-velocity distributions of VLII populations and measurements of Local Group galaxies presents a strong likelihood that Tucana, Cetus, NGC3109, SextansA, SextansB, Antlia, NGC6822, Phoenix, LeoT, and NGC185 have passed through the Milky Way.

From Table t4 we expect that the following Local Group Objects have with high likelihood ( > 50 per cent) at some point in time passed through the virial radius of the Milky Way: NGC3109, SextansA, SextansB, Antlia, Cetus, Tucana, NGC6822, Phoenix, LeoT, and NGC185.

The separation between the associated and unassociated populations in the distance-velocity plane in VLII was applied in the Local Group to identify field dwarfs that may be associated with the Milky Way: Tucana, Cetus, Antlia, NGC3109, SextansA, SextansB, NGC6822, Phoenix, LeoT and NGC185.

sources.summary: The survey describes NGC 3109 as a southern VLA-ANGST source whose H I profile hints at a larger spiral disk and whose channel maps show a butterfly pattern from a flat rotation curve.

quotes.quote: The southern sources NGC 3109, NGC 247, Antlia, and DDO 6 were observed mostly in the hybrid BnA-, CnB-, and DnC-array configurations that feature elongated placements of the antennas along the northern arm.

On the other end of the mass spectrum, a few galaxies (NGC 404 NGC 3741, DDO 190, Sextans A, DDO 181, NGC 3109, NGC 247) exhibit hints of the more familiar double-horned profile expected from larger spiral disks. The maximum linewidth is observed in NGC 247 with w_ 20 200 . Channel Maps Channel maps of the galaxies are presented in Figs. fig:n247 through fig:kkh98 (natural weighting). Given the high spectral resolution of our data, there is typically only little flux in each velocity bin. Some galaxies, mainly the more massive ones such as NGC 247 or NGC 3109 show the classic ``butterfly'' pattern of a spiral galaxy, a tell-tale sign for a flat rotation curve.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3109 as Antlia's Magellanic-type neighbor, whose warped disk and alignment with Antlia's tidal feature support a close interaction between the pair.

quotes.quote: This elongation is in the direction of Antlia's nearest neighbor, the Magellanic-type NGC 3109. The tail is offset by <10^ from a vector linking the centers of the two galaxies, indicative of interactions between the pair. Combined with the warped disc previously identified in NGC 3109, Antlia and NGC 3109 must be at a small separation for tidal features to be present in Antlia.

sources.summary: The paper observed NGC 3109 in 2022, identified one early K-type RSG, and corrected a candidate YSG to a foreground source using spatial and Gaia information.

quotes.quote: Multi-object spectroscopy and data reduction The MOS observations for eight of the galaxies were obtained in 2020-2021 (ESO programme 105.20HJ) and for the remaining two galaxies (NGC 1313 and NGC 3109) in 2022 (ESO programme 109.22W2).
NGC 3109 We identified one target as a RSG of early K-type (NGC3109-167).

These diagrams helped us correct the YSG classification for NGC 3109--1209 (due to its redshifted spectrum that matched the radial velocity of the target galaxy) to a foreground source, since it is not located on the disk of the host galaxy and has a high proper motion in Gaia DR3.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3109 as one of four Local Group disk galaxies selected for in-depth stellar-population gradient work using wide-field and HST imaging plus chemical-evolution modeling.

quotes.quote: As a next step in our study we selected four prominent Local Group disk galaxies, NGC 6822, NGC 3109, IC 1613, and IC 10 to analyze in-depth the radial (in-plane) and vertical (extraplanar) gradients as a function of stellar age in a wide range of galactocentric distances. Our data include wide-field imaging with ground-based telescopes aimed at reaching the old ( 10 Gyr) stellar population, such as horizontal branch (HB) stars --signposts of an ancient stellar population--, and deep pencil beam fields with HST/ACS WFPC2 reaching the old main-sequence turnoffs (based in part on the LCID team data). We are working on the modeling of the star formation history and chemical evolution of the different stellar sub-structures in these galaxies (see also Aparicio et al., Gallart et al., and Hidalgo et al., this meeting).

gmw_00000401.name: IC 1613
gmw_00000401.overview: IC 1613 is a nearby, isolated dwarf irregular galaxy located within the Local Group [arXiv:1206.1238, arXiv:1712.05963]. It features a central H I bar [arXiv:0904.4455] and exhibits active, ongoing star formation [arXiv:2310.06785] that is particularly intense in its northeastern region [arXiv:1206.1238].

sources.summary: The paper studies IC 1613 as a metal-poor irregular galaxy, building stellar and OB-association catalogues and describing its extended stellar component, H I bar, and active star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: In preparation for the new instrumentation coming up on the GTC, our goal is to build a list of massive star candidates in the metal-poor irregular galaxy IC 1613. The catalogue must have very high astrometric accuracy, apt for the current generation of multi-object spectrographs. A census of OB associations in this galaxy is also needed, to provide important additional information about age and environment of the candidate OB stars. From INT-WFC observations, we have built an astrometric and photometric catalogue of stars in IC 1613.

The stellar component of IC 1613 is known to extend little farther than 16 .5 from its centre BAG07 . The optically brightest part is well separated into 2 sides by a central strip of H i , usually referred to as 'the bar'. The bar is the NE ridge of a hole of the H i distribution where the SW side of the galaxy is enclosed Sal06 . Star formation is ongoing at the two sides of the bar and at the SW part of the H i cavity, but it is more intense on the NE lobe of the galaxy where spectacular giant H ii shells blown by OB associations MCW88,VGal01 are seen.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 1613 as a nearby low-metallicity Local Group dwarf irregular with sub-SMC stellar metallicity and recent massive star formation, especially in its northeastern region.

quotes.quote: IC 1613 is a dwarf irregular galaxy in the Local Group with a distance modulus of (m-M)_0 = 24.31 0.06 dolphin01 . It has a metallicity of (O/H)+12 = 7.80 0.10 as determined from its B-supergiants bresolin07 , whereas nebular studies vary between 7.60 and 7.90 lee03 . The metallicity of the B-supergiants indicates Z_ IC 1613 = 0.13 0.07 Z_ (using solar abundances from asplund09 ). This value is below the value derived by most authors for the SMC O- and B-stars.

For example, heap06 obtain Z_ SMC = 0.2 Z_ from an analysis of O-stars and dufton05 obtain Z_ SMC = 0.25 Z_ from an analysis of B-supergiants. The situation is similar for other authors, e.g. bouret03 , who obtained Z_ SMC = 0.2 Z_ . Therefore the stellar metallicity in IC 1613 is below the SMC value and the study of its stars represents a step forward in our analyses at low metallicities. IC 1613 shows a recent and intense burst of massive star formation, particularly in its NE part.

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 1613 as a low-extinction Local Group dwarf irregular, deriving a Cepheid distance and reddening from near- and mid-infrared period-luminosity relations and testing metallicity effects.

quotes.quote: We have observed the dwarf galaxy IC 1613, at multiple epochs in the mid--infrared using Spitzer and contemporaneously in the near--infrared using the new FourStar near-IR camera on Magellan. We have constructed Cepheid period--luminosity relations in the J , H , K_ s , [ 3.6 ] and [ 4.5 ] bands and have used the run of their apparent distance moduli as a function of wavelength to derive the line--of--sight reddening and distance to IC 1613. Using a nine--band fit, we find E(B-V) = 0.05 0.01  mag and an extinction--corrected distance modulus of _ 0 = 24.29 0.03_ statistical 0.03_ systematic  mag. By comparing our multi--band and [ 3.6 ] distance moduli to results from the tip of the red giant branch and red clump distance indicators, we find that metallicity has no measurable effect on Cepheid distances at 3.6 m in the metallicity range 1.0 Fe/H 0.2 , hence derivations of the Hubble constant at this wavelength require no correction for metallicity.

IC 1613 was discovered by wolf_1906 . It is a type IB(s)m dwarf irregular galaxy in the Local Group 1971ApJ...166...13S, 1991trcb.book.....D , situated at high galactic latitude at a consensus distance of 736 49  kpc Average distance based on 57 measurements in NED: http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/cgi-bin/nDistance?name=IC+1613 . This converts to a true distance modulus of = 24.33 0.07  mag, which is slightly closer than M31. IC 1613 is highly resolved and its position above the plane of the Milky Way results in low foreground extinction.

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 1613 as an irregular Local Group benchmark whose numerous Cepheid, RR Lyrae, and TRGB distances yield a recommended modulus of 24.34 mag consistent with later averages.

quotes.quote: The inclusion of IC 1613 is particularly interesting from the perspective of metallicity differences: the galaxy's Cepheids, for instance, are characterized by a significantly different metallicity compared with their Galactic counterparts, [ Fe/H ] 1 dex (for a discussion, see e.g. Majaess et al. 2009).

Distance determinations to M33 and IC 1613 Figure m33dist.fig is equivalent to Figure m31dist.fig , except that it relates to distance estimates to M33 and IC 1613. Among the different distance tracers available for M33, those based on Cepheids ( N = 56 , of which three are Type II Cepheids), RR Lyrae variables ( N = 14 ), and the level of the TRGB ( N = 20 ) are most numerous. Similarly, the distance determinations to IC 1613 based on Cepheids ( N = 84 ; two Type II measurements), RR Lyrae variables ( N = 14 ), and the TRGB magnitude ( N = 30 ; including a small number of other giant-based distance determinations published in the 1980s) dominate the total tally. We will thus focus our discussion on these tracers. As for M31, we first updated the calibration of the distance estimates based on Cepheids and RR Lyrae variables (as well as those based on long-period variables, for M33) relative to an LMC distance modulus of (m-M)_0^ LMC = 18.50 mag.

For IC 1613 the three post-2000 distance estimates based on RC magnitudes (Dolphin et al. 2001, 2003; Udalski et al. 2001) are fully consistent with our recommended value. We also point out that Scowcroft et al. (2013) derived a weighted average of 57 distance estimates from the NASA Extragalactic Database (NED) that is consistent with our result, i.e., (m-M)_0^ IC 1613 = 24.33 0.07 mag, although the NED data set is incomplete at the time of writing and their approach does not allow control of population-specific systematic uncertainties, as we have attempted here.

sources.summary: The paper presents IC 1613 as a near face-on dwarf irregular with clumpy HI bubbles, a shallow and wiggly rotation curve, and a poor fit driven by disequilibrium and uncertain inclination.

quotes.quote: IC 1613: IC 1613 was first discovered by 1929AN....234..407B . It is a near face-on dwarf irregular on the edge of the Local Group, some 740 kpc away 2013ApJ...773..106S . It has been mass modelled numerous times in the literature before e.g. 1989AJ.....98.1274L,2015AJ....149..180O , while many authors have noted the clumpy nature of its ISM, with substantial HI bubbles and shells 2002A&AT...21..223L,2006A&A...448..123S .

IC 1613: a near face-on disequilibrium dwarf In contrast to our excellent fits for WLM and NGC 6822, IC 1613 gives a very poor fit (compare the black lines with the red data points in the rotation curve panel). If fitting over the full data range, our model pushes on the lower bound of our M_ 200 prior since it cannot reproduce the extremely shallow rise of IC 1613's rotation curve. For this reason we exclude the inner region, fitting over the range [ 1.9,2.5 ] kpc. (This explains why the reduced _ red ^2 = 0.13 reported in Table tab:data seems surprisingly good despite the obviously poor fit.) The poor fit for IC 1613 is perhaps not surprising when considering its gas morphology.

sources.summary: The paper discusses IC 1613 as a nearby dwarf where near-infrared TRGB measurements agree across regions, linking that consistency to homogeneous star formation and limited metallicity variation.

quotes.quote: Recent TRGB measurements in the near-infrared JHK bands in the nearby galaxy IC 1613 found that the TRGB magnitudes derived from different regions of the galaxy were consistent, implying a TRGB luminosity independent of any radial gradient in stellar age or metallicity within the galaxy Hatt2017, Madore2018 . However, the star formation history for this galaxy has been shown to be remarkably homogeneous across similar radii Skillman2014 . Furthermore, metallicity variations in IC 1613 are expected to be small for two reasons.

First, in keeping with its low stellar mass, IC 1613 is metal poor today, so, by definition, the variations in metallicity are limited. Specifically, Kirby2013 determine the mean stellar [ Fe/H ] = -1.19 , with a dispersion of 0.37 dex. Even the current ISM oxygen abundance, as determined from \ regions, is 12+ log(O/H) = 7.73 0.04 (which translates to [ O/H ] = -0.96 using the solar oxygen abundance from Asplund2009 ) and shows only mild evidence of chemical evolution Bresolin2007 .

Second, the older stellar populations (i.e., TRGB progenitors) of dwarf galaxies appear to be well-mixed radially, resulting in very weak gradients in the metallicity of the older stellar populations e.g., Hidalgo2013 . Thus, the TRGB magnitudes at different radii in IC 1613 would be expected to agree as they are measured from stars of similar age and metal content.

sources.summary: The paper presents Chandra observations of nearby irregular IC 1613, describing its northeast star-forming region, S8 as the galaxy's brightest X-ray source, and the galaxy's Local Group distance and low metallicity.

quotes.quote: We conducted an observation of the nearby irregular galaxy IC 1613 with the Chandra X-ray Observatory using the S3 chip of the ACIS with an effective exposure time of 49.9 ksec. The observation primarily targeted the extensive bubble and star formation region in the northeast quadrant of the galaxy. The only known supernova remnant (SNR) in IC 1613, S8, is also the galaxy's most luminous X-ray source (L _ X (0.3-8 keV) 5-6 10^ 36 erg s ^ 1 ).

IC 1613 is classified as a DDO Irr V vandenBergh00 lying within the Local Group. Estimates of its distance land around 730 kpc ( Dolphin01 , Karachentsev04 ). The most recent distance determination is 724 17 kpc Hatt17 . We here adopt 725 kpc. IC 1613 is known to be a low-metallicity galaxy ( [ Fe/H ] = -1.3, Kunth2000 ).

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 1613 as a detailed distance comparison case, discussing its low-dust suitability, extensive published distance record, metallicity assumptions, and Gaia-anchored RR Lyrae distance.

quotes.quote: To illustrate our distance determinations relative to the broader literature, we consider the case of IC 1613, a star-forming dwarf galaxy located at the end of the LG. IC 1613 has a low inclination angle and relatively little internal or foreground dust, making it particularly well-suited for distance determinations at low-metallicity. According to NED, there have been over 100 distance measurements published for IC 1613. Figure fig:ic1613 shows Population II (i.e., TRGB and RR Lyrae) distances for IC 1613 that have been published over the last decade.

Finally, our distance to IC 1613 is =24.42 0.03 . We anchor our data with the Neeley2019 RR Lyrae data with updated Gaia eDR3 parallaxes (see Table tab:mwrrl ) and adopt a mean metallicity of Fe/H =-2.0 . Though our selected metallicity may seem too low given the other studies, we argue that it is a reasonable choice. Our adopted metallicity falls within the CMD-based age-metallicity relationship measured by skillman2014 .

sources.summary: The review uses IC1613 as a dwarf-irregular case study where HI holes and inclination biases complicate rotation curves and dark-matter mass modeling.

quotes.quote: We show an example of HI holes and their correlation with OB associations for the nearby dwarf irregular galaxy IC1613 in Fig. fig:holes (left panel; data taken from ). Notice that the smallest, fastest-expanding, holes correlate well with OB associations (blue), while the largest HI holes have no measured expansion and correlate poorly with the OB associations. This latter provides further evidence that the HI holes originate from expanding hot superbubbles.

In Fig. fig:holes (right panel), we show an example of this behaviour for the isolated dwarf irregular IC1613. This is a rare case where we have both gas kinematics from the LittleTHINGS survey oh11 and stellar kinematics kirby14 . The black data points show the rotation curve for IC1613 derived from its HI gas kinematics using the state-of-the-art tilted ring fitting code ^ 3D barolo iorio17 . ^ 3D barolo favours an inclination for IC1613 of i=39 2^ with small formal uncertainties. This yields a very slowly rising rotation curve that, at face value, implies the presence of an enormous low-density dark matter core with little-to-no inner dark matter at all oman16 .

sources.summary: The paper studies IC 1613 as a gas-rich isolated dwarf irregular, measuring red-supergiant metallicities and temperatures and finding low metallicity, no spatial segregation, and mixing lengths matching its RSG population.

quotes.quote: We report a spectroscopic study on red supergiant stars (RSGs) in the irregular dwarf galaxy IC 1613 in the Local Group. We derive the effective temperatures ( T_ eff ) and metallicities of 14 RSGs by synthetic spectral fitting to the spectra observed with the MMIRS instrument on the MMT telescope for a wavelength range from 1.16 m to 1.23 m. A weak bimodal distribution of the RSG metallicity centered on the [ Fe/H ] = 0.65 is found, which is slightly lower than or comparable to that of the Small Magellanic Cloud (SMC). There is no evidence for spatial segregation between the metal rich ( [ Fe/H ] >-0.65 ) and poor ( [ Fe/H ] <-0.65 ) RSGs throughout the galaxy.

IC 1613 is an excellent laboratory to study RSGs for a number of reasons. IC 1613 is a gas-rich, isolated galaxy with no past interaction with other galaxies. The distance of about 730 kpc Dolphin2001,Piet2006 , a low inclination of i=38 Lake1989 and the high Galactic latitude implying a low extinction value of E(B-V)=0.02-0.04 Sandage1971,Freedman1988a,Cole1999 provide a great opportunity to access the resolved stellar populations with a reasonable exposure time from a ground-based telescope. Several studies indicate that IC 1613 has a nearly constant star formation rate over its entire lifetime Cole1999,Skillman2003,Skillman2014 , hosting old ( >10 Gyr), intermediate-age ( 1-10 Gyr), and young ( < 1 Gyr) stellar populations.

sources.summary: The paper uses MUSE spectroscopy of IC 1613 to measure stellar velocities and metallicities, detecting significant stellar rotation, agreement with H I gas kinematics, and asymmetric-drift differences among stellar populations.

quotes.quote: Our aim is to determine the kinematics of individual stars as a function of age in IC 1613, a star-forming, gas-rich and isolated dwarf galaxy of the Local Group. We present results of a new spectroscopic survey of IC 1613 conducted with MUSE, an integral field spectrograph mounted on the Very Large Telescope. We extracted 2000 sources, from which we separated stellar objects for their subsequent spectral analysis. The quality of the dataset allowed us to obtain accurate classifications ( T_ eff to better than 500 K) and line-of-sight velocities (with average _v 7 km s ^ 1 ) for about 800 stars.

We also obtained reliable metallicities ( _ Fe/H 0.25 dex) for about 300 RGB stars. The kinematic analysis revealed for the first time the presence of stellar rotation with high significance. We found general agreement with the velocity field of the neutral gas component, although on average the stars showed greater velocity dispersion and slower rotation, which can be attributed to asymmetric drift. Examining the kinematics of the different stellar components, the MS stars appear to follow closely that of the gas.

sources.summary: The paper studies IC 1613 as a nearby low-metallicity dwarf irregular, finding faint shells, possible SNR-related shock ionization, rejected Wolf-Rayet candidates, and evidence for a lower WR formation rate.

quotes.quote: We present a study of the nearby low-metallicity dwarf galaxy IC 1613, focusing on the search for massive stars and related feedback processes, as well as for faint supernova remnants (SNR) in late stages of evolution. We obtained the deepest images of IC 1613 in the narrow-band , and emission lines and new long-slit spectroscopy observations using several facilities (6-m BTA, 2.5m SAI MSU, and 150RTT telescopes), in combination with the multi-wavelength archival data from MUSE/VLT, VLA, XMM- Newton , and Swift /XRT. Our deep narrow-band photometry identifies several faint shells in the galaxy , and we further investigate their physical characteristics with the new long-slit spectroscopy observations and the archival multi-wavelength data. Based on energy balance calculations and assumptions about their possible nature, we propose that one of the shells is a possible remnant of a supernova explosion. We study five out of eight Wolf-Rayet (WR) star candidates previously published for this galaxy using the emission line mapping, MUSE/VLT archival spectra, and new long-slit spectra.

Using presently the deepest mapping in the and emission lines of the ionised gas in the nearby dIrr galaxy IC 1613 together with new long-slit spectroscopy at the 6-m BTA and archival 8-m MUSE/VLT data we carried out a study of faint ionised regions and emission from massive stars in this galaxy. The main findings are the following: The most of the shells (B1, B3--B7) are related with OB associations. Their ionisation is caused by photoionisation of UV photons of nearest O-stars, as it followed from their line ratio diagnostic diagrams and energy budget evaluation ( the case of B1, B3 and B7 ). The B2 shell is intriguing as there are no stars that could release the required UV-luminosity to ionise it. There is the X-ray source projected into the shell , however it may be also interpreted as a background galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper reports isolated, unusually high recent SFR peaks in IC 1613, verifies the bright-source photometry, then removes two points as likely Galactic-halo contamination.

quotes.quote: In Figure fig:SFHs_All , it is evident that IC 1613 exhibits exceptionally high SFRs at t=6.96 and t=7.16 (also see Table tab:The Best Simulated Model CMDs and Corresponding SFHs ). These SFRs are nearly a hundred times higher than those at earlier lookback times and contribute approximately 1.6 10^ 6 M_ to the stellar mass of IC 1613. However, unlike NGC 6822, these two peaks in the SFH of IC 1613 are not continuous and appear as isolated points. To asses the reliability of these unusually high SFRs, we examined the information related to the sources responsible for these peaks, which is summarized in Table tab:Information on the Two Brightest RSGs in IC 1613 .

For the UKIRT JHK bands which are listed in tab:Information on the Two Brightest RSGs in IC 1613 and PS1 grizy bands, not included in Table tab:Information on the Two Brightest RSGs in IC 1613 , these sources are the brightest. Consequently, we consider the photometry of these two sources to be reliable.

Nevertheless, considering the discontinuity of SFH, it is plausible that these two sources are contaminated by red giants in the Galactic halo. Therefore, in the analysis of the SFH of IC 1613, we removed these two points.

sources.summary: The paper presents near-infrared JK photometry of IC 1613, describing it as a faint Local Group irregular with young red supergiants, rich intermediate-age AGB stars, and distance constraints.

quotes.quote: Here, we present NIR observations of IC 1613. IC 1613 is a faint, irregular galaxy within the Local Group. Recently, Cole et al. (1999) studied the history of star formation in the central part of the galaxy using HST VI photometry. They found a dominant old stellar population --- red giant branch and red clump stars, and a prominent sequence of intermediate age AGB stars.

In summary, we find substantial evolved stellar populations in our three fields in IC 1613, including the region of ongoing star formation. The brightest stars are massive (20 - 25 M_ ) red supergiants with K magnitudes between 13 - 13.5 and (J-K) 1 . Based upon a comparison with the Padua isochrones (Bertelli et al. 1994), these brightest RSGs are very young, with ages between 8 and 25 Myr. The main feature of the NIR CMDs is the rich intermediate age AGB population.

sources.summary: The paper reviews IC 1613 as a Local Group dwarf irregular with catalogued H II regions, superbubbles, and an adopted direct oxygen abundance for H II 37.

quotes.quote: IC 1613 This is a well-studied dI in the Local Group whose position is near the celestial equator. The M_ HI /L_B value for IC 1613 is typical for its luminosity compared to other dIs. Its H II regions have been catalogued by , , , and . The brightest H II region was identified by Sandage as 3 (S3), which has been labelled H II 37 by .

, , and obtained photoelectric spectrophotometry of S3, and all three studies showed that the ionization source was a single Wolf-Rayet (W-R) star. and obtained resolved stellar photometry and derived distances from the tip of the red giant branch and the red clump, respectively; these distance measurements are in agreement (730 kpc). and obtained Fabry-Perot interferometry at \ and \ to study the kinematics of S3 and S8 and found that the nebular emission exhibits two-lobe structure with superbubbles covering the entire galaxy.

Our spectrum of H II 37 (Fig. fig_wr1613 ) also exhibits broad Wolf-Rayet features near 4471, 4686, and 5800 . Due to a strong \ detection, an oxygen abundance of 12 + log(O/H) = 7.62 0.05 was derived, which is the adopted value here,

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 1613 as a nearby, isolated, low-metallicity dwarf irregular where the survey found the first two candidate planetary nebulae inside its optical extent.

quotes.quote: The gas-rich dwarf irregular galaxy IC 1613 was first discovered by wolf06 and because of its proximity (730 kpc, dolphin01 ), its high Galactic latitude (-60 .6) and consequently small Galactic extinction, has provided an excellent opportunity to observe stellar populations in a low metallicity environment. In fact, its oxygen abundance as derived from \ regions is approximately 12 + log (O/H)=7.7 ( skillman ; lee03 ). Moreover, IC 1613 is a relatively isolated non-interacting irregular galaxy, as proven by the lack of stellar clusters generated by interactions with other galaxies vdb00B . From an evolutionary point of view, it is a very primitive galaxy because the stellar mass is comparable to the gas mass hodge91 . freedman presented B, V, R and I stellar photometry pointing out the presence of stars in a wide range of ages, from young to intermediate and old population. Recent Hubble Space Telescope observations by skillman03 found an enhanced star formation rate (SFR) from 3 to 6 Gry ago.

IC 1613 The objects belonging to IC 1613 presented in this paper are the first candidate PNe discovered in this galaxy. lequeux in an objective-prism survey for emission-line objects in this galaxy identified one candidate PN, but neither a following survey for \ regions skillman nor this work could confirm this object. The two candidates lie inside the optical size of the galaxy (16 20 , according to ables ). The position and fluxes of the PN candidates are listed in Table Tab_pos and marked in Figure Fig_pos .

sources.summary: The paper uses Spitzer mid-infrared Cepheid photometry in IC 1613 to derive a true distance modulus of 24.27 mag, describing it as a gas-rich, star-forming Local Group dwarf.

quotes.quote: We present mid-infrared Period-Luminosity relations for Cepheids in the Local Group galaxy IC 1613. Using archival IRAC imaging data from Spitzer we were able to measure single-epoch magnitudes for five, 7 to 50-day, Cepheids at 3.6 and 4.5 m. When fit to the calibrating relations, measured for the Large Magellanic Cloud Cepheids, the data give apparent distance moduli of 24.29 0.07 and 24.28 0.07 at 3.6 and 4.5 m, respectively. A multi-wavelength fit to previously published BVRIJHK apparent moduli and the two IRAC moduli gives a true distance modulus of 24.27 0.02 mag with E(B-V) = 0.08 mag, and a corresponding metric distance of 715 kpc.

The Local Group galaxy, IC 1613 is a highly-resolved dwarf of type IB(s)m (de Vaucouleurs et al. 1991). IC 1613 is rich in gas and is actively forming stars; as a consequence it also contains many Classical (Population I) Cepheid variables. These stars have been the subject of a variety of multi-wavelength distance determinations, which place the host galaxy at a distance of about 700 kpc, comparable to that of M31, the Andromeda nebula.

sources.summary: The paper presents IC 1613 as a very metal-poor irregular galaxy whose OB associations trace giant HII shells, an age gradient, and recent star formation history.

quotes.quote: We present our work on the very metal-poor irregular IC 1613 (Garcia et al. 2010a). The association properties are not only a powerful aid towards finding the most interesting candidate massive stars, but also reveal the galaxy's structure and recent star formation history.

They group in the NE lobe of the galaxy, where IC 1613 displays spectacular giant HII shells. We find association ages ranging from age [ yr ] = 5.9 to 7.7, usually with some spread. For each association, we provide its age as a colored contour in Fig. fig_3 (cyan age [ yr ] =5.9 , blue =6.2 , dark green =6.5 , light green =6.8 , yellow =7.1 , orange =7.4 , red =7.7 , violet =8.0 ). There seems to be a galactic-scale age gradient following the NE direction, with the youngest members located in the NE lobe. This region, which also exhibits the largest age dispersion in the galaxy, is located in a density enhancement of neutral hydrogen (see Garcia et al. \ 2010a and Fig. fig_3 ).

sources.summary: The paper compares IC 1613 Cepheids with SMC templates, finds their P-C and P-L relations indistinguishable, and derives individual distances from the SMC relations.

quotes.quote: Sandage 2 Department of Physics and Astronomy, Univ. of Basel Klingelbergstrasse 82, 4056 Basel, Switzerland g-a.tammann@unibas.ch The Observatories of the Carnegie Institution of Washington, 813 Santa Barbara Street, Pasadena, CA 91101, USA. -- Deceased on November 13, 2010 Received 21 December 2010 / Accepted 22 April 2011 The metal-poor, fundamental-mode (P0) and first-overtone (P1) Cepheids in the dwarf galaxies IC 1613, WLM, Pegasus, Sextans A, Sextans B, and Leo A are compared with the about equally metal-poor Cepheids of the Small Magellanic Cloud (SMC). The P-C and P-L relations of IC 1613 The P-C relations in (B V)^ 0 and (V I)^ 0 and the P-L relations in B , V , and I of the P0 Cepheids and the excluded variables are shown in the five panels of Fig. fig:01 . The scatter in the data for the IC 1613 Cepheids about the SMC relations in the five panels is the same as of the SMC Cepheids themselves. Forced linear fits over the whole period range for the IC 1613 and SMC Cepheids agree to within 1 . The relations of the two galaxies are indistinguishable.

The IC 1613 data fit the SMC relations very well, their scatter being the same as for the SMC Cepheids proper. On the assumption that P0 and P1 Cepheids in IC 1613 and SMC have the same luminosity and that (m-M)_ SMC ^ 0 =18.93 , the individual distances of IC 1613 Cepheids are derived by comparing them with the corresponding P-L relations of SMC from Sect. sec:2 .

sources.summary: The paper uses IC 1613 as a nearby dwarf irregular comparison, smoothing earlier ionized-gas observations to test resolution effects on gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: In order to analyze the effects of spatial resolution on the observed kinematics, we examined two nearby and well-studied dwarf irregular galaxies of the Local Group: IC 10 and IC 1613. We have earlier observed both galaxies at the 6-m telescope with the same equipment, described in Section  obs . The spatial size of regions, encompassed by current star formation is about 1 kpc, just like in the galaxies described above (hereinafter -- the `main sample'). A detailed description of the distributions of ionized and neutral gas, the identification of the expanding HI and HII bubbles, and a discussion of the relation between these bubbles and young stellar groups are given in Lozinskaya2008 and Egorov2010 for IC 10, and in Lozinskaya2003 for IC 1613.

To imitate the effect of low resolution, the original data cubes for IC 10 and IC 1613 in the line were first smoothed by the two-dimensional Gaussians, and then binned, so that the resulting pixel size amounted to 5.6 arcsec. Table  tab_ic lists the distance to the galaxy, and the initial ( ) and smoothed ( _ smo ) spatial resolution. The smoothed data show how these two galaxies would look like when observed with a resolution of _ smo 40 pc, i.e. under the same conditions as the objects of the main sample.

sources.summary: The paper presents new red-giant spectroscopy for IC 1613, using Bernard photometry, a Cepheid distance modulus, extinction corrections, and target selection like NGC 6822.

quotes.quote: We present new spectroscopy of red giants in NGC 6822, IC 1613, the Pegasus dIrr, Leo A, and Aquarius. These are the first published results from red giant spectroscopy in all of these galaxies except NGC 6822, which was studied by tol01 .

IC 1613 We selected DEIMOS targets for IC 1613 from ber07 's ( ber07 ) photometric catalog, kindly provided to us by E. \ Bernard. We assumed a Cepheid-based distance modulus of 24.34 tam11 . The faint magnitude limit was I = 23 . The extinction corrections were A_V = 0.08 and A_I = 0.05 sak04 . Other details are the same as NGC 6822.

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 1613 as a nearby low-metallicity dwarf irregular whose OB-star winds are SMC-like; it finds SMC-like iron abundance, confirmed low oxygen abundance, and subsolar young-population alpha/Fe.

quotes.quote: We present the first quantitative UV spectroscopic analysis of resolved OB stars in IC 1613. Because of its alleged very low metallicity ( 1/10 , from H 2 regions), studies in this Local Group dwarf galaxy could become a significant step forward from the SMC towards the extremely metal-poor massive stars of the early Universe.

The terminal velocity of IC 1613 O-stars is clearly lower than Milky Way counterparts, but there is no clear difference between IC 1613 and SMC or LMC analogue stars. We find no clear segregation with host galaxy in the terminal velocities of B-supergiants, nor in the /   ratio of the whole OB star sample in any of the studied galaxies. Finally, we present first evidence that the Fe-abundance of IC 1613 OB stars is similar to the SMC, in agreement with previous results on red supergiants. With the confirmed 1/10 solar oxygen abundances of B-supergiants, our results indicate that IC 1613's /Fe ratio is sub-solar.
IC 1613 is one of the closest dIrr to the Milky Way DM=24.27, Dal01 , with low foreground extinction E(B-V)=0.02, LFM93 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes IC 1613 blue supergiants, finding bimodal metallicities tied to H I structure and deriving an FGLR distance modulus consistent with other measures.

quotes.quote: We present the spectral analysis of 21 blue supergiant stars of spectral type late B to early A within the Local Group dwarf galaxy IC 1613 based on VLT-FORS2 low resolution spectra. Combining our results with studies of early B type blue supergiants we report a wide bi-modal distribution of metallicities with two peaks around [ Z ] -0.50 dex and [ Z ] -0.85 dex. The bi-modal distribution correlates with spatial location, when compared with column densities of neutral hydrogen in IC 1613.
