Whereas NGC 891 has been studied extensively with ground-based telescopes, here the small scale structure of the extended emission line gas is presented at high spatial resolution of 0 1, corresponding to 4.6 pc at the distance to NGC 891.

The majority of the H emission is diffuse. Several discrete features (e.g., filaments) are visible as well. Some of the filaments reach distances of up to 2.2 kpc above the galactic plane, and are extremely collimated, even at high galactic latitudes. We compare the morphology of these filaments with theoretical models, which describe possible transport mechanisms in a general way. Despite the prominent dust lane, many bubbles, shells and super-shells can be discerned in the midplane.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 891 as the key test case where self-consistent dust and stellar geometry models reproduce both optical and far-infrared/submillimeter morphology and dust-heating contributions.

quotes.quote: Such a test has been done in just one case (for NGC 891; Popescu et al. ) by comparing the images at 170 and 200 m taken with the ISOPHOT instrument on board the Infrared Space Observatory (ISO) with the model predictions of Popescu et al. (see Fig. 10). The comparison between the observed maps and the simulated maps for the diffuse component (at 170 and 200 m ) show a remarkable agreement. To search for small differences between the model and the observations, not detectable in the maps due to the high dynamical range of the displayed data, residual maps of the difference between the observed and the simulated maps are also shown. The residuals show the localised sources, which account for about 10 of the total emission at these wavelengths, in accordance with the prediction of the model. Another comparison is between observed and predicted radial profiles (see Fig. 11, left panel).

Here again one can see a very good agreement. Again, the small excess emission in the observed profile is in agreement with the model prediction for localised sources. To date, NGC 891 is the only galaxy for which an intrinsic distribution of dust and stars could be found from a self-consistent modeling and which simultaneously accounts for both the optical and FIR morphologies.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 891 at 850 and 450 microns, comparing dust continuum with CO and infrared emission and deriving dust temperatures, gas mass, and central disk properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 891 is a bright, non-interacting spiral galaxy (SA(s)b: De Vaucouleurs et al. \ 1976). It is very nearly edge-on ( i > 88^ , PA = 23^ ; Sofue Nakai 1993). NGC 891 is one of the major members of the NGC 1023 group and its distance is estimated to be 9.5 Mpc for H_ 0 =75 Mpc ^ 1 , making for a scale of 22 '' /kpc or 46 pc/ '' . Many authors have remarked on the apparent similarity between NGC 891 and the Milky Way.

We have mapped the submillimetre emission from the edge-on galaxy NGC 891 at wavelengths of 850 m and 450 m. The appearance of both maps is very similar to those obtained by others at 1300 m and in the ^ 12 CO line. The integrated infrared spectrum of NGC 891 shortwards of 100 m is dominated by emission from PAH particles and very small dust grains. Longwards of 100 m emission from large dust grains dominates.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 891 extra-planar gas with galactic fountains plus infall, finding low-angular-momentum accretion improves the halo lag fit and implies an infall rate near the star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: We applied our model of a fountain that interacts with infalling gas to the \ observations of NGC 891 oos07 . As in FB06, clouds were ejected nearly vertically from the disc with a velocity drawn from a Gaussian distribution, and the dispersion of this distribution was adjusted to optimize the agreement between the model and observational data cubes. Fig. f_models891 (leftmost column) shows five sample channel maps for NGC 891 at 28'' (about 1.3 ) resolution; heliocentric velocities in are in the top-left corners. A key feature of the data is that the apparent vertical thickness of the channel maps increases as the velocity moves away from values characteristic of fast rotation towards the systemic velocity.

We find that the total accretion rate of cold gas required by the model with dominant polar component of accretion is M_ inflow 3.4 , which is very close to the star formation rate of NGC891 SFR =3.8 ; pop04 . When the dominant component of the infall velocity is radial, the data are best fitted when the fountain gas suffers a phase change such that it becomes visible as \ when |z| peaks. A galaxy with the star-formation rate of NGC 891 produces a significant flux of ionizing radiation and it is plausible that a large fraction of the outgoing fountain flow would be ionised. The right-central column shows channel maps for a model in which cold material is accreting at a rate M_ inflow 2.3 . We see that this model fits the data as well as the model with polar accretion shown in the left-central column.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 891 with HST/ACS, measuring thick-disk and spheroid stellar populations, metallicity distributions, weak halo gradients, and differences from analogous Milky Way structures.

quotes.quote: We present deep VI images obtained with the Advanced Camera for Surveys on board the Hubble Space Telescope, covering three fields in the north-east side of the edge-on disk galaxy NGC 891. The observed fields span a wide range of galactocentric distances along the eastern minor axis, extending from the plane of the disk to 12 kpc, and out to 25 kpc along the major axis. The photometry of individual stars reaches 2.5 magnitudes below the tip of the red giant branch. We use the astrophotometric catalogue to probe the stellar content and metallicity distribution across the thick disk and spheroid of NGC 891.

With its high inclination 89.8 0.5 kregel+vanderkruit05 NGC 891 is the ideal target for the study of vertical structure and stellar populations above the plane of the disk. At a distance of 9.7 Mpc mouhcine+07 , it is the closest edge-on spiral galaxy with morphological type, disk structure and mass similar to that of the Milky Way vanderkruit84, garcia-burillo+92 . However, it has been acknowledged in numerous studies, that it has much higher vertical extent of neutral and ionised gas structure garcia-burillo+92,scoville+93,kamphuis+07 , with a huge neutral HI halo surrounding it oosterloo+07,sancisi+08 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 891 as a Milky Way analogue with resolved stellar-halo substructures, including a giant stream and a large thick cocoon-like component around its disk and bulge.

quotes.quote: Here, we present the first panoramic view of a spiral galaxy beyond the Local Group, based on the largest, contiguous, ground-based imaging survey to date resolving the stellar halo of the nearest prime analogue of the Milky Way, NGC 891 ( D 10 Mpc). The low surface brightness outskirts of this galaxy are populated by multiple, coherent, and vast substructures over the 90 kpc 90 kpc extent of the survey. These include a giant stream, the first to be resolved into stars beyond the Local Group using ground-based facilities, that loops around the parent galaxy up to distances of 50 kpc. The bulge and the disk of the galaxy are found to be surrounded by a previously undetected large, flat and thick cocoon-like stellar structure at vertical and radial distances of up to 15 kpc and 40 kpc respectively.

As part of this effort, we have targeted the edge-on galaxy NGC 891, often considered as the nearest prime analog of the Galaxy. Our group recently used deep optical imaging data, obtained with the Advanced Camera for Surveys on board the Hubble Space Telescope (HST/ACS), to investigate the properties of the resolved extra-planar stellar populations over the south-east quadrant of NGC 891, extending up to 10 kpc from the galactic plane mouhcine07,ibata09,rejkuba09,harris09 . Succinctly summarized, those studies indicate that the thick disk of NGC 891 shares comparable structural and chemical properties to its Galactic counterpart.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Herschel/SPIRE images of NGC 891, finding a broken radial dust profile, no extended halo excess, and asymmetric SW dust emission associated with its H I disk.

quotes.quote: We analyse Herschel/SPIRE images of the edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 891 at 250, 350, and 500 m. Using a 3D radiative transfer model we confirm that the dust has radial fall-off similar to the stellar disk. The dust disk shows a break at about 12 kpc from the centre, where the profile becomes steeper.

We have analysed SPIRE observations of the edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 891 and examined both their contribution to the galaxy's SED and the galaxy's morphological properties at these wavelengths. Emission within the central 4 4 broadly follows a radial exponential distribution with a scalelength similar to those derived for the stars in the surface brightness fits of XilourisSub1998 . Emission above the galactic plane can be explained with radiation from the galactic plane picked up by the PSF wings.
With their high resolution, SPIRE observations confirm the excess emission associated with the asymmetric HI disk, already found in deep ISOPHOT observations by PopescuA&A2003b .

sources.summary: The paper reports ZEUS mid-J CO and [C I] detections in NGC 891's nucleus and models them with H2 data, finding warm molecular gas is mainly shock-excited rather than PDR-heated.

quotes.quote: We have detected the CO(6-5), CO(7-6), and [ C I ] 370 m lines from the nuclear region of NGC 891 with our submillimeter grating spectrometer ZEUS on the CSO. These lines provide constraints on photodissociation region (PDR) and shock models that have been invoked to explain the H _ 2 S(0), S(1), and S(2) lines observed with Spitzer. We analyze our data together with the H _ 2 lines, CO(3-2), and IR continuum from the literature using a combined PDR/shock model. We find that the mid-J CO originates almost entirely from shock-excited warm molecular gas; contributions from PDRs are negligible.

Due to its proximity (9.5 Mpc), and edge-on presentation i 89^ ; Sancisi.Allen.1979 , NGC 891 is the primary target for studies of the effects of energetic processes associated with star formation on the interstellar medium in normal spiral galaxies. The edge-on view of NGC 891 results in greatly enhanced gas columns so that relatively weak lines such as the low-lying quadrupole rotational transitions of H _ 2 become observable. The mid-IR H _ 2 rotational transitions in NGC 891 were studied for the first time with ISO Valentijn.vdWerf.1999 and then more recently with Spitzer Stacey.Charmandaris.Boulanger.2010 .

sources.summary: The paper measures metal absorption in NGC 891's halo, finding near-solar or supersolar depleted-corrected abundances that favor disk-origin fountain gas over low-metallicity accretion.

quotes.quote: The edge-on galaxy NGC 891 is similar to the Milky Way in galaxy type and optical luminosity and it lies close enough (10 Mpc) that it has been mapped extensively in several ISM components, including , X-ray emitting gas, and warm ionized gas RandKulkarniHester90,BregmanPildis1994,Rand97,SwatersSancisivanderHulst97,Healdetal2006 . A heroic 21 cm observation Oosterlooetal2007 is deep enough to map low column densities to heights as great as 22 kpc above the plane, and 9 \ of \ gas lies in the halo. They argue that the \ halo is a combination of infalling material and a galactic fountain.
Gas being accreted onto NGC 891 is unlikely to have near-solar abundances.

Consequently, we conclude that the gas is from the disk of the galaxy and it has been brought up to a height of 5 kpc by galactic disk processes. This favors a galactic fountain for the origin of the metal-bearing gas. If the disk metallicity in NGC 891 is similar to the Milky Way, this sight line above the inner disk should have a metallicity above the solar value.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 891 as a clean edge-on case with deep HI observations, a massive extraplanar component, and little detached HI beyond its disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 891 is instead a clean case. This perfectly edge-on galaxy is located 9.5 Mpc away from us and has a systemic velocity incompatible with Milky Way's HVCs. It has been observed in a campaign carried out with the Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope for more than 200 hours, and is one of the deepest HI observations ever made.

A large extraplanar component has been detected amounting to about 25 of the total HI mass, or 1.2 10^ 9 . Most of this gas is located within 8 from the disc with a filament extending up to about 20 . Although we now regard NGC 891 as a remarkable and likely extreme case of the phenomenon of extraplanar gas, several other galaxies observed with the right sensitivity also show the presence of this component .
The second, more remarkable fact is that around NGC 891, beyond 10-20 there is basically no emission detected anywhere.

sources.summary: The paper presents H-band imaging polarimetry of edge-on NGC 891, finding weak disk polarization, asymmetric nulls, and evidence for dichroic mid-plane polarization transitioning to scattered light off-plane.

quotes.quote: The edge-on galaxy NGC 891 was probed using near-infrared (NIR) imaging polarimetry in the H band (1.6  m) with the Mimir instrument on the 1.8 m Perkins Telescope. Polarization was detected with signal-to-noise ratio greater than three out to a surface brightness of 18.8 mag arcsec ^ 2 . The unweighted average and dispersion in polarization percentage ( P ) across the full disk were 0.7 and 0.3 , respectively, and the same quantities for polarization position angle (P.A.) were 12 \ and 19 , respectively. At least one polarization null point, where P falls nearly to zero, was detected in the NE disk but not the SW disk.

Several other asymmetries in P between the northern and southern disk were found and may be related to spiral structure. Profiles of P and P.A. along the minor axis of NGC 891 suggest a transition from magnetic (B) field tracing dichroic polarization near the disk mid-plane to scattering dominated polarization off the disk mid-plane. A comparison between NIR P.A. and radio (3.6 cm) synchrotron polarization P.A. values revealed similar B-field orientations in the central-northeast region, which suggests that the hot plasma and cold, star-forming interstellar medium may share a common B-field .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 891 as a nearly edge-on comparison galaxy with higher star formation, higher far-infrared luminosity, an unstable inner disk, and a thicker CO disk.

quotes.quote: The star formation rate in these galaxies seems relatively low compared to the SFR in NGC 891 (Paper I). also found that NGC 4565 and 5907 show lower star formation than NGC 891. The far-infrared luminosity at 40--400 \ by IRAS (often used as a tracer for SFR) is 10.18, 10.03, 9.61, 9.80 in log _ 10 (L/L_ ) for NGC 891, 4157, 4565, and 5907, respectively ( 2003AJ....126.1607S ). Inclination and Gas Disk Thickness Unlike NGC 891, whose inclination is known to be > 89 ( 2007AJ....134.1019O ), the other galaxies in our sample (NGC 4157, 4565, and 5907) appear less inclined.

In this regime, two galaxies (NGC 891 and 4157) show unstable regions predicting star formation in the inner disk, while the other galaxies (NGC 4565 and 5907) show a marginally stable disk. Considering the relatively low SFR in NGC 4565 and 5907 compared to the SFR in NGC 891 and 4157, the Q_ tot profile with constant velocity dispersions shows a reasonable prediction for the massive star formation.
The strongly star-forming galaxies (NGC 891 and NGC 4157) show a thicker CO disk compared to the other galaxies, suggesting a correlation between the disk thickness and star formation activity.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 891 as a benchmark edge-on spiral, using multiwavelength infrared data to measure thin and thick dust components and infer dust properties above the midplane.

quotes.quote: NGC 891 has been studied for decades and represents one of the best studied cases of an edge-on galaxy. We use deep PACS data together with IRAC, MIPS and SPIRE data to study the vertical extent of dust emission around NGC 891. We also test the presence of a more extended, thick dust component.

For all wavelengths considered the emission is best fit with the sum of a thin and a thick dust component. The scaleheight of both dust components shows a gradient passing from 70 m to 250 m. This could be due to a drop in dust heating (and thus dust temperature) with the distance from the plane, or to a sizable contribution ( 15 - 80 ) of an unresolved thin disc of hotter dust to the observed surface brightness at shorter wavelengths. The scaleheight of the thick dust component, using observations from 70 m to 250 m has been estimated to be (1.44 0.12) kpc, consistent with previous estimates (extinction and scattering in optical bands and MIR emission).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 891 as the edge-on reference for the [N II] 205 micron and 24 micron relation, comparing its correlation with other galaxies and combining it with M51 for normal star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: In a recent study of the nearby edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 891, hughes2014a exploited such an empirical relationship to predict the \ 205 m line emission from a higher resolution 24 m image. Although this appears to be a powerful and promising technique for estimating the \ 205 m line emission from more widely available Spitzer MIPS data, the underlying nature of the correlation is still unknown due in part to uncertainties associated with observations of high inclination systems. Integration along the line-of-sight through an edge-on spiral galaxy will include a range of physical environments, from active star-forming \ regions to diffuse ionised gas, making it difficult to interpret the origin of the relationship. In this paper, we thus attempt to assess whether the \ 205 -- 24 m correlation has some physical origin or is merely an artefact of line-of-sight projection effects in an edge-on disc. We search for the presence of a correlation in Herschel and Spitzer observations of face-on and interacting galaxies, and compare our findings to the NGC 891 correlation of hughes2014a .

We thus combine the NGC 891 and M51 data, representing normal star-forming regions, to define the best-fit relation as _ 10 _ SFR = (0.79 0.05) _ 10 F_ [ NII ] 205 + (4.60 0.05) and valid in the ranges of 8.9< _ 10 F_ [ NII ] 205 <-7.5 and 2.4< _ 10 _ SFR <-1.2 , where units are as stated previously.
In this paper, we aimed to test whether the \ 205 -- 24 m emission correlation observed in NGC 891 arises from line-of-sight projection effects in an edge-on disc.

sources.summary: The paper probes NGC 891's gaseous halo with background AGN sightlines, measuring warm ionized gas, depletion patterns, low-metallicity clouds, and possible accretion or fountain origins.

quotes.quote: The nearby edge-on galaxy NGC 891 has a multi-component gaseous halo and a background AGN (LQAC 035+042 003) projected 5 kpc above the disk near the minor axis. Against the UV continuum of this AGN, we detect lines from 13 ions associated with NGC 891 in new HST /COS spectra. Most of the absorption is from the warm ionized gas with T=4.22 0.04 , n_ H =-1.26 0.51 , and N_ H =20.81 0.20 .

This low metallicity material could be the cold mode accretion from IGM or the tidal disruption of satellites in the NGC 891 halo. An absorption line study of this transition gas in an edge-on galaxy is possible for NGC 891, where a background AGN (LQAC 035+042 003) projects to 5 kpc above the disk and for the inner part of the galaxy Bregman:2013aa .

We analyzed the HST /COS spectra to detect the gaseous components in the NGC 891 halo either close to the minor axis (LQAC 035+042 003) or further away along the major axis (3C 66A). Three absorption systems are detected in these two sightlines.

sources.summary: The paper revisits NGC 891 FUSE halo observations, reporting a marginal O VI detection in field 2 and contrasting its weaker emission with NGC 4631.

quotes.quote: NGC 891 is a nearby, edge-on unbarred spiral galaxy at a distance of 9.1 0.4 Mpc 2011RadburnSmith , but unlike NGC 4631, does not have any large companions. NGC 891 is usually compared to our own Milky Way galaxy, in both size and luminosity. There have been numerous studies of the halo of NGC 891 2005Temple,2018HodgesKluck,2019Qu,2020Das , which indicate the presence of both neutral hydrogen, a hot halo, and warm ionized gas of less than solar metallicity.

NGC 891 fig:spec_all shows the Day+Night spectra of three NGC 891 fields (1,2,3) observed by LWRS. Previously all three fields were considered 1032 non-detections. In this re-visit of the spectra, we find a 3.2 signal of 1032 line emission at field 2, although this is only a marginal detection.
We find stronger emission in NGC 4631 than in NGC 891.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 891 as an edge-on spiral and SZ outlier whose halo signal rises to about 200 kpc, implying an unusually large hot-gas mass likely tied to a group.

quotes.quote: NGC 891: An edge-on spiral (Sb) that is similar to the Milky Way and lies in a modest group, whose main member is suggested to be NGC 1023, although NGC 891 has a slightly brighter K band luminosity; they are separated by more than 1 Mpc. NGC 1023, at D = 10.88 Mpc, lies beyond the 10 Mpc limit and is not included in this survey.
There is a clear outlier in the SZ signal, where NGC 891 lies 4.6 from the median line defined by the weighted mean of the other 11 galaxies (Figure fig:S1vsNGC ).

We also consider the signals from the outlier system, NGC 891, where the normalization S_1 is six times larger than the stack of 11 galaxies. The signal Y() rises up steadily until 200 kpc, at which point it flattens to a value of Y() 2.6 10^ 2 kpc ^2 , about 5 times larger than for the stack of 11 galaxies (Figure fig:NGC891Ytot ). At 250 kpc, the signal is Y = 2.51 0.31 (stat) 0.27 (sys) 10^ 2 kpc ^2 ; the sum of the two errors is 0.41 10^ 2 kpc ^2 . The rise is steeper than for the stack of 11 galaxies and is more consistent with = 0.5 (for d = 2 and r_ cut = 200 kpc, = 0.53 0.10 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 891 as a nearby edge-on spiral template, matching its magnetic-field observations before scaling field strength and geometry to the GRB host galaxy.

quotes.quote: It was found that, in addition to the plane-parallel field, X-shaped halo field is prominent. Note that stacked observations of halos of various galaxies suggest that the presence of this field component is general Xshape-general . At the same time, the halo field in the model of Pshirkov-GMF is purely toroidal, see Appendix a:field:toro . We introduce the additional X-shape component to the field model.

Given rich results of the field measurements, it would be possible to construct a much more precise model for NGC 891, but for our purposes the present toy model is sufficient: we plan to adopt it for HG, for which there are no chance to observe the spiral-arm structure.

The proportionality B ( SFR )^ 1/3 scaling-origin-simple was expected for nearby galaxies; see scaling-origin-detailed for a detailed recent discussion and further references. Simple equipartition arguments see e.g. equip-new suggest that B ( SFR /v)^ 1/2 , where v is the escape velocity of cosmic rays.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 891 as the prototype edge-on spiral whose SED model required young stars and additional dust, then compares its star-formation density with four similar systems.

quotes.quote: In short, we used the intrinsic spatial distribution of dust and stars (in the B, V, I, J, K bands) derived for this galaxy by X99 as a constraint on the old stellar population and part of the dust distribution. In order to fully explain the optical-FIR-submm SED, it was found necessary to add to the components of the X99's solution both a young stellar population (to correct for a shortfall in FIR brightness in terms of re-radiated non-ionizing UV) and more dust mass (to correct for a shortfall in submm brightness). Our solution explained both the observed SED in the FIR and submm, as well as the observed radial profile at 850 m. We found that the dust is predominanly heated by the young stellar population.

Using tools previously described and applied to the prototype galaxy NGC 891, we model the optical to far-infrared spectral energy distributions (SED) of four additional edge-on spiral galaxies, namely NGC 5907, NGC 4013, UGC 1082 and UGC 2048. Comparing the model predictions with IRAS and, where available, sub-millimeter and millimeter observations, we determine the respective roles of the old and young stellar populations in grain heating. In all cases, the young population dominates, with the contribution of the old stellar population being at most 40 , as previously found for NGC 891.

sources.summary: The paper measures NGC 891 diffuse ionized gas halo kinematics, finding a vertical azimuthal velocity gradient near 15 km s^-1 kpc^-1 consistent with neutral gas.

quotes.quote: We present WIYN SparsePak observations of the diffuse ionized gas (DIG) halo of NGC 891. Preliminary results of an analysis of the halo velocity field reveal a clear gradient of the azimuthal velocity with z which agrees with results for the neutral gas. The magnitude of the gradient has been determined, using two independent methods, to be approximately 15 km s ^ 1 kpc ^ 1 .

Rotation curves were derived for each PV diagram using the envelope tracing method (e.g., ). Because NGC 891 is nearly edge-on ( i > 88 ^ ; ), the edge of each line profile furthest from the systemic velocity (the ``envelope'') corresponds to gas at the line of nodes; this velocity is thus approximately the azimuthal velocity at that R . The azimuthal velocity is found by ( ): v_ az =(v_ env v_ sys )/ (i)- _ inst ^2+ _ gas ^2 , where v_ env is the velocity at the location of the envelope, _ inst is the velocity resolution of the instrument, and _ gas is the velocity dispersion of the gas. Azimuthal velocity curves were constructed from the PV diagrams, and are shown in Figure figure:rotcurs . Extinction in the midplane prevented reliable velocities from being found for z 0 ^ . It is clear from Figure figure:rotcurs that the derived azimuthal velocities decrease with height.

sources.summary: The paper presents deep WSRT observations of NGC 891, describing its nearby edge-on disk, multi-phase halo, extended H I spur, lop-sided disk, and slowly rotating halo gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 891 is one of the best known and studied nearby edge-on galaxies. It is at the distance of 9.5 Mpc, classified as a Sb/SBb, and it is often referred to as very similar to the Milky Way (e.g. \ ). NGC 891 has been the subject of numerous studies at different wavelengths that have led to the detection of various halo components: an extended radio halo ( ), a thick layer of diffuse ionised gas (DIG) (e.g. \ ), and diffuse extra-planar hot gas ( ).

New observations of NGC 891 have been obtained with the Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope (WSRT) with a total integration time of about 200 hrs ( ). Fig. \ 1 shows the new total map of NGC 891 in contours overlaid on a grey-scale DSS optical image. This halo is remarkably extended with a spur of gas reaching a distance of 15 kpc from the plane. The disc of NGC 891 is lop-sided, more extended on the S-W side of the galaxy (right in Fig. \ 1) and possibly truncated on the N-E (left) side (see also ; ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 891 as an edge-on spiral with an extended, massive, slowly rotating neutral-gas halo that pure galactic-fountain models cannot reproduce.

quotes.quote: New deep \ observations of the nearby edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 891 obtained with the Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope show an extended and massive (25 of the total \ mass) halo component of neutral gas oos07 . Fig. \ f_totalHI shows the total \ map of NGC 891 (countours + white shade) with overlaid an optical image (grey-scale). The map is rotated 67 degrees counter-clock wise with respect to the on-sky orientation. The neutral gas everywhere extends to large heights ( z 8 kpc) from the plane. Some features, in particular a long filament, extend to much larger distances ( 20 kpc from the plane).
The kinematics of the halo gas in NGC 891 is characterized by a regular decrease in rotational velocity with z (gradient of 15 ) fra05 .

We have seen that: 1) the spiral galaxy NGC 891 is surrounded by a massive halo of neutral gas extending up to more than 8 kpc from the plane; 2) extraplanar gas such as that in NGC 891 seems to be common in spiral galaxies and is analogous to the HVCs of the Milky Way; 3) models purely based on a galactic fountain, with or without interaction with the hot corona, fail to reproduce the data.

sources.summary: The paper argues NGC 891 anchors a line of higher-redshift galaxies toward NGC 1260, suggesting it ejected material, companions, and possibly the SN2006gy progenitor along its minor axis.

quotes.quote: The strongest line of galaxies, however, consists mostly of E's and S0's originating from the large Sb (NGC 891) and ending on NGC 1260 and the supernova SN2006gy. All the galaxies with redshifts 4800 < cz < 6000 as given in NED are shown here in Figure 1. Although the galaxies in the line originating in NGC 891 are predominantly in the 5300 to 5700 km/sec range of redshift, the redshift of NGC891 itself is only cz = 528 km/sec. It is intriguing to note that it would imply a distance closer by a factor 10 and luminosity smaller by about a factor of 100 for the supernova if its progenitor had been ejected out of NGC 891 along with some of the higher redshift material.

But I believe there is enough evidence that large galaxies like NGC 891 eject material which becomes higher redshift companions that I would argue for the ejection of the supernova progenitor in that process. That NGC 891 could have ejected the extremely active radio, X-ray, infra red galaxy Perseus A (NGC 1275) is suggested by the elongation of the perseus A cluster back along the line to NGC 891. Moreover the NGC 891 minor axis is only about 18 deg. off the line to Per A.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 891 as an edge-on galaxy with X-shaped magnetic fields, smooth rotation measures, an even axisymmetric spiral field, and an outward large-scale field direction.

quotes.quote: As an example we show the magnetic field vectors of the edge-on galaxie NGC 891 together with the radio continuum emission at 3.6 cm (made with the Effelsberg 100-m telescope The Effelsberg 100-m telescope is operated by the ailsshMax-Planck-Institut f u Radioastronomie (MPIfR) in Bonn. overlayed on an optical image of the galaxy in Fig. n891 . The rotation measures (RM) determined between our 3.6 cm-map and the 6.2 cm-map of Sukumar Allen (1991) vary smoothly along the major axis of the galaxy with positive values in the southern part and negative in the northern part of the galaxy. This indicates a large-scale toroidal field within the galactic disk. As the RMs have equal signs above and below the disk along the major axis, the azimuthal magnetic field in NGC 891 seems to be an even axisymmetric spiral field (ASS) (Krause 1990) as generated by a mean-field dynamo (cf. Sect. sec:regular field ).

From the HI- and CO-line observations (Rupen 1991, Garc a-Burillo Gu e lin 1995) we know that the rotation velocities in NGC 891 are also negative in the northern part of the galaxy and positive in the southern one. Assuming trailing optical spiral arms (and a magnetic field orientation primarily parallel to the opical arms as observed in face-on galaxies) we can infer that the direction of the large-scale ASS field in NGC 891 is outwards .

sources.summary: The paper presents an HST/ACS search for NGC 891 globular clusters and describes the nearby edge-on late-type disk as a Milky Way-like system with thick disk, halo populations, and resolved halo stars.

quotes.quote: We use deep images taken with the Advanced Camera for Surveys on board the Hubble Space Telescope of the disk galaxy NGC 891, to search for globular cluster candidates. This galaxy has long been considered to be a close analog in size and structure to the Milky Way and is nearly edge-on, facilitating studies of its halo population.

NGC 891, a nearby large late-type disk galaxy, has often been described in shorthand as a ``clone'' of the Milky Way, since it has a very similar total luminosity, bulge size, and disk with prominent dust lanes e.g. vanderkruit1984 . A study of surface-brightness photometry mor97 revealed the presence of an extensive thick disk. Its orientation almost precisely edge-on to our line of sight makes it particularly attractive for studies of its disk and halo stellar populations, facilitating comparative studies of components such as the thick disk, stellar streams and substructures, and the total visible mass and extent of the halo. In addition, NGC 891 is close enough to us that HST imaging is easily capable of resolving the halo stars, enabling direct star-by-star statistical studies of its old stellar populations.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 891 as an edge-on spiral with counter-rotating clouds, lagging extraplanar gas, and a high-column-density filament useful for testing infall and outflow mixing.

quotes.quote: In particular, the counter-rotating clouds seen near the edge-on spiral NGC 891 are compelling examples of this infall (Oosterloo et al. 2007). However, they also argue that the rotation curves of extraplanar \ provide indirect evidence for the accretion of matter onto NGC 891 and other galaxies.

2007) for gas above the plane of NGC 891 are best explained by the addition of low angular momentum neutral material into the emitting regions. While the majority of the emitting gas at high- \ is likely still associated with galactic fountain-type circulation, the 10 \ or so of the gas that is newly accreted in their model is flowing in at a rate nearly equal to the star formation rate.

However, the lagging \ rotation is seen in NGC 891 at heights as low as z 4-5 kpc. These heights may be accessible to \ observations for some nearby galaxies. In addition, the nature of anomalous velocity clouds or high column density clouds at large projected heights, such as the 22 kpc filament seen in NGC 891 (Oosterloo et al. 2007), could be tested using the IR/ \ ratio.

sources.summary: PN.S observations of NGC 891 trace planetary-nebula number counts through the disk, detect objects beyond the thick-disk transition, and find no kinematic evidence that the thick disk is a distinct component.

quotes.quote: The upper panel shows the number counts of PNe detected in the edge-on spiral NGC 891 as a function of distance from its plane. The lines show a possible decomposition of the disk into thin and thick exponential components Morrisonetal97 . The data drop below the model close to the plane, as PNe are difficult to detect against the high surface brightness of the galaxy in this region, and some PNe will also be lost in NGC 891's strong dust lane. However, in the region we are interested in away from the plane, the PN number counts trace the light distribution very well, underlining their nature as generic tracers of the stellar population. Further, we are clearly detecting them in significant numbers out to beyond the transition where the thick disk becomes the dominant component.
Clearly, the data are essentially consistent with this simple single-component model: there is no kinematic evidence that the thick disk in NGC 891 is in any way a distinct entity.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 891 as a key nearby disk example with substantial extra-planar neutral gas, a measured rotational lag, a long H I filament, and fountain-driven accretion modeling.

quotes.quote: Extra-planar neutral gas is detected in edge-on galaxies at distances of up to 10-20 kpc from the plane and, in the case of NGC 891, it amounts to about 25 of all the gas in this galaxy (see Fig. \ f891 ). Table tExtraplanar summarizes the best studied galaxies so far. The main kinematical feature of the extra-planar gas is its decreasing rotation velocity with increasing height from the plane (vertical rotational gradient).

NGC 891 has a long filament ( M_ HI 1.6 10^7 ) extending up to about 20 kpc from the plane of the disk (Fig. \ f891 ). NGC 2403 also has a filament with a similar \ mass located in projection outside the bright optical disk (Fig. \ f2403 ).

For NGC 891 we found a best-fit accretion rate of about 3 (see curve in Fig. \ f891 ). This model also predicts that most of the extra-planar gas is produced by the galactic fountain and only a small fraction (about 10 ) is extragalactic.

sources.summary: HALOGAS uses NGC 891 as the benchmark for a massive neutral-gas halo, emphasizing its 22 kpc extent, large H I fraction, filaments, lagging rotation, and likely accretion signatures.

quotes.quote: Indeed, deep observations of a few nearby spirals -- most notably the edge-on NGC 891 oosterloo_etal_2007 , the moderately-inclined NGC 2403 fraternali_etal_2002 , and the face-on NGC 6946 boomsma_etal_2008 - have shown that large quantities of cold gas exist in their halos, at large vertical distances from the thin disk. In the case of NGC 891, observations with the Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope (WSRT) revealed a huge neutral gas halo which extends up to a vertical distance of 22 kpc, with massive filamentary structures far from the disk. In total, the \ halo of NGC 891 contains almost 30 of the galaxy's total \ mass.

It has been pointed out that if the largest \ filament in the halo of NGC 891 was created by disk star formation, an energy equivalent to 10^5 supernovae would be required. This suggests an external origin instead oosterloo_etal_2007 . A final piece of evidence suggestive of accretion in NGC 891 comes in the form of small, 10^6 M_ clouds which are counter-rotating in the halo.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 891 as an instructive edge-on galaxy for PAH halo survival, estimating cosmic-ray gradients and PAH lifetimes at high galactic latitudes.

quotes.quote: The study of NGC 891, the edge-on twin of the Milky Way, is particularly instructive. While the vertical distribution of the 8 m PAHs emission is noticeably narrower than that of the cold dust radiating at 450 m, modeling reveals that this likely reflects the variation of the stellar population from the disk to the halo; e.g., the dominant (FUV) heating sources of the PAHs are located in the disk while the old stellar population, which contributes substantially to the heating of the dust, extends some 3 kpc above the disk whaley09 . Some of the PAH molecules, as well as the dust, present at high galactic latitudes may originate from mass losing AGB stars in the halo.

Because of its similarity with the Milky Way, we adopt for NGC 891 the same diffusion scale height z_ D = 2.5 kpc. This implies that the latitudinal gradient from Eq. latGrad_eq is the same for both galaxies. The CR intensity variation in NGC 891 with respect to the solar neighborhood is given by the following expression I_ i, NGC 891 (0, |z|, E) I_ i, MW (r_ , 0, E) = (1.5 3.8 3 ) |z|/z_ D where 1.5 is the CR enhancement factor in the galactic center with respect to the solar neighborhood and the factor 3.8/3 takes into account the increased cosmic-ray intensity in NGC 891 due to its higher star formation rate 3.8 M _ yr ^ 1 , popescu04 with respect to the Milky Way 3 M _ yr ^ 1 , scalo86, pran98 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 891 as a well-studied edge-on spiral with an extended multi-phase halo, substantial halo H I, neutral gas reaching 8-10 kpc, and a filament extending to 20 kpc.

quotes.quote: Spectacular examples for galaxies with extended gaseous H i halos are the nearby spiral galaxies NGC 891 and NGC 262 (e.g., Swaters, Sancisi, van der Hulst, 1997; Oosterloo, Fraternali, Sancisi, 2007). NGC 891 is one of the best-studied edge-on galaxies. Observations in different wavelength regimes (Whaley et al., 2009, and references therein) revealed the existence of an extended radio halo, an extended layer of diffuse ionised and hot-ionised gas (e.g., Bregman Pildis, 1994).

Deep H i observations with the Westerbork Radio Sythesis Telescope (WSRT) were performed by Oosterloo, Fraternali, Sancisi, 2007 and they found that 25 of the total H i mass resides in the halo. The neutral gas extends to large heights of z 8 10 kpc. A long filament extends even up to z 20 kpc .

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 891 has a UV halo interpreted as an extragalactic reflection nebula, centrally concentrated UV light, matching soft X-ray and UV structure, and convincing 160 micron and PAH halos.

quotes.quote: This conclusion was reinforced by hutton14 , and seon14 detected a UV halo around NGC 891 that they argue is also an eRN.

For example, in NGC 891, NGC 2683, and NGC 3044 most of the light is concentrated near the center of the galaxy (Figure figure.gallery_normal ), whereas in NGC 4216 or NGC 5907 the surface brightness is comparable across most of the disk.

The soft X-ray halos correspond reasonably well to the UV halo structure in NGC 891, NGC 2683, and NGC 4217, but not in NGC 5170. There are fewer normal galaxies with X-ray data than with H images, and only four with X-ray halos, so it is unclear how well the morphology matches the UV. The extraplanar 160 m emission coincides with the UV, but it is not always as extensive. NGC 55, NGC 891, and NGC 4096 have the most convincing 160 m halos, but their prominence does not appear to be connected to that in H or the radio continuum. Likewise, the prominence of the radio halos seems unrelated to the prominence of the UV halo or extraplanar emission in other bands. The only normal galaxies in mccormick13 with extraplanar PAH emission are NGC 55 and NGC 891. In the former case, the PAHs do not follow the UV halo, whereas in the latter they do.

sources.summary: The chapter uses NGC 891 as a benchmark edge-on spiral with massive extraplanar neutral gas, lagging rotation, and fountain accretion requiring condensation.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:ngc891 shows deep observations (blue shade and contours) of the edge-on spiral NGC 891 overlaid on an optical image Oosterloo+2007 . The layer extends above the plane to remarkable distances with a scale-height h 2.2 . The total mass of located outside the disc is about 1.2 10^9 , which corresponds to almost 30 of the total mass. This galaxy is likely to be on the high end of the spectrum of extraplanar gas properties, in particular for its large extraplanar mass.

The main feature of the extraplanar gas of NGC 891 is the lag in rotation that is not reproduced by a simple galactic fountain model (Fig. \ fig:fountainN891 b). The introduction of accretion onto fountain clouds following eq. \ eq:mdot produces the nice match with the data shown by the blue curve in Fig. \ fig:fountainN891 .

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 891 with NGC 4565, finding corrected C-band maps and modeling that indicate NGC 891's halo cosmic-ray transport is probably advective with an accelerating wind.

quotes.quote: The two nearby spiral galaxies, NGC 891 and NGC 4565, differ largely in their detectable halo extent and their star formation rates. The question is how these differences are related to the (advective and/or diffusive) CRE transport in the disk and halo. In C-band, the total power emission of both galaxies has been corrected for missing zero spacing flux with the single-dish 100-m Effelsberg telescope. After subtracting off the thermal emission, maps of the total magnetic field strength and the nonthermal spectral indices as well as vertical nonthermal scale heights at different radii could be determined XVI .

XVI found that the CRE transport in the halo of NGC 891 is probably dominated by advection with an accelerated galactic wind reaching escape velocity at a height of 9 to 17 kpc. This wind is likely to coexist with diffusion-dominated regions.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 891 patchy at 530 pc resolution, more regular after smoothing, with wavelet peaks tracing smaller magnetic structures and large-scale polarized disk emission.

quotes.quote: Also NGC 891 shows a patchy structure at scales larger than the HPBW of 12 (corresponding to 530 pc) in their magnetic field vectors and their RM values, although it was referred to as a kind of prototype of an X-field structure by observations at the 100-m telescope at Effelsberg at much lower resolution of 84  HPBW krause2009 . Again, we smoothed our Q- and U-cubes to 20  HPBW (corresponding to 880 pc) before RM-synthesis. The corresponding results are presented in Fig.  n891_20all . The magnetic field angles as well as the RM-values show again a more regular pattern that is not only due to a higher signal-to-noise in the smoothed maps. This is best visible in the RM-maps (panel 4 and panel 5) in the central part of NGC 891.

The second peaks in the power spectra are located at scales of about 1.5 (  4 kpc) and 3.8 (  8 kpc) in NGC 891 and NGC 4631, respectively, are due to the large-scale polarized emission from the disk.
Also the merged version of NGC 891 shows a vertical monotonically increasing degree of polarization up to distances of 5 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper reports HAWC+ FIR polarimetry of NGC 891, finding disk-parallel magnetic vectors, deeper disk sampling than NIR or radio, low polarization in some arms, and tentative vertical fields.

quotes.quote: For NGC 891, the FIR polarization vectors within an intensity contour of 1500 MJy sr^ 1 are oriented very close to the plane of the galaxy. The FIR polarimetry is probably sampling the magnetic field geometry in NGC 891 much deeper into the disk than is possible with NIR polarimetry and radio synchrotron measurements. In some locations in NGC 891 the FIR polarization is very low, suggesting we are preferentially viewing the magnetic field mostly along the line of sight, down the length of embedded spiral arms. There is tentative evidence for a vertical field in the polarized emission off the plane of the disk.

M51 and NGC 891 provide two well studied examples of nearly face-on (M51) and edge-on (NGC 891) galaxies. We are interested in probing the magnetic field geometry in these two systems to compare far-infrared (FIR) observations with optical, near-infrared (NIR) and radio observations, and to search for clues to the mechanism(s) for generating and sustaining magnetic fields in spiral galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper uses Herschel SPIRE FTS feature-finder data to measure NGC 891 gas rotation, finding N II and C I profiles follow H I and imply linked neutral and ionized ISM regions.

quotes.quote: In this letter we demonstrate the use of the FF information extracted from three observations of the edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 891 to measure the rotation of N II and C I gas at Far-infrared frequencies in complement to radio observations of the [ H I ] line and the CO(1-0) transition. We find that measurements of both N II and C I gas follow a similar velocity profile to that of H I showing a correlation between neutral and ionized regions of the interstellar medium (ISM) in the disk of NGC 891.

NGC 891 is a nearby ( D 9.5 Mpc for H_0 = 75 km s^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ) edge-on spiral galaxy (inclination angle 89^ ) and is similar in structure to the Milky Way vanDerKuit1981 . CO observations of the galaxy have shown a 4 kpc molecular ring and a high velocity disk surrounding the nucleus Garcia1992, Scoville1993, Sofue1996 . [ H I ] measurements of this galaxy are more extend ( R>10 kpc) and both CO and [ H I ] measurements show some asymmetries with the south end of the galaxy being more extended than the north end Sancisi1979, Sofue1996, Kijeong2011 .
Our results suggest that such a correlation exists within the disk of NGC 891.

sources.summary: The paper reports bright AGB stars and clear tidal-stream substructure in NGC 891 halo, with higher AGB/RGB ratios in stream and major-axis fields than the inner halo.

quotes.quote: NGC 891 The panoramic view of NGC 891 presented in shows abundant stellar halo substructure (very apparent in Fig. \ substructuremap ), most notably a large tidal stream that appears to wrap around the galaxy more than once. The GHOSTS HST fields are oriented on the side of NGC 891 with more limited coverage, but it is clear that Fields 5, 6, and 7 overlap with overdensities associated with NGC 891's tidal stream in both Fig. \ substructuremap and from the flattening of the surface density profile at those radii in Harmsen17 . The AGB/RGB ratio for this substructure region we found to be 0.99^ +0.05 _ 0.04 , similar to the major axis value of 0.96 0.04 .

Meanwhile, the inner part of the stellar halo gives an AGB/RGB ratio of 1.22^ +0.05 _ 0.07 , which is significantly lower than the other two. M81 (NGC 3031) M81 (NGC 3031) shows much higher uncertainties in the AGB/RGB ratio compared to the other three galaxies due to a significantly smaller number of both AGB and RGB stars, which can be seen in the stacked CMDs in figure Composite CMDs . In spite of the large error bars we are able to obtain ratios for the major axis + M82 halo fields and the minor axis fields.

sources.summary: NGC 891 is paired with NGC 7814 as an edge-on spiral with a similar rotation curve but disk-dominated morphology; RCFM fits both and captures differing outer inflections.

quotes.quote: NGC 7814 and NGC 891 The spiral galaxies NGC 7814 and NGC 891 present an interesting challenge to dark matter and MOND models. Both galaxies are presented edge-on on the sky, and both have essentially identical RCs, but are extreme opposites in morphologies. NGC 7814 is a bulge dominated galaxy and NGC 891 is almost entirely a disk galaxy.

Frat1 asks ``why are these RCs so identical if their dark matter halos are necessarily different to accommodate the differences in the luminous mass?'' In the RCFM paradigm the two RCs are very similar in magnitude, but differ in their inflections at large radii; NGC 7814 inflects up, whereas NGC 891 inflects down. RCFM fits to these galaxies are successful, see Fig. fitCompare7814_891 and Tables  tab:taleOftwoGalaxies7814 and tab:taleOftwoGalaxies .

sources.summary: The paper reports ISO far-infrared detection of cold dust in NGC 891’s extended H I disk, arguing that the disk is metal-enriched rather than primordial.

quotes.quote: The detection was achieved through a dedicated deep Far-Infrared observation of a large field encompassing the entire HI disk of the edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 891. Our discovery indicates that the extended HI disk of NGC 891 is not primordial in origin.

Here we present the first detection of cold dust in an extended HI disk, achieved through a dedicated deep Far-Infrared (FIR) observation of a large field encompassing the entire HI disk of the edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 891, made using the ISOPHOT instrument (Lemke et al. 1996) on board the Infrared Space Observatory (ISO)(Kessler et al. 1996). We chose NGC 891 for this observation as it has an asymmetric HI disk (Swaters et al. 1997), so any FIR counterpart should also be asymmetric and thus be more easily recognisable.

The derived intrinsic distributions of dust and stars in NGC 891 were constrained from the optical/NIR images of NGC 891, as well as using data at 60, 100, 450 850 m (Alton et al. 1998b) and data at 1.3 mm (Guelin et al. 1993). Here we compare the FIR profiles with the HI profiles and concentrate on the emission beyond the optical disk.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 891 to demonstrate dust and infrared-emission modeling, matching optical/NIR images and linking its FIR/submillimeter emission to UV heating and star formation.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:ngc891 shows NGC 891 in five bands and the corresponding model images (Xilouris et al. ). The agreement between the model and the observations is very good. This is seen more clearly in Figure fig:histo , where the residuals between the model and the observations are shown (Xilouris et al. ).

The model accounts (Fig. fig:firmodel , right) not only for the spectral energy distribution (SED) of NGC 891 in the FIR/submm regime (Popescu et al. ), but also for its observed surface brightness at 170 and 200 m (Popescu et al. ). Furthermore, the model showed that the heating of the dust is done mainly by the UV. Thus, the star formation rate in NGC 891 is directly connected to its infrared emission.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 891 as an edge-on spiral with cold dust in its extended HI disk and FIR emission that models attribute mainly to diffuse, UV-powered dust.

quotes.quote: This question was answered in the affirmative by Popescu Tuffs , through dedicated deep FIR maps of a large field encompassing the entire HI disk of the edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 891, made using ISOPHOT at 170 and 200 m (see Fig. 2.). The large amounts of grains found in the extended HI disk of NGC 891 (gas-to-dust ratio of 1 ) clearly shows that this gaseous disk is not primordial, left over from the epoch of galaxy formation. It was suggested that the detected grains could have either been transported from the optical disk (via the halo, using mechanisms such as those proposed by Ferrara , Davies et al. , Popescu et al. or through the action of macro turbulence) or that they could have been produced outside the galaxy (for example transferred in interactions with other galaxies).

At 170 and 200 m the model for NGC 891 predicts that the bulk of the FIR dust emission is from the diffuse component. The close agreement between the data and the model predictions, both in integrated flux densities, but especially in terms of the spatial distribution, constitutes a strong evidence that the large-scale distribution of stellar emissivity and dust predicted by the model is in fact a good representation of NGC 891. In turn, this supports the prediction of the model that the dust emission in NGC 891 is predominantly powered by UV photons.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 891 as a nearby edge-on spiral with extra-planar neutral gas, active star formation, a multiwavelength halo, and HI data used to test galactic-fountain dynamics.

quotes.quote: The (Sb) spiral galaxy NGC 891 is one of the best known and extensively studied nearby edge-on galaxies. We assumed a distance of 9.5 Mpc vdk81b , which leads to a luminosity L_ B 2.6 10^ 10 . The disc of NGC 891 shows intensive star formation at a rate SFR 3.8 pop04 .

The halo region has been studied at various wavelengths and shows a variety of components from radio continuum all78 to hot X-ray emitting diffuse gas bre94 . NGC 891 is also considered to be very similar to the Milky Way vdk84 . NGC 891 has been studied in HI several times with ever increasing sensitivity e.g. san79, rup91, swa97 and most recently with very deep observations (with more than 200 hours of integration) obtained with the Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope (WSRT) oos05 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 891 as a well-studied spiral radio halo, noting its integrated nonthermal spectral index and halo steepening with distance from the disk.

quotes.quote: One of the best studied radio halos is that of the spiral galaxy NGC 891 (Hummel et al. ). Its spatially integrated nonthermal radio spectral index is 0.78 (Niklas et al. ), only slightly below the average spectral index of galaxies (see Fig  figtwo ). In Fig.  figfive we show its radio spectral index distribution in the halo.

The data are at a resolution of 40'', corresponding to 1.8 kpc at the distance of 9.5 kpc of NGC 891 . The spectrum steepens very quickly within the first 2-3 kpc. It continues to steepen more slowly outside this range, especially in the eastern side of the halo.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 891's extraplanar infrared emission, finding enhanced halo [Ne III]/[Ne II], inadequate pure photo-ionization models, and PAH features with rough vertical scale heights.

quotes.quote: We present infrared spectroscopy from the Spitzer Space Telescope at one disk position and two positions at a height of 1 kpc from the disk in the edge-on spiral NGC 891, with the primary goal of studying halo ionization. Our main result is that the [ Ne III ] / [ Ne II ] ratio, which provides a measure of the hardness of the ionizing spectrum free from the major problems plaguing optical line ratios, is enhanced in the extraplanar pointings relative to the disk pointing. Using a 2D Monte Carlo-based photo-ionization code which accounts for the effects of radiation field hardening, we find that this trend cannot be reproduced by any plausible photo-ionization model, and that a secondary source of ionization must therefore operate in gaseous halos. We also present the first spectroscopic detections of extraplanar PAH features in an external normal galaxy. If they are in an exponential layer, very rough emission scale-heights of 330-530 pc are implied for the various features.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 891 as an edge-on spiral whose UV-to-mm SED is reproduced with old stars, extended diffuse dust, clumpy dust, and embedded young UV sources.

quotes.quote: A model is provided for the edge-on galaxy NGC 891. The SED of the galaxy from the UV to the submm/mm range can be well reproduced by: a bulge/disk configuration of old stars together with an extended dust disk, as suggested by the analysis of optical/near-infrared images; a clumpy dust distribution of the same mass as the diffuse dust disk, together with a UV emitting component, half of which in the form of a diffuse disk and half in sources embedded in clouds.

Building on the studies of XilourisSub1998 and PopescuA&A2000 , simulations have been produced for the well known case of the edge-on spiral NGC 891. The main results can be summarised as follows. enumi The global SED of NGC 891 from the UV to the mm can be reproduced by a model including: a diffuse dust disk, more widespread than the stellar and of moderate optical depth ( ^ f. o. _ V =1); a clumpy dust distribution, of mass similar to that of the diffuse disk; a population of old stars, smoothly distributed in an exponential disk and a bulge, emitting about 75 of the bolometric radiation; a population of young stars emitting the remaining fraction mainly in the UV, roughly half of which embedded in clouds.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 891 as the benchmark gaseous-halo case, noting its extended H I halo, non-unique status, and lagging halo gas.

quotes.quote: The discovery of a very extended H i halo in NGC 891 has shown that 21 cm observations are a powerful tool to study the structure and kinematics of gaseous halos. Since this discovery 1997ApJ...491..140S many investigations have followed studying this 2007AJ....134.1019O and other galaxies. Investigations at 21 cm, as well as other wavelengths, have shown that NGC 891 is not a special case in having a gaseous halo 2000A&A...356L..49S,2001A&A...377..759L,2003A&A...406..505R,2005A&A...439..947B,2005A&A...436..101W,2008A&A...490..555B .

The gas in the halo of NGC 891 shows rotational velocities that are lower then the velocities of the gas in the disk. This 'lag' is observed in H i 2005ASPC..331..239F as well as in H 2006ApJ...647.1018H,2007A&A...468..951K and determined to be linear with a magnitude of 16 km s ^ 1 kpc ^ 1 in the vertical direction.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 891 as a nearly edge-on, Milky-Way-like spiral whose clumpy dust and spiral structure shape attenuation, spectral-energy-distribution fits, and inclination-dependent photometry.

quotes.quote: We produce three-dimensional Monte-Carlo radiative transfer models of the edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 891, a fast-rotating galaxy thought to be an analogue to the Milky Way. The models contain realistic spiral arms and a fractal distribution of clumpy dust. We fit our models to Hubble Space Telescope images corresponding to the B and I bands, using shapelet analysis and a genetic algorithm to generate 30 statistically best-fitting models. These models have a strong preference for spirality and clumpiness, with average face-on attenuation decreasing from 0.24(0.16) to 0.03(0.03) mag in the B(I) band between 0.5 and 2 radial scale-lengths. Most of the attenuation comes from small high-density clumps with low ( 10 ) filling factors.

sources.summary: The paper reports a new ULX candidate in NGC 891, describes the galaxy as a nearby edge-on Milky Way analog, and reviews prior X-ray and supernova monitoring.

quotes.quote: In this Letter, we report the appearance of a new ULX candidate in the nearby isolated, edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 891. The galaxy is thought to be a Milky Way analog in luminosity devaucouleurs91 and color vanderkruit81 , and, like the Galaxy, it is a barred spiral e.g. garcia-burillo95 . In Section 2, we describe the XMM-Newton detection and other observations, in Section 3 we attempt empirical fits to the X-ray spectrum, and in Section 4 we discuss our results in context. Throughout this Letter, we use a Galactic column of N_H = 6.5 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 kalberla05 as well as the Wilkinson Microwave Anisotropy Probe cosmology H_0 = 71 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , _ = 0.73 , and _m = 0.27 ; spergel07 . NGC 891 is about 9-10 Mpc away temple05,tully09 , and we adopt d 9 Mpc. t A new bright source was discovered in a 133 ks XMM-Newton exposure of NGC 891 (obsID 0670950101) with an aperture-corrected 0.3-10 keV EPIC-pn flux of F_X = 1.6 10^ 12 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 891 as a nearby edge-on spiral, reproducing its UV-to-FIR SED and color gradient while finding single-disk models cannot match both UV and FIR emission.

quotes.quote: We investigate the three-dimensional structure of the nearby edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 891 using 3D Monte Carlo radiative transfer models, with realistic spiral structure and fractally clumped dust. Using the spiral and clumpiness parameters found from recently completed scattered light models we produce lower resolution SED models which reproduce the global UV-to-FIR SED of NGC 891. Our models contain a color gradient across the major axis of the galaxy - similar to what is seen in images of the NGC 891.

Single disk models are unable to simultaneously match the UV and FIR SED of NGC 891, even when the dust mass and stellar luminosity are increased or clumpiness and spirality are added. The poor fit from single disk models was also noted by .

sources.summary: The paper resolves NGC 891 with near-infrared imaging, finding a super-thin disk, disk asymmetries from young stars, and a truncated inner bar-like structure.

quotes.quote: We probe the disk structure of the nearby, massive, edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 891 with sub-arcsecond resolution JH band images covering 10 kpc in radius and 5 kpc in height. We measure intrinsic surface brightness profiles using realistic attenuation corrections constrained from near- and mid-infrared ( Spitzer ) color maps and three-dimensional Monte-Carlo radiative-transfer models. In addition to the well-known thin and thick disks, a super-thin disk with 60-80 pc scale-height---comparable to the star-forming disk of the Milky Way---is visibly evident and required to fit the attenuation-corrected light distribution. Asymmetries in the super-thin disk light profile are indicative of young, hot stars producing regions of excess luminosity and bluer (attenuation-corrected) near-infrared color. To fit the inner regions of NGC 891, these disks must be truncated within 3 kpc, with almost all their luminosity redistributed in a bar-like structure 50 thicker than the thin disk.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 891's dust and gas with Herschel, SCUBA, H I, and CO data, deriving global dust mass, temperature, and pixel-scale dust maps.

quotes.quote: We investigate the connection between dust and gas in the nearby edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 891, a target of the Very Nearby Galaxies Survey. High resolution Herschel PACS and SPIRE 70, 100, 160, 250, 350, and 500 m images are combined with JCMT SCUBA 850 m observations to trace the far-infrared/submillimetre spectral energy distribution (SED). Maps of the \ 21 cm line and CO(J=3-2) emission trace the atomic and molecular hydrogen gas, respectively. We fit one-component modified blackbody models to the integrated SED, finding a global dust mass of (8.5 2.0) 10 ^ 7 M _ and an average temperature of 23 2 K, consistent with results from previous far-infrared experiments. We also fit one-component modified blackbody models to pixel-by-pixel SEDs to produce maps of the dust mass and temperature.

sources.summary: The paper builds on and reanalyzes NGC 891 as a MW-like disk with super-thin, thin, and thick components, an inner truncation, bar, and nuclear disk.

quotes.quote: In hereafter Schechtman-Rook13 Schechtman-Rook13 we demonstrated our methods, constructing full spectral energy distribution (SED) RT models of the well-studied MW-like galaxy NGC 891. These models contained multiple stellar populations as well as fractal dust clumps, and were used to compute a general relationship between infrared color and attenuation. When applying these corrections to NIR data for NGC 891, we found not only a super-thin disk ( h_ z 100 pc) but also an inner disk truncation, an exponential-like bar, and a nuclear disk.

It also serves to confirm that NGC 891 and the MW are not alone in their nested multi-component disk structure, in which thinner disk components are also radially smaller. In particular, NGC 4013 appears to have super-thin, thin and thick components with similar luminosity ratios to NGC 891.

sources.summary: The paper uses deep WSRT observations of NGC 891 to show that improved H I sensitivity reveals an extended halo and long filament, mostly tied to disk feedback with possible accretion/minor-interaction contributions.

quotes.quote: Fig. \ fig:n891progress shows three observations of the same galaxy, NGC 891, obtained in the last thirty years with the Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope (WSRT). The improvement in sensitivity from 1 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 (left panel; Sancisi&1979 ) to 1 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 (right panel; oosterloo ) has revealed a completely different picture of the \ distribution in this galaxy. The most recent data have shown the presence of an extended \ halo which everywhere extends up to 8-10 kpc above the disk of NGC 891, and with a long filament extending up to 20 kpc. Most of this gas is likely to be produced by stellar feedback from the disk Fraternali&Binney2008 but a fraction could come from accretion. The long filament is, however, likely due to a minor interaction (cf. \ oosterloo, mapelli ).

sources.summary: The paper bins NGC 0891 fields to comparable spatial scales, finds a stellar-halo colour gradient, and notes a redder minor-axis colour jump near 38 kpc that may trace substructure.

quotes.quote: However, NGC 0891, NGC 4565 and NGC 7814 are at further distances, indicated in Table table:trgb . Therefore, the FoVs of the fields located around these galaxies cover larger linear extensions, having side lengths from 9 to 14 kpc. In order to obtain colour measurements that represent the properties of stars from similar spatial regions, we divide each field of the more distant galaxies in either three or four radial bins, such that each region for which a colour measurement is made covers 3 kpc on a side.

Half of the galaxies (NGC 4565, NGC 0891, and NGC 7814) show fits consistent with stellar halo colour gradients whereas the remaining three galaxies (NGC 0253, NGC 3031, and NGC 4945) have rather flat colour profile. However, it is interesting to note that both NGC 0891 and NGC 4565 show a jump, i.e. a redder colour, in the minor axis colour profile at approximately 38 kpc, which may be related to substructure in these galaxies likely either in the form of a stellar stream or shell.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 891 as the observational comparison for its halo-lag model, showing modeled rotation curves that reasonably reproduce H I data above the disk.

quotes.quote: In Fig.  fig:vphi a, we show V_ as a function of radius for NGC 891. The data, kindly supplied by Dr. Filippo Fraternali see their Fig. 15 oos07 , provided the best model fit to the HI observations in this galaxy. They are shown as the curves with points for the midplane and for three heights, z , above the major axis. The solid curves (blue, green and red) show our values using Eqn.  eq:vphiexp , where V is the underlying measured in-disk rotational velocity (curve at z=0 ). These curves are reasonable representations of the data, given the simplicity of our model.

sources.summary: The paper reports the first quantitative stellar-halo density, axis-ratio, and mass measurements for NGC 891, detecting known stream/cocoon substructure and estimating a total stellar halo mass of 2.7e9 solar masses.

quotes.quote: NGC 891 As far as we are aware, our measurement is the first quantitative measurement of the stellar halo density profile, axis ratio and mass for NGC 891. In particular, the mass of the stellar halo between 10 and 40 projected minor axis equivalent kpc is 8.6^ +0.7 _ 0.5 2.6 10^8 M_ (random and systematic errors respectively). This corresponds to an estimated total stellar halo mass of 2.7 1.2 10^9 M_ , corresponding to 5 2 of NGC 891's total stellar mass. NGC 891 has been imaged using Subaru's SUPRIME-CAM Mouhcine10 , leading to the discovery of extensive stellar streams and a relatively dense `cocoon' of stars in the inner parts of NGC 891's stellar halo (their Fig. \ 1). We clearly detect the stream and cocoon (towards Mouhcine10 's positive Z direction) on the minor axis fields between 25 and 40 kpc, where the density profile is close to flat.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 891 as an edge-on CHANG-ES test galaxy for thermal radio predictions, finding non-thermal spectral steepening and possible 22 micron extinction.

quotes.quote: We analyze the application of star formation rate (SFR) calibrations using H and 22 micron infrared imaging data in predicting the thermal radio component for a test sample of 3 edge-on galaxies (NGC 891, NGC 3044, and NGC 4631) in the Continuum Halos in Nearby Galaxies -- an EVLA Survey (CHANG-ES). We use a mixture of H and 24 micron calibration from calzettietal07 , and a linear 22 micron only calibration from on the test sample. We apply these relations on a pixel-to-pixel basis to create thermal prediction maps in the two CHANG-ES bands: L- and C-band (1.5 GHz and 6.0 GHz, respectively). We analyze the resulting non-thermal spectral index maps, and find a characteristic steepening of the non-thermal spectral index with vertical distance from the disk after application of all methods. We find possible evidence of extinction in the 22 micron data as compared to 70 micron Spitzer Multband Imaging Photometer (MIPS) imaging in NGC 891.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 891 has a dumbbell-shaped synchrotron halo with radial scale-height growth, significant synchrotron losses, and mainly advective cosmic-ray transport in an accelerated galactic wind.

quotes.quote: The synchrotron halo scale heights of NGC 891 increase with radius, which is in line with the dumbbell shape of its radio continuum halo and indicates significant synchrotron losses of the CREs. Still, there may be regions where CRE escape losses dominate. Assuming energy equipartition, the average (maximum) total magnetic field strength in the disc of NGC 891 is 10 G (15 G). In the disc of NGC 4565 we measure 6 G (7 G). The CRE transport in the halo of NGC 891 is probably dominated by advection. This advection flow is an accelerated galactic wind of velocity 150 km s^ 1 near the mid-plane, and reaches the escape velocity at a height of 9 to 17 kpc, depending on major-axis position.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 891 as the canonical edge-on spiral for radiative-transfer and SED modeling of dust disks, photometric corrections, and multi-wavelength dust structure.

quotes.quote: There are several groups using RT models (mostly of NGC 891) to map typical dust in spiral galaxies. model NGC 891 in detail with a diffuse disk and stellar nursery components to explain its multi-wavelength behavior. This NGC 891 model is the basis for a correction of disk galaxy photometry etc Pastrav12,Wijesinghe11a,Wijesinghe11b,Grootes13a . present the TRADING RT code and model NGC 891 with it Bianchi11 as well as follow-up on the initial Xilouris work Bianchi07 . analyze NGC 891 in a completely new way, using large-scale SED models together with small-scale dust structures observed in HST fields to model the dust disk of this canonical edge-on galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper uses archival Chandra and XMM-Newton observations of NGC 891 to study its ULX population, identifying persistent sources and a transient central-region ULX detected in a short observation.

quotes.quote: Currently archival data covers approximately a 17 year span of NGC 891 and a 6 year span of NGC 4157. NGC 891 was observed by XMM-Newton on 2002-08-22 (ObsID 0112280101), 2011-08-25 (ObsID 0670950101), and observed five times with in a month in 2017 (ObsIDs 078076101-078760501), as well as twice by Chandra on 2000-11-01 (ObsID 794) and on 2011-12-20 (ObsID 14376). The ULXs in NGC 4157 were detected in all of the the Chandra and XMM-Newton observations, but only two of the ULXs in NGC 891 were persistent across the archival data.

The other transient source, NGC 891 ULX2 was only detected in the second of the Chandra observations (ObsID 14376 taken in December, 2011), but was not detected in all of the previous and subsequent data sets (see Figure fig:flare ). This study identifies a transient ULX associated with the central region of NGC 891.

sources.summary: The paper models ULXs in NGC 891, describing it as an edge-on barred spiral, Milky Way analog, and host environment whose disk column affects absorption estimates.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we model the spectra of three ULXs in the edge-on, barred spiral galaxy NGC 891 using archival XMM-Newton and Chandra observations dating since 2000. NGC 891 is a nearby analog to the Milky Way in terms of luminosity, color, and morphology 1991rc3..book.....D, 1981A&A....95..116V, 1995A&A...299..657G . This particular region of interest has been extensively observed because it hosts the supernova SN 1986J.

However, NGC 891 is viewed almost edge-on, and therefore one also needs to take into account the column density through the disk of that galaxy. This depends on the line of sight but could have a value of N_H =20.7 dasetal20 , or could be much higher and almost comparable in value to the observed column density aokietal91 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 891 as a nearby edge-on spiral comparison for EAGLE mock X-ray halos, noting inner-axis emission, deep XMM-Newton upper-limit constraints, and a likely low-end halo mass.

quotes.quote: We aggregated the scores, and determined that the intermediate-sized discs are most comparable to typical spiral galaxies with discs, such as NGC 891.

Observations of inclined galaxies, including NGC 891, NGC 3079, NGC 4631, NGC 5775, NGC 5907, show enhanced emission along the disc axis, but on the scale of the optical extent of the galaxy within 10 kpc of the center li08,hodgeskluck18, hodgeskluck20 . None of these observations are able to detect significant emission beyond 10 kpc in the equatorial direction, which may be a result of their lower halo masses than our more massive halo stack here.

The lack of extended emission in deep NGC 891 XMM-Newton observations hodgeskluck18 may already set an upper limit for detectable emission, which may also suggest that X-ray emisssion around discs is in reality fainter than EAGLE predicts. It is likely, however, that NGC 891 lives in a halo at the low-mass end of the 4 stack based on its stellar mass.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 891 as a Milky-Way-like disk with a dusty halo, arguing compact stellar clusters could maintain its extraplanar gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 891 The galaxy NGC 891 is similar to the Milky Way Bok1981,Kruit1984,Sofue1987,Bottema1991,Dettmar1992,dahlem . Its IR, H , CO, [ CII ] 158 m emissions and the synchrotron radio-halo and other galaxy-scale structures are fed by SFR 5 to 10 yr ^ 1 as inferred from dahlem . Recently Yoon2020 described a FIR emission from a dusty halo extending upto 8 kpc above the disc. It may indicate that even though averaged over the disc surface SFR is only 0.03 yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 , under certain conditions it can launch a large scale elevation of the interstellar gas. From the point of view of our simulations, such conditions can be fulfilled when the injection of energy into the ISM is provided by compact stellar clusters: about a hundred OB-associations of 0.04 yr ^ 1 with the total rate 4 yr ^ 1 spread randomly over the disc can maintain such a halo.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 891 as the original test case for the adopted radiative-transfer SED model, noting its detailed geometry, dust heating, SED fit, and predicted FIR morphology.

quotes.quote: Finally, Popescu et. al ( ; P00 hereafter) developed the detailed theoretical model which we use in this paper and which was first tested and applied to the edge-on spiral NGC 891. In their work, they used a detailed geometric description of the galaxy based on optical observations from Xilouris et al. ( ). They also included the effect of localized absorption of the UV in star forming regions. They found that 30 of the dust heating is attributed to the old stellar population. The model of P00 was successful not only in fitting the SED of NGC 891 but also in predicting the FIR morphology of this galaxy (Popescu et al. ).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 891 extraplanar gas observations as a comparison, noting neutral gas to 15 kpc, a vertical rotational lag, possible beam smearing near the disk, and the need for gas accretion beyond fountain models.

quotes.quote: Gas infall and extra planar gas Fraternali et al. (2005) observed the rotation velocity of neutral gas as a function of scale height above the disk for the galaxy NGC 891 which has a luminosity and rotational speed similar to the Milky Way. The found extra planar neutral gas up to distances of 15 kpc above the plane and found a velocity gradient of about 15 km s ^ 1 kpc ^ 1 in the vertical direction. The gas closest to the disk, at z<1.3 kpc, appears to corotate with it but they argue that due to the limited angular resolution this may be the effect of beam-smearing. They considered a galactic fountain model in order to explain these observations, but that model failed to reproduce the amount of lagging. They argue that gas accretion may be important as well in these situations.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 891 as a DLG95 benchmark for radio-halo and star-formation scaling, grouping it with widespread-SF galaxies showing radio-continuum radii larger than star-forming radii.

quotes.quote: In a first attempt to establish general rules of behaviour for gaseous halos of spirals, we (Dahlem, Lisenfeld, Golla 1995; hereafter DLG95) investigated the dependence of the properties of radio halos on the level of SF in the underlying disks of NGC 891 and NGC 4631. At the time, these were the only two galaxies for which we had data of sufficient quality to conduct such studies.
