Two independent analyses of W Comae were performed using standard cuts and produced consistent results.

Observations of W Comae totaling 39 hours led to a 6.2 detection, allowing for a VHE spectrum in the low-flux state to be derived for the first time. We see no significant curvature in the VHE spectrum for W Comae, with the data being best fit to a power law model.

sources.summary: W Comae shows 0.1-0.7 count/s X-ray changes, high total fractional variability, concave spectra in low and elevated states, and larger spectral variability in the soft band.

quotes.quote: W Comae: The observed count flux rate (see the upper panel of Fig. lc_wcomea ) changes in the range of 0.1-0.7 counts/s. The figure shows also the soft (0.3-2.0 keV) and the hard (2.0-10.0 keV) energy band light curve as well as the hardness ratio evolution. The longterm variability of the blazar can be characterized with fractional variability amplitude resulting in F_ var =(94 1) , F_ var =(69 2) , F_ var =(56 6) , and F_ var =(28 9) for the total, the soft, the hard and hardness ratio light curve respectively. These results show that the highest variability is observed in the energy range of 0.3-10 keV. As in the case of previously discussed sources, separate intervals are distinguished to investigate spectral features in the different states.

sources.summary: The paper identifies ON 231, also W Comae, as a HE/VHE BL Lac with historical optical outbursts, polarization ranges from 5.2-19.6 percent, EVPA variation, and a reported 237-degree rotation.

quotes.quote: ON 231 ON 231, also commonly known as W Comae, (z=0.102 ) is a HE and VHE ray bright BL Lac object acciari2008,acciari2009 . Observations taken in 1981-1982 (MJD 45000) and 1991-1992 (MJD 48500) cover similar ranges with a polarisation degree ranging from 5.2-19.2 and an EVPA of 50.0-93.3 ^ wills2011 . The source underwent three major outbursts in March 1995 (MJD 49800), February 1996 (MJD 50120) and January 1997 (MJD 50450) tosti1998a and a more energetic outburst in June 1998 (MJD 50800-51000) which saw the brightness increase by 3 magnitudes in the R band tosti1999 . observed this source from 2008-2010 and during this period the polarisation was 3.5-19.6 with an EVPA of 60-80 ^ .
They find a maximum degree of polarisation of 33.8 1.6 and a large rotation of the EVPA of 237 ^ which coincided with a ray flare in June 2008.

sources.summary: The paper confirms W Comae's z=0.102 emission-line redshift and detects host-galaxy absorption lines in its high-SNR optical spectrum.

quotes.quote: W Comae : weistrop1985 provided an optical spectrum and estimated a redshift of z = 0.102 based on the detection of [ OIII ] 5007 and H _ emission lines. This redshift was not confirmed by finke2008 , though their spectra cover only the range from 3800 to 5000 . In 2003 the host galaxy of W Comae was resolved by nilsson2003 .

From our (SNR 220) optical spectrum we confirm the detection of [ O III ] 5007 and H _ emission lines at z = 0.102. In addition we detect at the same redshift the absorption lines due to Ca II (3934, 3968 ) doublet, G-band 4305 , and Mg I 5175 from the host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper studies ON 231 during a June 2008 outburst, modeling its X-ray flares with synchrotron and inverse-Compton components and relating flux to break energy.

quotes.quote: We present a detailed temporal and spectral study of the intermediate type blazar, ON 231 with observations taken by Swift and XMM-Newton satellites during an outburst phase in June 2008. The source shows multiple X-ray flares in that period when the flux amplitude varies in the range of 27-38. The X-ray spectra are well fitted with a broken power-law model indicating the presence of both synchrotron and inverse Compton components.

ON 231, also known as W Comae ( z = 0.102) was the 1st intermediate type synchrotron peaked (ISP) blazar detected in TeV ray range by VERITAS in March, 2008 when the source went to an outburst phase ( ). The source entered into a second outburst state in June in the same year, which triggered a multi-frequency campaign to observe the source in different wavelengths.

sources.summary: The paper presents VLBI observations of 1219+285, an elliptical-galaxy BL Lac source at redshift 0.102, showing eastward jet structure, southern diffuse extension, and apparent jet-direction changes.

quotes.quote: 1219+285 lies in the centre of an elliptical galaxy at a redshift of 0.102 weistrop85 . This source is essentially unresolved to the VLA D array kollgaard92 . The VLBI jet extends to the east of the core and a number of superluminal components have been identified gabuzda94 . The images in Figures  fig:1219+285_2.3ghz to fig:1219+285_0.3ghz show a large diffuse extension towards the south roughly perpendicular to the known VLBI jet. There is an apparent change in jet direction visible in the 2.3 GHz data.

sources.summary: The paper discusses ON 231 as a possible contributor to an EGRET source, analyzes previously unpublished XMM-Newton data, and finds a broken power law with marginal extra absorption.

quotes.quote: This is another EGRET source that could be composed of two or more contributions: indeed, although ON 231 is outside the global 99 probability contours of 3EG J 1222+2841 , there is a strong association with the emission at E>1 GeV (Lamb Macomb 1997). The present XMM-Newton data set has never been analysed and shows evidence of high background towards the end of the observation. The broken power law model provides the best fit to the data. An absorption in addition to the Galactic column is marginally detected. The values are consistent with, although slightly different to the fit to the BeppoSAX data of an observation in 1998 , when ON 231 was in outburst (Tagliaferri et al. 2000).

sources.summary: The paper describes W Com as a TeV IBL with optical, X-ray, and gamma-ray outbursts, using VERITAS, Fermi/LAT, Swift, and AGILE data in SED modeling.

quotes.quote: W Com. The first TeV intermediate-frequency-peaked BL Lac (IBL) object, an evidently bright outburst in the optical and X-ray bands was observed in 1998 (Tagliaferri et al. 2000). W Com is confirmed to be a TeV source by the observations of VERITAS during a strong TeV flare in the middle of March 2008 with an integrated photon flux above 200 GeV of 9 Crab (Acciari et al. 2008) and the data of the TeV observation are presented as opened squares in Fig. Fig:1 . Both the SEDs quasi-simultaneously obtained during the TeV flare and during the optical/X-ray outburst can be well fitted by the single-zone syn+SSC model (Zhang 2009). The broadband SED quasi-simultaneously obtained with observations of Fermi /LAT and swift from Tavecchio et al. (2010b) is also shown in Fig. Fig:1 (red squares).

sources.summary: The paper gives J1221+2813 an individual BL Lac radio-structure entry, describing its arcsecond or VLBA morphology, flux measurements, gamma-ray association, and source classification.

quotes.quote: J1221+2813. This source is known to be an extremely variable object, in infrared especially (Fan Lin 1999), lying in the center of an elliptical galaxy (Weistrop et al. 1985). It was initially identified as the variable star W Comae. Arcsecond scale radio images (Kollgaard et al.

sources.summary: The paper describes ON 231 as a VHE BL Lac source in its monitoring sample and reports its optical/radio flux level, variability trends, and correlation behavior.

quotes.quote: ON 231 : The average brightness in both bands is above the median of the sample, and the light curves show clear outbursts. Visually the outbursts do not appear correlated and while the optical light curve shows highly significant decreasing trend, the radio light curve shows an increasing trend. DCF analysis finds no significant correlation (see Fig. Fig:lc20 ). In summary, the visual appearance and obtained results are very similar as for ON 325.

sources.summary: VERITAS established W Comae as an intermediate-frequency peaked BL Lac object emitting at very high energies, with variability, a steep spectrum, and a second brighter flare.

quotes.quote: The first IBL to be established at very high energies was W Comae, a known EGRET source at a redshift of z = 0.102 , detected by VERITAS during 40 h of observation in Spring 2008 . This source exhibited strong variability and a very steep energy spectrum, with differential spectral index of = 3.81 0.35_ stat 0.34_ sys . A second VHE flare from W Comae was detected in June 2008 at a significantly higher flux level than the first .

sources.summary: The paper describes ON 231 as a blazar with previously known concave curvature and one analyzed observation showing significant curvature with a 4.33 keV break.

quotes.quote: ON 231: This blazar has shown significant concave curvature in literature (i.e. Donato et al. 2005; Tagliaferri et al. 2000; Wierzcholska Wagner 2016). Wierzcholska Wagner (2016) fitted the Swift/XRT observation of this source using the broken power law and found a break at 2.01 keV. Donato et al. (2005) found the break energy to be at 3.09_ 1.092 ^ +0.557 . We analyzed the spectra of this source on four occasions and found significant curvature in one of the observations performed in 2002. We found a break energy of 4.33_ 1.87 ^ +0.51 keV which is in consistent with previous studies within errorbars.

gmw_00000421.name: J0916a
gmw_00000421.overview: J0916a is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy at a redshift of 0.49 [arXiv:1901.05618, arXiv:2011.06914]. It hosts an extremely strong ionized-gas outflow extending to a radius of 4 kpc [arXiv:1901.05618, arXiv:2011.06914], which co-exists with intense ongoing star formation [arXiv:1901.05618].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AKARI-FIS-V2 J0916248+073034 as a z=0.49 ULIRG with an extremely strong ionized-gas outflow but ongoing intense star formation.

quotes.quote: AKARI-FIS-V2 J0916248+073034, identified as a ULIRG at z=0.49 in the spectroscopic follow-up observation, indicates signatures of an extremely strong outflow in its emission line profiles. Its [ O iii ] 5007 emission line shows FWHM of 1830 \ and velocity shift of 770 \ in relative to the stellar absorption lines. Furthermore, low-ionization [ O ii ] 3726 \ 3729 doublet also shows large FWHM of 910 \ and velocity shift of 380 .

After the removal of an unresolved nuclear component, the long-slit spectroscopy 2D image possibly shows that the outflow extends to radius of 4 kpc. The mass outflow and energy ejection rates are estimated to be 500 \ and 4 10^ 44 , respectively, which imply that the outflow is among the most powerful ones observed in ULIRGs and QSOs at 0.3<z<1.6 . The co-existence of the strong outflow and intense star formation (star formation rate of 990 ) indicates that the feedback of the strong outflow has not severely affect the star-forming region of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper reports NuSTAR follow-up of J0916a, an intermediate-redshift ULIRG with a powerful ionized outflow, faint current X-ray/MIR AGN emission, and likely fading activity.

quotes.quote: Among the sources, AKARI J0916248+073034 (hereafter J0916a) at z_ spec = 0.49, indicates signatures of an extremely strong ionized-gas outflow in the Subaru / FOCAS long-slit spectroscopic image with slit width of 0.5 , Chen2019. The long-slit spectroscopic image shows that the outflow extends to a radius of 4 kpc. The mass-loss and energy-ejection ( E _ k ) rates are estimated to be 500 \ and 10^ 44.6 , respectively, implying that J0916a has one of the highest E _ k ionized outflows among ULIRGs / AGNs at z < 1.6 and it is comparable to the most powerful outflows in quasars at z 2 e.g., Harrison2012, Zakamska2016.

There is no previous X-ray observation for J0916a and it is not detected in the /BAT 14-195 keV survey due to its faintness at a relatively high redshift. In this letter we report the first hard X-ray follow-up of J0916a with NuSTAR Harrison2013. J0916a locates at a unique position out of the 95 range of the BASS sample, which indicates that both of its core (corona) and circumnuclear (torus) AGN emission are drastic declining.

gmw_00000422.name: JO201
gmw_00000422.overview: JO201 is a massive, barred spiral galaxy in the Abell 85 cluster undergoing extreme, near-face-on ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:2006.11543, arXiv:2301.03090]. It features an active galactic nucleus at its core [arXiv:1902.04486] and long, star-forming tails of stripped gas [arXiv:1811.00823, arXiv:2302.10615].

sources.summary: The paper finds JO201 is a massive ram-pressure-stripped jellyfish galaxy with clumpy HI and H-alpha tails, substantial HI deficiency, and enhanced star formation for its observed gas content.

quotes.quote: We present atomic hydrogen ( ) observations with the Jansky Very Large Array of one of the jellyfish galaxies in the GAs Stripping Phenomena sample, JO201. This massive galaxy (M _ = 3.5 10 ^ 10 ) is falling along the line-of-sight towards the centre of a rich cluster (M _ 200 1.6 10^ 15 , _ cl \ 982 55 ) at a high velocity 3363 . Its H emission shows a 40 kpc tail, which is closely confined to its stellar disc and a 100 kpc tail extending further out. We find that \ emission only coincides with the shorter clumpy H tail, while no \ emission is detected along the 100 kpc H tail. In total, we measured an \ mass of M _ HI = 1.65 10^ 9 , which is about 60 lower than expected based on its stellar mass and stellar surface density.

JO201 has an enhanced SFR compared to field galaxies of the same stellar mass for its observed \ content. We find that the time it would take to deplete its \ through star formation at its current rate is 0.27 Gyr (similarly that of JO206 - 0.5 Gyr) which is much shorter than that of spiral galaxies in the field ( 2 Gyr; Leroy2008 ). Given its observed SFR, JO201 would need to have ten times its current \ mass in order to be a `normal' spiral.

sources.summary: The paper uses JO201 as a worked example of ram-pressure stripping, modelling how halted star formation reddens and smooths its disc into a lenticular morphology.

quotes.quote: To illustrate the outcome of our spectrophotometric evolution modelling, we focus on one of the most well-studied `jellyfish' galaxies in the GASP sample, JO201. JO201 is a late-type galaxy Sab type, from Fasano+12 moving through the dense intracluster medium of Abell 85 ( z = 0.0557 ) at supersonic speeds along the line-of-sight, showing a stellar disc accompanied by large projected tails of emitting gas that extend out to 100 from the galaxy center Bellhouse+17 . Signatures of gas stripping are also present in the molecular component traced by CO emission-line observations with APEX Moretti+18 and ALMA Moretti+20a,Bacchini+23 . The kinematic analysis of Bellhouse+17 suggests that JO201 must so far have lost about half of its gas during infall via ram pressure stripping, in agreement with the deficiency inferred from JVLA data by Ramatsoku+20 .

Clearly, as the galaxy evolves with time (proceeding from left to right in Fig. f:JO201_evo_maps ), it is subject to a number of photometric and structural changes: its disc, initially blue and with a marked spiral arm structure, becomes redder, fainter and smoother, with no clear indication of a spiral arm pattern after 3 4 . The dimming of the disc is more pronounced in the galaxy outskirts, where the stellar population is younger, leading to a progressive shrinking of the galaxy effective radius down to 70 80 of the initial value, with small variations depending on the band and quenching scenario considered. The morphological transformation occurs in all evolutionary scenarios analysed (different rows in Fig. f:JO201_evo_maps ), but there are minor differences due to star formation proceeding in the inner regions for the outside-in scenarios. The differences are particularly evident at t = 1 2 , where the instantaneous quenching scenario provides a galaxy which is already red and smooth, whereas blue and clumpy regions are still visible in the disc for the outside-in quenching cases, especially for longer t_ strip , as expected.

sources.summary: The paper describes JO201 as a spectacular A85 jellyfish galaxy with redshifted ionized gas trailing the stellar component, many star-forming knots, complex cold-gas kinematics, and unusually high molecular gas content.

quotes.quote: JO201 JO201 is one of the most spectacular jellyfish galaxies revealed by the GASP survey: it is moving at high velocity in the A85 cluster mostly along the line of sight, so that in the outskirts of the disk (starting from 6 kpc from its center) the ionized gas is increasingly redshifted. This indicates the presence of gas trailing behind the stellar component gaspII . Our procedure revealed the presence of 148 H emitting knots, 122 of them being classified as star forming.

0.45 0.45 0.45 0.45 The behavior of cold gas in JO201 is rather complex. The cold gas found in the main body of the galaxy as well as in the pointings closest to the center (D and F) seems to be compatible, in term of its velocity, with the ionized gas traced by the H emission, while more external pointings (B, C and E) show prominent cold gas emission at a different velocity with respect to the warm gas component. More in detail, pointing D shows a double peak, with one peak compatible with a null velocity with respect to the galaxy center, and a secondary peak (prominent in CO, less luminous in H ).

Looking at the ratio between the total measured mass of molecular hydrogen and the total galaxy stellar mass we find that it is always smaller than one ( 0.42, 0.20, 0.14 in JO204, JO206 and JW100, respectively ) except in JO201, where we detect a mass of molecular gas similar to the stellar mass of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper presents JO201 as an extreme jellyfish disk galaxy in Abell 85, with ram-pressure-stripped tails, star-forming knots, an AGN-dominated core, and outwardly decreasing metallicity.

quotes.quote: We present a study of the physical properties of JO201, a unique disk galaxy with extended tails undergoing extreme ram-pressure stripping as it moves through the massive cluster Abell 85 at supersonic speeds mostly along the line of sight. JO201 was observed with MUSE as part of the GASP programme. In a previous paper (GASP II) we studied the stellar and gas kinematics. In this paper we present emission-line ratios, gas-phase metallicities and ages of the stellar populations across the galaxy disk and tails.

We find that while the emission at the core of the galaxy is dominated by an active galactic nucleus (AGN), the disk is composed of star-forming knots surrounded by excited diffuse gas. The collection of star-forming knots presents a metallicity gradient steadily decreasing from the centre of the galaxy outwards, and the ages of the stars across the galaxy show that the tails formed 10^9 yr ago. This result is consistent with an estimate of the stripping timescale ( 1 Gyr), obtained from a toy orbital model.

sources.summary: The paper presents JO201 as a ram-pressure-stripped jellyfish spiral with an AGN, central CO/UV cavity, reduced recent central star formation, and quenching attributed to AGN feedback.

quotes.quote: We report evidence for star formation quenching in the central 8.6 kpc region of the jellyfish galaxy JO201 which hosts an active galactic nucleus, while undergoing strong ram pressure stripping. The ultraviolet imaging data of the galaxy disk reveal a region with reduced flux around the center of the galaxy and a horse shoe shaped region with enhanced flux in the outer disk. The characterization of the ionization regions based on emission line diagnostic diagrams shows that the region of reduced flux seen in the ultraviolet is within the AGN-dominated area. The CO J _ 2-1 map of the galaxy disk reveals a cavity in the central region.

The star formation rate map of the galaxy disk shows that the star formation suppression in the cavity occurred in the last few 10 ^8 yr. We present several lines of evidence supporting the scenario that suppression of star formation in the central region of the disk is most likely due to the feedback from the AGN. The observations reported here make JO201 a unique case of AGN feedback and environmental effects suppressing star formation in a spiral galaxy.

sources.summary: For JO201, the paper notes complex tail kinematics, direct UV detection of young tail clumps, mostly pure-SF tail emission, and tail SFR at 10-20% of total.

quotes.quote: The two galaxies for which this effect is more important in the tails are JW100 and JO201, for which detailed diagnostic diagrams based on two Gaussian component fits were presented in and Bellhouse et al. For the majority of galaxies, the NII DD and and SII DD generally show an excellent agreement, and they indicate star formation as the predominant ionization mechanism in the tails (in JO113, JO141, JO160, JO171, JO175, JO201, JO204, JO206, JO49, JO60, JO95, JW100, JW39 plus JO147 and JO194 from SII DD) or a SF+Composite origin (NII DD for JO194, and especially for JO147). In the same fashion, the FUV and NUV light of the O and B young stars in the clumps of the tails was directly observed with UVIT@ASTROSAT in JO201, one of the galaxies in our sample George2018 , where we found a remarkable agreeement between the SFR of individual clumps derived from the FUV and from H .

It is interesting that the two galaxies with the ``warmest'' tails, JO147 and JW100 (and to a less extent JO201), are those for which the OI DD finds a strong LINER component. Considering instead only the pure SF component (no Composite) in the NII DD, this dominates ( 80 ) the emission in the tails of 9 of our galaxies (JO113, JO141, JO160, JO171, JO201, JO206, JO49, JO95, JW39). The SFR occurring in the tail represents 10 to 20 of the total in 6 of our galaxies (JO206, JW39, JO204, JO201, JW100 and JO171), which are those with the longest tails, as it is reasonable to expect.

sources.summary: The paper highlights JO201 tail structures where H and UV-selected clumps and complexes overlap, and notes its especially bright H-selected clumps in both disk and tail.

quotes.quote: Fig. JO201_complexes shows a zoomed-in F606W image of some structures in the tails of JO201, to better appreciate the different spatial distributions of star-forming complexes (dark violet contours), H selected clumps (dark orange) and UV-selected clumps (violet). Typically, an H selected clump has a corresponding UV-selected clump, while the viceversa is not true. Indeed, the number of UV-selected clumps is higher than the number of H selected ones (see below). Furthermore, the corresponding UV-selected clump is generally bigger and almost completely encompasses the H selected clump. Similarly, star-forming complexes contain many UV- and H selected clumps, embedded in fainter, optical regions.

JO201 stands out for its population of bright H selected clumps, both in the disk and in the tail, while in the extraplanar regions the brightest clumps are those of JO206 (located in the crest to the top right of the disk, see Fig. 6.4).

sources.summary: JO201 has three fireball groups; the paper describes their kpc-scale optical/UV/H-alpha structure, H-alpha displaced away from the galaxy center, and elongation along the center direction or spiral sub-tails.

quotes.quote: In Fig. plot:rgb we show eight clear examples of groups of fireballs, three in JO201, three in JO204 and two in JW100, respectively. The fireball associations have size of a few kpc and are all characterized by extended optical and UV emission, with compact bright H clumps. The H emission is always displaced in the opposite direction with respect to the center of the galaxy. On the other hand the UV light, despite more compact in correspondence of the H emission, is more diffuse and characterized by a long tail pointing towards the galaxy center.

In the case of JO201, the fireballs are elongated either along a direction aligned with the center of the galaxy or along the spiral arm-like sub-tails that are likely to characterize the large-scale morphology of the stripped material (in Appendix app:subtail we define the sub-tails for each galaxy and study possible trends along it). In a single fireball many compact H clumps are found.

sources.summary: The paper includes JO201 among HST-observed GASP jellyfish galaxies and notes previous MUSE work finding its tail star-forming regions typically younger than 800 Myr.

quotes.quote: The data Our work is based on images obtained using the UVIS channel of the WFC3 camera on board of HST for six jellyfish galaxies in the GASP sample: JO175, JO201, JO204, JO206, JW39 and JW100. These galaxies were observed in five filters: F275W, F336W, F606W, F680N and F814W, sampling the wavelength range from 2475 to 9560 , with F680N covering the H emission line in the redshift range of the targeted galaxies (0.042 - 0.066). The FWHM of the PSF of the observations in this redshift range corresponds to 70 pc.

A similar analysis has been performed for JO201 by using MUSE data from the GASP project and the sinopsis spectral synthesis code Fritz2017 . The authors find that the the star-forming regions detected in H with MUSE in the tail that galaxy are typically younger than 800 Myr (with two outliers as old as 2 Gyr).
In Fig. fig:goodbadugly , we provide examples of F275W clumps in JO201 that were rejected according one of the quality control criteria, and also of a fit considered good (although not perfect).

sources.summary: JO201 is moving extremely fast through a relatively massive cluster ICM; the paper groups it with seven systems in exceptional ram-pressure conditions and cites dedicated studies of it.

quotes.quote: Let us consider the 7 massive galaxies with very high values. JW100 is the most massive galaxy in the GASP sample and it is hosted in a relatively low-mass cluster, but it is moving at very high speed and it is very close to the cluster centre, where the ICM is denser. JO85 and JO171 are hosted in two of the most massive clusters ( >1000 ), while JO201 is moving at extremely high speed in the ICM of a relatively massive cluster. Finally, JO204, JO206 and JW39 are very close to the centre of their host cluster centre and moving at quite high speed.

All these seven galaxies are in exceptional conditions regarding at least one the main parameters regulating the ram-pressure intensity. We note that, besides JO201, all other six galaxies have a tail of stripped gas with a long extension on the plane of the sky, indicating that the component on the plane of the sky of the velocity of the galaxies in the ICM is dominant with respect to the one along the line of sight; the measured radial velocity is therefore a lower limit of their actual speed. Five of these galaxies are studied in detail in dedicated papers ( JW100, pogg+jw100 ; JO171, gaspV ; JO201, gaspII,gaspXV ; JO204, gaspIV ; JO206, gaspI ).

sources.summary: The paper describes JO201 as a massive jellyfish galaxy in Abell 85 undergoing extreme near-face-on ram-pressure stripping, with stripped knots and gas distributed along unwound spiral-arm curves.

quotes.quote: One of the most heavily studied jellyfish galaxies of the GASP survey to date has been JO201, analysed both individually in Bellhouse2017,George2018,George2019,Bellhouse2019,Ramatsoku2020 and as part of wider studies Poggianti2017b,Vulcani2018,Moretti2018,Radovich2019 . JO201 is a massive galaxy falling into the massive cluster Abell 85 at supersonic speeds close to the line of sight toward the observer, exhibiting signs of extreme near-face-on ram pressure stripping. The spatial distribution of the stripped knots hinted for the first time the possibility that the spiral arms could be "unwound" or broadened as an effect of the face-on gas stripping by the ICM (see Bellhouse2017 and Figure 2 of George2018 ). In addition to the more commonly observed "jellyfish" features such as compression of the disc and one-sided tails, the trailing knots and the ionised gas appear to be distributed along the curves of the spiral arms.

Notable examples of unwinding spiral arms include JO201, which in the H map shows an extremely extended arc of stripped material to the east of the galaxy in Figure  fig:thumbnails , far outside the galaxy disk.

sources.summary: The paper treats JO201 as a comparison jellyfish galaxy: its published ram-pressure-stripped tail shows a 0.5 dex metallicity decline over 60 projected kpc, with inferred ICM mixing up to 80%.

quotes.quote: Four galaxies with stripped ionized gas reaching over 50 kpc (in projection) from the galaxy center, represent the best candidates for this study: JO201, JO206, JW100, and JW39. Since the metallicity profile of the ram-pressure stripped gas from JO201 is already published in bellhouse2019 , in this Letter we mainly present the results based on the other three galaxies, discussing the implications for JO201 only in the text.

A similar behavior is also observed in JO201, whose tail metallicity trends are very similar to those of the three galaxies presented here; as shown in Fig. 6 of bellhouse2019 , the metallicities of clumps in the tail decrease by 0.5 dex moving away from the galaxy center, along 60 projected kpc of distance.

For JW100 and JW39 we derive that 30 - 40 of the most metal-poor clumps could be constituted by gas from the ICM; for JO206 the mixing can reach values as high as 60 ; while, for JO201 the effect is evaluated up to the 80 .

sources.summary: The paper models JO201 as a barred jellyfish galaxy whose stellar disk is lopsided and whose molecular gas kinematics show possible face-on ram-pressure effects.

quotes.quote: JO201 The I band image in Fig. fig:iband_images shows that JO201 has a stellar bulge. Moreover, the elongated shape of the isophotes in the inner regions suggests that JO201 hosts a stellar bar, as reported by . Sanchez-Garcia et al.

sources.summary: The paper uses JO201 as a ram-pressure-stripped Seyfert-like cluster example, mapping its BPT classifications, metallicity peak, ionization parameter, and stripped-tail gas.

quotes.quote: The top panel of Figure fig:maps shows the NII-BPT diagrams of the field galaxy '8993-12705' ( z=0.030 , \ M _*/ M _ = 10.96) and a zoom on the cluster galaxy JO201 ( z= 0.0446 , \ M_*/ M_ = 10.79), which is experiencing strong RPS as discussed in .

On the other side, the metallicity in JO201 peaks around 12 + log (O/H) 9.0 in the galaxy center in correspondence to the AGN, and we note that also the gas in the right lower side of the stripped tail shows similar values as well. We also observe two high-metallicity and high-ionization parameter elongated regions symmetrically oriented with respect to the galaxy center. The peak of the AGN ionization parameter ( (U) 1.6 ) and of the metallicity (12 + log (O/H) 9.0) is presumably tracing the actual position of the AGN, more precisely than the NII-BPT classification map in which the AGN-like region is extending well beyond the NLR.

sources.summary: The review highlights JO201 as a jellyfish galaxy where multiwavelength data indicate AGN feedback, central molecular-gas depletion, suppressed recent star formation, and AGN-ionized gas.

quotes.quote: Moreover, the combination of MUSE and multiwavelength data has provided strong evidence for the effects of AGN feedback in the jellyfish galaxy JO201 (George et al. 2019, see also Bellhouse et al. 2017, 2019). The central 8kpc region of JO201 is depleted of both molecular gas (as traced by a CO ALMA observation) and of recent and ongoing star formation (as traced by NUV and FUV imaging with UVIT@ASTROSAT) (Fig. 3). This region is filled with gas ionized by the AGN (as seen by MUSE).

sources.summary: The paper includes JO201 among ALMA-observed jellyfish galaxies and finds literature-like depletion times under one conversion but short HI depletion times in prior work.

quotes.quote: Observations of the CO(1-0) emission (rest frequency 115.271 GHz) of our four galaxies, namely JO201, JO204, JO206 and JW100, have been obtained with ALMA during Cycle 5 (project 2017.1.00496.S).

When using the Accurso2017 scaling relation, the timescales for JW100 and JO204 are still longer than normal galaxies, while the other two are more consistent with the literature values (JO201 and JO206).

Finally, considering depletion times, we note that in GASPXXVIII , for JO201 and JO206, we found that HI depletion times are much shorter than the average value found in galaxy disks, apparently implying a very efficient star formation (as far as HI is concerned) and/or a low HI content for their SFR.

sources.summary: The paper describes JO201 as a GASP jellyfish galaxy with an AGN, multi-component emission lines, and compact central HST emission consistent with an outflow near the line of sight.

quotes.quote: Results from the GASP survey showed that JO201, JO204, JO206 and JW100 galaxies host an AGN rado+2019 ; JW39 central region has a LINER-like spectrum pelu+2022 ; finally ionization in the central region of JO175 is dominated by SF activity gaspXIII,rado+2019. MUSE data also showed that emission lines in JO201, JO204 and JW100 clearly present at least two components in the line profiles, one broader ( _v 200-500 km s ^ 1 ) and the other narrower ( _v < 200 km s ^ 1 ). In JO201 both [ O 3 ] components overlap well with the \ central, more compact emission; this agrees with the interpretation that in JO201 the outflow orientation is close to the line of sight.

gmw_00000423.name: JO204
gmw_00000423.overview: JO204 is a barred jellyfish galaxy in the Abell 957 cluster undergoing ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:1708.09035, arXiv:2301.03090]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus with an outflow [arXiv:2301.08279] and features a 90-kiloparsec neutral gas tail containing star-forming fireballs [arXiv:2004.04754, arXiv:2310.07267].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes JO204 as a jellyfish galaxy undergoing ram-pressure stripping, with a long ionized gas tail, regular stellar rotation, retained gas-tail rotation, and recent outer-disk quenching.

quotes.quote: In the context of the GAs Stripping Phenomena in galaxies with Muse (GASP) survey, we present the characterization of JO204, a jellyfish galaxy in A957, a relatively low-mass cluster with M=4.4 10^ 14 M_ . This galaxy shows a tail of ionized gas that extends up to 30 kpc from the main body in the opposite direction of the cluster center. No gas emission is detected in the galaxy outer disk, suggesting that gas stripping is proceeding outside-in. The stellar component is distributed as a regular disk galaxy; the stellar kinematics shows a symmetric rotation curve with a maximum radial velocity of 200km/s out to 20 kpc from the galaxy center.

The radial velocity of the gas component in the central part of the disk follows the distribution of the stellar component; the gas kinematics in the tail retains the rotation of the galaxy disk, indicating that JO204 is moving at high speed in the intracluster medium. Both the emission and radial velocity maps of the gas and stellar components indicate ram-pressure as the most likely primary mechanism for gas stripping, as expected given that JO204 is close to the cluster center and it is likely at the first infall in the cluster. The spatially resolved star formation history of JO204 provides evidence that the onset of ram-pressure stripping occurred in the last 500 Myr, quenching the star formation activity in the outer disk, where the gas has been already completely stripped.

sources.summary: The paper includes JO204 in the HST GASP jellyfish sample and reports that tail star-forming-complex onset-age percentiles vary down to 284 Myr for JO204.

quotes.quote: The data Our work is based on images obtained using the UVIS channel of the WFC3 camera on board of HST for six jellyfish galaxies in the GASP sample: JO175, JO201, JO204, JO206, JW39 and JW100. These galaxies were observed in five filters: F275W, F336W, F606W, F680N and F814W, sampling the wavelength range from 2475 to 9560 , with F680N covering the H emission line in the redshift range of the targeted galaxies (0.042 - 0.066). The FWHM of the PSF of the observations in this redshift range corresponds to 70 pc.

In Fig. fig:onset , we show box plots tracing the distributions of t_0^ for each galaxy in our sample, with whiskers indicating the 1 and 99 percentiles. The 99 percentiles of t_0^ are very similar between galaxies, varying from 284 Myr for JO204 to 383 Myr for JO206. These values are comparable to that of , who constrain the ages of blobs in the tail of IC 3418 to be younger than 400 Myr. Taken at face value, the similarity of the maximum t_0^ in different galaxies points to a scenario where the stripping timescales do not strongly depend on galaxy properties and surrounding environment.

sources.summary: The paper presents JO204 as an A957 jellyfish galaxy probably on first infall, stripped mostly in the sky plane, with 92 star-forming knots and CO components shaped by ram pressure and rotation.

quotes.quote: JO204 JO204 is probably at its first infall in the A957 cluster gaspIX and is mostly being stripped on the plane of the sky, as demonstrated by the coherent rotation of the stripped gas with respect to the undisturbed stellar component (shown in the upper right panel of Fig. fig:JO204_1G ). It possesses 92 star forming knots, according to the procedure described in Sec. sec:muse (see also gaspIV ), but only 51 of them have SII lines measurements within the range where the Proxauf+2014 calibration can be applied. The average ionized gas mass in these knots turns out to be 5.3 10^4 M_ , and the total ionized gas mass within these star forming knots is 1.8 10^7 M_ (one order of magnitude smaller than the JO206 galaxy, see Sec. sec:JO206 ). As for the CO emission, shown in Fig. fig:JO204_1G , lower right panels, it is again consistent with the overall distribution of the ionized gas in the central pointing (containing an AGN also in this case), while in the two external slightly off-disk regions the CO line is larger than the H emission and a double Gaussian fit (shown in the two lower right panels) reveals that the CO is again showing probably a double component: one that is coincident in velocity with the H emission, and another one moving at a lower absolute velocity value. 0.45 0.45 0.45 0.45 0.44 In this case the galaxy is moving in the plane of the sky and the net effect of the gas stripping on the velocity of the molecular gas is less easily seen.

In all galaxies we were able to measure significant amounts of cold gas both in the galaxy main body and along the tails: the central pointings reveal an H_2 mass that goes from 8.29 10^9 M_ in JO204 to 19.17 10^9 M_ in JW100.

sources.summary: JO204 has an AGN ionization cone reaching into the tail, but much tail H emission remains star-formation powered; pure-SF emission is lower, while tail SFR is 10-20% of total.

quotes.quote: For the majority of galaxies, the NII DD and and SII DD generally show an excellent agreement, and they indicate star formation as the predominant ionization mechanism in the tails (in JO113, JO141, JO160, JO171, JO175, JO201, JO204, JO206, JO49, JO60, JO95, JW100, JW39 plus JO147 and JO194 from SII DD) or a SF+Composite origin (NII DD for JO194, and especially for JO147). Finally, we note that all three DDs agree that two of the galaxies (JO135 and JO204) present a ionization cone from the central AGN that extends for several kpc and contributes to the ionization of gas even in the tails see also Poggianti2017b . Nonetheless, also in these galaxies a large fraction of the tail H emission appears to be powered by SF (53 /68 /37 in JO135 and 77 /85 /71 in JO204 from the NII/SII/OI DDs, see Table 2).

SF+Composite emission accounts for at least 98 of the tail emission in all galaxies, except in JO135 and JO204 that, as discussed above, have AGN ionization cones extending in the tail, and in JO147 where there is a 10 LINER component. SF still accounts for more than the half of the tail emission in JO175 and JO60 (66 and 58 , respectively), and only for about 20-30 in JO135 (29 ) , JO204 (36 ) and JO194 (21 ). The SFR occurring in the tail represents 10 to 20 of the total in 6 of our galaxies (JO206, JW39, JO204, JO201, JW100 and JO171), which are those with the longest tails, as it is reasonable to expect.

sources.summary: JO204 lies near its cluster center and moves quickly; the paper groups it with exceptional ram-pressure systems with long tails and notes AGN-like tail emission from its central AGN.

quotes.quote: In most galaxies the tails are ionised mainly by massive young stars; extended regions with AGN-like emission are observed in JO135 and JO204 which are likely due to the ionisation cone of the central AGN gaspIV,gaspVI,gaspXIX .

Let us consider the 7 massive galaxies with very high values. JW100 is the most massive galaxy in the GASP sample and it is hosted in a relatively low-mass cluster, but it is moving at very high speed and it is very close to the cluster centre, where the ICM is denser. JO85 and JO171 are hosted in two of the most massive clusters ( >1000 ), while JO201 is moving at extremely high speed in the ICM of a relatively massive cluster. Finally, JO204, JO206 and JW39 are very close to the centre of their host cluster centre and moving at quite high speed.

All these seven galaxies are in exceptional conditions regarding at least one the main parameters regulating the ram-pressure intensity. We note that, besides JO201, all other six galaxies have a tail of stripped gas with a long extension on the plane of the sky, indicating that the component on the plane of the sky of the velocity of the galaxies in the ICM is dominant with respect to the one along the line of sight; the measured radial velocity is therefore a lower limit of their actual speed. Five of these galaxies are studied in detail in dedicated papers ( JW100, pogg+jw100 ; JO171, gaspV ; JO201, gaspII,gaspXV ; JO204, gaspIV ; JO206, gaspI ).

sources.summary: The paper presents JVLA observations of JO204, a massive jellyfish galaxy with a 90 kpc neutral-gas tail, retained northern disk gas, stripped southern disk gas, and turbulent tail gas.

quotes.quote: We present JVLA-C observations of the \ gas in JO204, one of the most striking jellyfish galaxies from the GASP survey. JO204 is a massive galaxy in the low-mass cluster Abell 957 at z=0.04243. The \ map reveals an extended 90 kpc long ram-pressure stripped tail of neutral gas, stretching beyond the 30 kpc long ionized gas tail and pointing away from the cluster center. The \ mass seen in emission is (1.32 0.13) 10^ 9 M_ , mostly located in the tail. The northern part of the galaxy disk has retained some \ gas, while the southern part has already been completely stripped and displaced into an extended unilateral tail.

sources.summary: The paper describes JO204 as a barred system whose molecular gas is centrally concentrated with arm-like structures and kinematics mainly dominated by rotation rather than ram pressure.

quotes.quote: JO204 Before focusing on the molecular gas kinematics, it is worth noting two features of the stellar component. First, the innermost isophotes in the I band image (Fig. fig:iband_images ) show a boxy shape that might indicate the presence of a bar seen with high inclination with respect to the line-of-sight e.g. 1990Combes,1994Bettoni,1995Kuijken,1999Bureau,1999Merrifield . Unfortunately, dust obscuration and projection effects hamper any attempt to estimate the bar length from the optical images.

sources.summary: JO204 has three fireball groups with kpc-scale optical/UV/H-alpha structure; compared with JO201, its fireballs are less diffuse, more clustered, and contain smaller UV tails with fragmented star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: In Fig. plot:rgb we show eight clear examples of groups of fireballs, three in JO201, three in JO204 and two in JW100, respectively. The fireball associations have size of a few kpc and are all characterized by extended optical and UV emission, with compact bright H clumps. The H emission is always displaced in the opposite direction with respect to the center of the galaxy. On the other hand the UV light, despite more compact in correspondence of the H emission, is more diffuse and characterized by a long tail pointing towards the galaxy center.
The fireballs seen in JO204 appear less diffuse and even more clustered than those observed in JO201, with smaller UV tails but more fragmented star-forming regions.

sources.summary: The paper describes JO204 as an AGN-hosting GASP jellyfish galaxy with nuclear dust lanes, multi-component emission lines, and extended central emission associated with an outflow.

quotes.quote: Results from the GASP survey showed that JO201, JO204, JO206 and JW100 galaxies host an AGN rado+2019 ; JW39 central region has a LINER-like spectrum pelu+2022 ; finally ionization in the central region of JO175 is dominated by SF activity gaspXIII,rado+2019. Multiple dust lanes are present in the nuclear regions, in particular in JO204, JO206 and JW100: this is often observed in HST images of type-2 AGN. MUSE data also showed that emission lines in JO201, JO204 and JW100 clearly present at least two components in the line profiles, one broader ( _v 200-500 km s ^ 1 ) and the other narrower ( _v < 200 km s ^ 1 ). In JO204 the emission shows a more extended structure: the broader [ O 3 ] component overlaps with the central emission, the narrower one is peaked at two opposite positions along the \ emission; both components were associated to the outflow.

sources.summary: The paper includes JO204 in the ALMA CO survey, finds long depletion times under the Accurso scaling, and notes HI absorption prevents its HI estimate.

quotes.quote: Observations of the CO(1-0) emission (rest frequency 115.271 GHz) of our four galaxies, namely JO201, JO204, JO206 and JW100, have been obtained with ALMA during Cycle 5 (project 2017.1.00496.S).

When using the Accurso2017 scaling relation, the timescales for JW100 and JO204 are still longer than normal galaxies, while the other two are more consistent with the literature values (JO201 and JO206).

Three of our galaxies gaspXVII,GASPXXVIII Deb+, in prep. also possess an estimate of the HI gas content (preliminary from MeerKat data for JW100), while for the fourth one (JO204) this has been hampered by HI absorption due to the presence of a continuum source at the center of the galaxy Deb+2020 .

gmw_00000424.name: JO206
gmw_00000424.overview: JO206 is a massive jellyfish galaxy in the cluster IIZw108 that hosts a Seyfert active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2009.13287, arXiv:2208.11955] and features a stellar bulge and bar [arXiv:2301.03090]. It is undergoing extreme ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:2009.13287], which has produced a 90-kiloparsec-long tail of ionized gas [arXiv:2009.13287, arXiv:2112.09712] containing numerous in-situ star-forming clumps [arXiv:1803.06183, arXiv:2112.09712].

sources.summary: The paper describes JO206 as the GASP galaxy with the longest ionized gas tails, many in-situ star-forming knots, significant central and tail CO detections, and tentative farther molecular gas.

quotes.quote: JO206 JO206 is the GASP galaxy showing the longest ionized gas tails gaspI where 139 knots of star formation has been revealed through their H emission. With the exception of the central knot showing line ratios typical of AGN emission, almost all the other knots are consistent with being ionized by young stars formed in situ. Their median ionized gas mass is 1.5 10^5 M_ , and their total mass is 1.7 10^8 M_ , not including the diffuse H emission and the regions where the gas density could not be estimated using the [ SII ] 6716/ [ SII ] 6732 ratio. Our detections of the CO line, shown in Fig. fig:JO206_1G , are significant ( S/N >3 ) for the central pointing and for the region located at 30 kpc from the center (pointing B).

A free gaussian fit in the C and D pointings was not able to recover the molecular line, and we had to impose a peak position (-55 kms for the C pointing, -320 kms for the D pointing) in order to get the fit. If real, in C and D again the molecular gas appears to be at a different velocity with respect to the H emission. In any case the C pointing is only marginally significant ( S/N=2.3 , T_ ON =93 min), and the farthest D pointing (at 67 kpc) is consistent with a non detection. We notice, however, that the observations of both these pointings are shorter than what we asked for and longer exposures would be needed to ascertain the amount of molecular gas beyond 40 kpc from the galaxy main body.

sources.summary: The paper presents JO206 as a massive AGN-hosting jellyfish galaxy undergoing extreme ram-pressure stripping, with an ordered magnetic field and steep-spectrum radio emission in its long star-forming tail.

quotes.quote: We present the first measurement of the magnetic field strength and orientation within the disc and the (90 kpc-long) H emitting tail of the jellyfish galaxy JO206. The tail has a large-scale magnetic field ( >4.1 G), a steep radio spectral index ( 2.0 ), indicating an aging of the electrons propagating away from the star-forming regions, and extremely high fractional polarisation ( >50 ), indicating low turbulent motions. The magnetic field vectors are aligned with (parallel to) the direction of the ionised-gas tail and stripping direction.

JO206 is a massive galaxy (stellar mass M_ 9 10^ 10 M _ ), hosts an active galactic nucleus (AGN), and undergoes extreme ram-pressure stripping in the low mass cluster IIZW108 (see also SI, Sect. 1). The 2.7 GHz total intensity map is presented in Figure fig:Results (top left). The integrated total flux density of JO206 is ( 3.60 0.18 ) mJy.

It reaches ( 3.5 0.2 ) mJy in the disc (defined by the blue contour in Fig. fig:Results corresponding to the stellar disc convolved to the 2.7 GHz resolution) and ( 0.10 0.01 ) mJy in the tail (defined by the outermost radio contour, which is 3 for total intensity, 4.5 for polarisation, excluding the disc region). The peak emission (green cross in Fig. fig:Results , top left) is co-located with the AGN (central source, see SI, Sect. 8 ) contributing to the disc total flux density. We find a projected tail-length of 40 kpc in total intensity, which morphology is in good agreement with the H emission, though the H extends even further (Fig. fig:Results , black contours).

sources.summary: The paper presents JO206 as a textbook ram-pressure-stripped GASP galaxy with a long ionized-gas tail, cluster infall, star-forming clumps, and multiphase gas along the tail.

quotes.quote: JO206 is a textbook case galaxy to study ram-pressure stripping due to its 90 kpc-long H emitting ionized gas tail Poggianti2017 . JO206 is part of the low-mass cluster IIZW108 ( =575 km/s) at a redshift of z=0.05133 , it has a total stellar mass of M_ = 7.8 10^ 10 M _ and a star-formation rate of 4.8 M _ /yr and 0.5 M _ /yr in its disk and tail, respectively. It is falling into the cluster with a velocity of at least 1200 km/s at a projected distance of 300 kpc from the cluster center. The multiple star-forming clumps in the galaxy disk and tail were identified based on their H emission.

These clumps are generally found to coincide also with a reservoir of molecular gas Moretti2018,Moretti2020 (left panel in Fig. JO206_multi ) and neutral hydrogen Ramatsoku2019 (right panel in Fig. JO206_multi ). The molecular gas follows the clumpy structure of the star-forming knots. The lower spatial resolution H i observations show the presence of H i along the whole H emitting tail. However, the total amount of neutral + molecular gas is found to be normal Moretti2020 .

sources.summary: The paper includes JO206 in the HST GASP jellyfish sample and reports that its tail star-forming-complex onset-age percentile reaches 383 Myr, similar to the other galaxies.

quotes.quote: The data Our work is based on images obtained using the UVIS channel of the WFC3 camera on board of HST for six jellyfish galaxies in the GASP sample: JO175, JO201, JO204, JO206, JW39 and JW100. These galaxies were observed in five filters: F275W, F336W, F606W, F680N and F814W, sampling the wavelength range from 2475 to 9560 , with F680N covering the H emission line in the redshift range of the targeted galaxies (0.042 - 0.066). The FWHM of the PSF of the observations in this redshift range corresponds to 70 pc.

In Fig. fig:onset , we show box plots tracing the distributions of t_0^ for each galaxy in our sample, with whiskers indicating the 1 and 99 percentiles. The 99 percentiles of t_0^ are very similar between galaxies, varying from 284 Myr for JO204 to 383 Myr for JO206. These values are comparable to that of , who constrain the ages of blobs in the tail of IC 3418 to be younger than 400 Myr. Taken at face value, the similarity of the maximum t_0^ in different galaxies points to a scenario where the stripping timescales do not strongly depend on galaxy properties and surrounding environment.

sources.summary: The paper compares JO206 with JO201, describing it as a massive jellyfish in IIZw108 with a long ionized and HI tail, HI deficiency, and similar stripping properties.

quotes.quote: JO206 is a massive galaxy with a stellar mass, M _ = 8.5 10 ^ 10 . It lies at a redshift of z = 0.0513, near the centre of the low-mass (M _ 200 2 10^ 14 ) galaxy cluster IIZw108 with a velocity dispersion of 575 . This galaxy is characterised by a long tail ( 90 kpc) of ionised gas stripped away by ram pressure. In Ramatsoku2019 we analysed the \ gas phase of JO206 and found a similarly long \ tail extending in the same direction as the H tail. Its total \ mass is 3.2 10^ 9 , of which 1.8 10^ 9 \ (about 60 ) is in the stripped tail (see Fig. 3 in Ramatsoku2019 ).
It is also similar to the \ gas mass loss of JO206 which is 0.3 dex below the average \ fraction resulting in 50 \ deficiency.

sources.summary: The paper maps JO206 as a jellyfish galaxy with low-metallicity stripped-gas tails; the tail metallicity decreases by 0.1-0.4 dex outward, and inferred ICM mixing reaches about 60%.

quotes.quote: The panels (a) of Figs. fig:JW100 , fig:JO206 , and fig:JW39 clearly show that the gas metallicities in the tails of all galaxies are systematically lower than those in the galaxy disks. These trends are better identified looking at the panels ( _ i ), where we present the distribution of the metallicities of the clumps in each selected sub-tail against their position along the tail. As a reference of the metallicity in the disk, for each galaxy we indicate (with a horizontal solid line) the value at the effective radius taken from franchetto2020 . In most cases, metallicities in the tails are much lower than this value. Furthermore, in all sub-tails we detect a decrease of the gas metallicity moving along the stripping direction; the decrease varies between 0.1 and 0.4 dex, with the lowest metallicities mainly located in the furthest regions.

For JW100 and JW39 we derive that 30 - 40 of the most metal-poor clumps could be constituted by gas from the ICM; for JO206 the mixing can reach values as high as 60 ; while, for JO201 the effect is evaluated up to the 80 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies JO206 as a jellyfish galaxy with large-scale non-axisymmetric metallicity structure likely caused by ram-pressure-driven gas stripping and gas exchange between its disk and external gas.

quotes.quote: They are NGC 6926 and JO206, and we show their (undeprojected) H maps and metallicity fluctuation maps in fig:large_lcorr . Both galaxies show large non-axisymmetric metallicity fluctuations, demonstrating that the pipeline two-point correlation function computation is detecting a real feature of the galaxy metallicity distributions. NGC 6926 is a spiral luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG) with a Seyfert nucleus, and is at a very early stage of interaction with the dwarf galaxy NGC 6929 that lies 4 arcmin to its east outside the MUSE FoV; Dopita15, Herrero-Illana17 . JO206 is a jellyfish galaxy experiencing gas stripping caused by ram-pressure during its infall into a cluster Ramatsoku19 .

Yet a third possibility, which is likely the explanation for JO206, is that the large-scale non-axisymmetric metallicity structure to which our correlation length is responding is a result of ongoing gas exchange between the disc and gas outside the disc. In the case of JO206, the disc might be blended with the metal-poor gas in circumgalactic medium (CGM), while in NGC 6926 there may be gas exchange between the more metal-rich disc gas and the metal-poor gas in the dwarf with which the disc is starting to merge. These interactions could easily produce real metallicity structures that are correlated on >10 kpc scales.

sources.summary: JO206 is used as a clump-density example; its tail is star-formation dominated in NII/SII diagnostics, has a strong OI-diagram SF component, and places 10-20% of its SFR in the tail.

quotes.quote: For the majority of galaxies, the NII DD and and SII DD generally show an excellent agreement, and they indicate star formation as the predominant ionization mechanism in the tails (in JO113, JO141, JO160, JO171, JO175, JO201, JO204, JO206, JO49, JO60, JO95, JW100, JW39 plus JO147 and JO194 from SII DD) or a SF+Composite origin (NII DD for JO194, and especially for JO147). 6 we show the density map of clumps in JO206, as an illustrative case of the general trends.

Considering instead only the pure SF component (no Composite) in the NII DD, this dominates ( 80 ) the emission in the tails of 9 of our galaxies (JO113, JO141, JO160, JO171, JO201, JO206, JO49, JO95, JW39). Four of our galaxies have a particularly strong SF component according to the OI DD ( 40-60 in JO206, JO49, JW39 and JO194). The SFR occurring in the tail represents 10 to 20 of the total in 6 of our galaxies (JO206, JW39, JO204, JO201, JW100 and JO171), which are those with the longest tails, as it is reasonable to expect.

sources.summary: JO206 lies close to its host cluster center and moves quickly; it is among seven systems in exceptional ram-pressure conditions, with a long stripped-gas tail and a dedicated GASP study.

quotes.quote: Let us consider the 7 massive galaxies with very high values. JW100 is the most massive galaxy in the GASP sample and it is hosted in a relatively low-mass cluster, but it is moving at very high speed and it is very close to the cluster centre, where the ICM is denser. JO85 and JO171 are hosted in two of the most massive clusters ( >1000 ), while JO201 is moving at extremely high speed in the ICM of a relatively massive cluster. Finally, JO204, JO206 and JW39 are very close to the centre of their host cluster centre and moving at quite high speed.

All these seven galaxies are in exceptional conditions regarding at least one the main parameters regulating the ram-pressure intensity. We note that, besides JO201, all other six galaxies have a tail of stripped gas with a long extension on the plane of the sky, indicating that the component on the plane of the sky of the velocity of the galaxies in the ICM is dominant with respect to the one along the line of sight; the measured radial velocity is therefore a lower limit of their actual speed. Five of these galaxies are studied in detail in dedicated papers ( JW100, pogg+jw100 ; JO171, gaspV ; JO201, gaspII,gaspXV ; JO204, gaspIV ; JO206, gaspI ).

sources.summary: The paper finds JO206 has a bulge, a bar, asymmetric molecular gas, and complex CO kinematics consistent with bar perturbations plus ram-pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: JO206 The I band images in Fig. fig:iband_images shows that JO206 hosts a stellar bulge. In addition, the elongated shape of the isophotes in the inner regions indicate the presence of a stellar bar aligned with the disk major axis, as for JO201. The CO total intensity map in Fig. fig:maps_co10 shows that the morphology of the molecular gas distribution is asymmetric, suggesting that the ram pressure is directed towards south-east.

sources.summary: The paper finds JO206 has some of the largest disk and extraplanar clumps, bright UV filamentary structures, balanced tail and extraplanar clump counts, and bright extraplanar clumps.

quotes.quote: The largest clumps are found in the disks of JO175, JO201 and JO206 and in the extraplanar region of JO206. As shown by Fig. JO201_clumps_zoom , large disk clumps (red contours) typically contain several sub-clumps (yellow contours), while extraplanar and especially tail clumps often have only one level. One can also appreciate the effects of RPS on extraplanar clumps, like the filamentary structures in JO206 and JW100, which are particularly bright in UV (lower right panels in Figs. 6.4 and 6.6).

For what concerns the number of extraplanar and tail clumps, the prevalence of one over the other depends on the galaxy: in JW100 the number of extraplanar clumps is much larger than that of tail clumps in both the selection filters, in JO204 and JO206 they are almost of the same number, while in the other galaxies tail clumps are more numerous than extraplanar. The number of clumps in each category clearly depends on both the disk inclination and the stripping direction with respect to the line of sight. Furthermore, with the only exception being disk clumps in JO175, for the same spatial category there are more UV-selected clumps than H selected ones. This indicates that there are a number of stellar-only clumps, with little or no ionized gas left. The number of star-forming complexes in the tails of the galaxies is generally smaller than the number of tail UV-selected clumps, but larger than that of tail H selected ones, with the only exception being JO206, suggesting that many complexes are matched only to UV-selected clumps, without any H counterpart.

JO201 stands out for its population of bright H selected clumps, both in the disk and in the tail, while in the extraplanar regions the brightest clumps are those of JO206 (located in the crest to the top right of the disk, see Fig. 6.4).

sources.summary: The paper describes JO206 as a jellyfish galaxy with constrained disk and tail magnetic fields, a Seyfert nucleus, and old curved-spectrum radio emission in its tail.

quotes.quote: Previous works constrained such magnetic fields around 10-20 G in the stellar disks and 4-6 G in the tails of two jellyfish galaxies JO206 and JW100, see Muller_2021,Ignesti_2021 .

Finally, the sample also presents a high incidence of AGN with 5 out of 6 galaxies showing nuclear activity according to the Baldwin, Phillips Terlevich diagnostic diagrams BPT, Baldwin1981 : JW39, JO85, JO49 Peluso_2021 host LINER-like nuclei, while JW100 and JO206 have a Seyfert nucleus Poggianti2017b, Radovich2019 .

Indeed, evidence of steep-spectrum emission at high frequencies have already been reported for JW100, which shows an integrated, nonthermal spectral index =-0.92 0.15 and 2.43 0.37 in the 1.4-3.2 and 3.2-5.5 GHz bands, respectively, in the disk, and <-0.85 between 1.4 and 3.2 GHz in the tail Ignesti_2021 , and for JO206, which shows 1 in the disk and 2 in the tail between 1.4 and 2.7 GHz Muller_2021 . Moreover, by comparing the detection levels of JO206's radio tail in the new LOFAR data and in the maps presented in Muller_2021 , we can now roughly constrain the spectral index between 144 MHz and 2.7 GHz to =-1.1 , which further indicates that the synchrotron spectrum is curved, i.e. that the CRe population is old.

sources.summary: The paper describes JO206 as an AGN-hosting GASP jellyfish galaxy with nuclear dust lanes, inner-disk HST structure, and a faint broad [O III] component overlapping the central peak.

quotes.quote: Results from the GASP survey showed that JO201, JO204, JO206 and JW100 galaxies host an AGN rado+2019 ; JW39 central region has a LINER-like spectrum pelu+2022 ; finally ionization in the central region of JO175 is dominated by SF activity gaspXIII,rado+2019. Multiple dust lanes are present in the nuclear regions, in particular in JO204, JO206 and JW100: this is often observed in HST images of type-2 AGN. Finally, [ O 3 ] shows a very faint broader component in the central MUSE spaxels of JO206, and concluded that it was not possible to detect a meaningful outflow component: we notice however that this component overlaps well with the \ peak. Galaxy disk and stripped gas in the inner regions The high potential of the diagnostic power of UVIS observations can be appreciated in Fig. fig:rgb_uv_814 in which we show a zoom on the galaxy disk of JO204, JO206, and JW100 of the RGB images from Fig. fig:rgb.

sources.summary: The paper includes JO206 in the ALMA jellyfish sample, assigns it a high metallicity-dependent conversion factor, and finds its depletion time matches literature values.

quotes.quote: Observations of the CO(1-0) emission (rest frequency 115.271 GHz) of our four galaxies, namely JO201, JO204, JO206 and JW100, have been obtained with ALMA during Cycle 5 (project 2017.1.00496.S).

To mimic this effect, we also used a second metallicity-dependent formulation of AMorin+2016 (which gives results compatible with the studies by Schruba+2012,Genzel+2012 for higher redshift galaxies) where this factor reaches values that go from 0.9 (in JW100) to 2.0 (in JO206).

Evaluating the global (HI + ) depletion time for the three galaxies where we have both measurements, it turns out that within the galaxy disk the estimated depletion time is in agreement with literature values Leroy2008 only for the JO206 galaxy, while JO201 and JW100 have unusually long depletion times, unless we assume extremely low values for the .
